ida01.title: Image Connectivity and Morphological Basics
ida01.summary: Connectivity defines which pixels are 'neighbours'. 4-connectivity uses horizontal/vertical neighbours; 8-connectivity adds diagonals. Connected component labelling groups pixels into discrete objects вҖ” the foundation for object counting and region analysis.
ida01.context: Lecture 01 В· 12.10.2023 вҖ” Structural image analysis

left.title: 4-Connectivity
points: Neighbours: up, down, left, right (4 pixels)

right.title: 8-Connectivity
points: More inclusive вҖ” merges diagonal touches

sections.heading: Connected Component Labelling
sections.text: Given a binary image (foreground = 1, background = 0), connected component labelling (CCL) assigns a unique integer label to each connected group of foreground pixels. The classic two-pass algorithm: (1) scan left-to-right, top-to-bottom assigning provisional labels and recording equivalences when two neighbouring labels refer to the same component; (2) resolve equivalences with union-find and relabel. The result maps each foreground pixel to its component ID. Region properties (area, centroid, bounding box, eccentricity) are then computed per label вҖ” this is how cell counting in microscopy is done: threshold вҶ’ CCL вҶ’ filter by area.

sections.heading: Region Properties Computed from Labels
items: Area: number of pixels in the component
Centroid: mean $(x,y)$ position weighted by pixel count
Bounding box: min/max coordinates
Eccentricity: elongation of the best-fit ellipse (0 = circle, 1 = line)
Solidity: area / convex hull area (measures concavity)
Perimeter: number of boundary pixels (4- or 8-connected boundary)

ida02.title: Lecture 2 вҖ” Q & A
ida02.summary: IDA Lecture 2'nin tГјm sДұnav sorularДұ ve sade aГ§Дұklamalarla cevaplarДұ. Dijital gГ¶rГјntГј temsili, yoДҹunluk dГ¶nГјЕҹГјmleri, histogram, konvolГјsyon, separable filterlar, frekans alanДұ, median, morfolojik operasyonlar.
ida02.context: Lecture 02 В· 26.10.2023 вҖ” Basic Image Processing Toolbox

sections.heading: S1. Dijital gГ¶rГјntГјler nasДұl temsil edilir? 8-bit ile 16-bit farkДұ? Renk nasДұl kodlanДұr? 3B gГ¶rГјntГјler? Metadata ne iЕҹe yarar?
sections.text:
Dijital gГ¶rГјntГј, dГјzenli bir piksel ДұzgarasДұndan oluЕҹur. Her piksel belirli bir konumdaki ДұЕҹДұk yoДҹunluДҹunu sayДұsal olarak depolar. 2B gГ¶rГјntГј iГ§in bu Дұzgara MГ—N boyutunda bir matristir.

8-bit gГ¶rГјntГј 0-255 arasДұ 256 farklДұ deДҹer saklayabilir. 16-bit ise 0-65535 arasДұ 65536 deДҹer saklayabilir. Neden Г¶nemli? TДұbbi gГ¶rГјntГјler ve mikroskopi verileri genellikle Г§ok ince yoДҹunluk farklДұlДұklarДұ iГ§erir; 8-bit bunlarДұ temsil etmekte yetersiz kalДұr ve bilgi kaybДұ oluЕҹur. 16-bit bu dinamik aralДұk sorununu Г§Г¶zer.

Renk kodlamasДұ en yaygДұn olarak RGB ile yapДұlДұr: her piksel iГ§in kДұrmДұzДұ, yeЕҹil ve mavi kanalДұ ayrДұ ayrДұ saklanДұr. Bunun yanДұnda HSV (ton, doygunluk, parlaklДұk) veya CMYK gibi alternatif renk uzaylarДұ da vardДұr. Mikroskopide Г§ok kanallДұ gГ¶rГјntГјler de yaygДұndДұr: farklДұ floresan boyalar iГ§in ayrДұ kanallar.

3B gГ¶rГјntГј, 2B ДұzgaranДұn z ekseninde yДұДҹДұlmasДұyla MГ—NГ—Z boyutunda bir voksel hacmidir. Her voksel (volume pixel) 3B uzaydaki kГјГ§Гјk bir kГјpГј temsil eder.

Metadata gГ¶rГјntГјnГјn nasДұl alДұndДұДҹДұna dair ek bilgileri saklar: piksel boyutu (Ојm cinsinden voksel bГјyГјklГјДҹГј), zaman damgasДұ, kullanДұlan mikroskop parametreleri vs. Г–lГ§Гјm yapmak istiyorsan gerГ§ek fiziksel boyutu bilebilmek iГ§in metadata kritik Г¶neme sahiptir.

sections.heading: S2. YoДҹunluk dГ¶nГјЕҹГјmleri hangi tГјrlerde olur? Histogram nedir ve ne iЕҹe yarar? Gamma dГ¶nГјЕҹГјmГјnГјn amacДұ nedir?
sections.text:
YoДҹunluk dГ¶nГјЕҹГјmleri (intensity transformations), piksel deДҹerlerini noktasal olarak dГ¶nГјЕҹtГјrГјr вҖ” yani her pikselin yeni deДҹeri sadece kendi eski deДҹerine baДҹlДұdДұr, komЕҹularДұ Г¶nemli deДҹildir.

Lineer dГ¶nГјЕҹГјmler: y = aВ·x + b. 'a' kontrast kontrolГј, 'b' parlaklДұk kontrolГј saДҹlar. Histogram stretching de buraya girer: yoДҹunluk aralДұДҹДұnДұ [min, max] вҶ’ [0, 255]'e gererek kontrastДұ artДұrДұrsДұn.

Histogram eЕҹitleme (equalization): yoДҹunluklarДұ eЕҹit daДҹДұlacak Еҹekilde yeniden eЕҹler. ZayДұf kontrastlДұ gГ¶rГјntГјlerde Г§arpДұcДұ iyileЕҹtirmeler saДҹlar.

Gamma dГ¶nГјЕҹГјmГј: y = x^Оі. Bu neden gerekli? Д°nsan gГ¶zГј karanlДұk tonlara aydДұnlДұk tonlardan Г§ok daha duyarlДұdДұr. Оі<1 koyu tonlarДұ aГ§ar (gГ¶lgelerde detay ortaya Г§Дұkar), Оі>1 aydДұnlДұk tonlarДұ karartДұr. Ekranlar da genellikle gamma dГјzeltmesi gerektirir.

YoДҹunluk histogramДұ, gГ¶rГјntГјdeki her yoДҹunluk deДҹerinin kaГ§ piksel tarafДұndan kullanДұldДұДҹДұnДұ gГ¶steren daДҹДұlДұm grafiДҹidir. Dar histogramdan dГјЕҹГјk kontrast, Г§ok sola kaymДұЕҹ histogramdan gГ¶rГјntГјnГјn karanlДұk olduДҹu, bimodal histogramdan arka plan ve nesnenin net ayrДұldДұДҹДұ anlaЕҹДұlabilir. EЕҹikleme iГ§in de kullanДұlДұr.

sections.heading: S3. Linear spatial filtering ve 2B/3B konvolГјsyon nasДұl Г§alДұЕҹДұr? Anizotropik 3B gГ¶rГјntГјde izotropik kernel ne sorunu yaratДұr?
sections.text:
Linear spatial filtering, bir kernel (filtre maskesi) gГ¶rГјntГјnГјn Гјzerinde kaydДұrДұlarak her konumda komЕҹu piksellerin aДҹДұrlДұklДұ toplamДұnДұn hesaplanmasДұdДұr. Bu iЕҹleme konvolГјsyon denir.

NasДұl Г§alДұЕҹДұr: 3Г—3'lГјk bir Gaussian blur kernel ele al. Her piksel Гјzerine kernel'i hizala, kernel aДҹДұrlДұklarДұnДұ karЕҹДұlДұk gelen piksel deДҹerleriyle Г§arp, topla. Bu toplanmДұЕҹ deДҹer o konumun yeni piksel deДҹeri olur. Kernel boyunca kaydДұrarak tГјm gГ¶rГјntГјyГј tara.

2B'den 3B'ye geГ§ince kernel de 3B olur: MxNxZ boyutunda bir voksel hacmini iЕҹlemek iГ§in MxNxZ boyutunda bir kernel kullanДұrsДұn.

Anizotropik 3B gГ¶rГјntГј problemi Еҹudur: tДұbbi gГ¶rГјntГјlerde voksel boyutu her yГ¶nde eЕҹit deДҹildir. Г–rneДҹin x-y dГјzleminde 0.2 mm Г§Г¶zГјnГјrlГјk varken z yГ¶nГјnde 1 mm kesit kalДұnlДұДҹДұ olabilir. EДҹer izotropik bir Gaussian kernel uygularsan (her yГ¶nde aynДұ sigma) z yГ¶nГјnde Г§ok daha geniЕҹ bir alandan bilgi toplamДұЕҹ olursun, sanki gГ¶rГјntГјyГј z ekseninde aЕҹДұrДұ bulanДұklaЕҹtДұrДұyorsun. YapДұlar deforme gГ¶rГјnГјr. DoДҹru yaklaЕҹДұm voksel boyutuna gГ¶re Г¶lГ§eklendirilmiЕҹ anizotropik kernel kullanmaktДұr.

sections.heading: S4. Separable filter kernel nedir? NasДұl Г§alДұЕҹДұr? Ne faydasДұ var?
sections.text:
Separable filter, 2B (veya 3B) bir konvolГјsyonu birden fazla ardДұЕҹДұk 1B konvolГјsyona ayrДұЕҹtДұrabilen bir kernel tipidir. Gaussian filtresi bunun klasik Г¶rneДҹidir.

NasДұl Г§alДұЕҹДұr: 2B Gaussian filtresi uygulamak yerine Г¶nce yatay yГ¶nde 1B Gaussian, ardДұndan dikey yГ¶nde 1B Gaussian uygularsДұn. Matematiksel olarak bu iki adДұm, doДҹrudan 2B Gaussian uygulamakla tamamen eЕҹdeДҹerdir.

3B iГ§in ГјГ§ ardДұЕҹДұk 1B konvolГјsyon: Г¶nce x yГ¶nГјnde, sonra y yГ¶nГјnde, sonra z yГ¶nГјnde.

FaydasДұ hesaplama maliyetinde muazzam bir azalmadДұr. Bir NxN kernel'li 2B konvolГјsyonun maliyeti O(NВІ) iЕҹlem per piksel. Separable yaklaЕҹДұmda bu O(2N) per piksel olur. N bГјyГјdГјkГ§e bu fark dramatik biГ§imde aГ§ДұlДұr. 3B'de O(NВі) yerine O(3N). BГјyГјk tДұbbi gГ¶rГјntГјlerde veya 3B veri setlerinde bu fark Г§alДұЕҹma sГјresini saatlerden dakikalara indirebilir.

sections.heading: S5. Frekans alanДұnda filtreleme nasДұl Г§alДұЕҹДұr? AvantajlarДұ neler? 3B gГ¶rГјntГјye nasДұl geniЕҹler?
sections.text:
Fourier dГ¶nГјЕҹГјmГј bir gГ¶rГјntГјyГј uzaysal alandan frekans alanДұna taЕҹДұr. GГ¶rГјntГјdeki her piksel deДҹerinin hangi frekans bileЕҹenlerinden oluЕҹtuДҹunu gГ¶sterir. DГјЕҹГјk frekanslar genel parlaklДұk ve bГјyГјk yapДұlara karЕҹДұlДұk gelirken, yГјksek frekanslar keskin kenarlara ve gГјrГјltГјye karЕҹДұlДұk gelir.

Frekans alanДұnda filtreleme Еҹu Еҹekilde Г§alДұЕҹДұr: FFT uygula вҶ’ frekans alanДұnda filtre mask'Дұ Г§arp (Г¶rneДҹin dГјЕҹГјk frekanslarДұ geГ§, yГјksekleri kapat) вҶ’ ters FFT ile uzaysal alana dГ¶n. Matematiksel olarak bu, uzaysal alanda konvolГјsyonla tamamen eЕҹdeДҹerdir вҖ” bu konvolГјsyon teoreminin ana sonucudur.

AvantajlarДұ: Г§ok bГјyГјk kernel'ler iГ§in uzaysal konvolГјsyon O(NВІВ·KВІ) maliyetlidir, ama frekans alanДұnda her zaman O(NВІ log N) maliyetle yapДұlabilir, yani bГјyГјk kernel'lerde frekans alanДұ Г§ok daha hДұzlДұdДұr. AyrДұca tam olarak hangi frekanslarДұ geГ§irmek istediДҹini net biГ§imde tanДұmlayabilirsin (ideal low-pass, band-pass gibi).

3B gГ¶rГјntГјye geniЕҹleme doДҹrudur: 3B FFT uygularsДұn, 3B frekans alanДұnda filtrelersin, 3B ters FFT ile geri dГ¶nersin. Hesaplama artДұЕҹДұ O(NВі log N) olur ama prensip aynДұdДұr.

sections.heading: S6. Non-linear rank order filterlar nedir? Median, min, max filtreleri nasДұl Г§alДұЕҹДұr? FaydalarДұ neler?
sections.text:
Non-linear filterlar, piksel deДҹerlerinin aДҹДұrlДұklДұ toplamДұ yerine baЕҹka bir fonksiyon uygular вҖ” dolayДұsДұyla sГјperpozisyon prensibi geГ§erli deДҹildir.

Rank order filter fikri Еҹudur: komЕҹuluk penceresindeki tГјm piksel deДҹerlerini sДұralarsДұn (rank'larsДұn) ve bu sДұralДұ diziden belirli bir elemanДұ seГ§ersin.

Median filter: sДұralДұ dizinin ortanca deДҹerini al. Bu neden kullanДұЕҹlДұ? "Salt and pepper" gГјrГјltГјsГјne (rastgele beyaz/siyah pikseller) karЕҹДұ son derece etkilidir. Gaussian blur da gГјrГјltГјyГј giderir ama kenarlarДұ bulanДұklaЕҹtДұrДұr; median ise kenarlarДұ korurken gГјrГјltГјyГј siler, Г§ГјnkГј aЕҹДұrДұ deДҹerler (0 veya 255) sДұralДұ dizide baЕҹa veya sona dГјЕҹer ve ortanca seГ§ilmez.

Min filter (erosion): penceredeki en kГјГ§Гјk deДҹeri seГ§. Binary gГ¶rГјntГјlerde parlak nesneleri kГјГ§ГјltГјr, karanlДұk bГ¶lgeleri geniЕҹletir.

Max filter (dilation): penceredeki en bГјyГјk deДҹeri seГ§. Binary gГ¶rГјntГјlerde parlak nesneleri bГјyГјtГјr, kГјГ§Гјk delikleri kapatДұr.

Bu filterlar morfolojik operasyonlarДұn temelini oluЕҹturur.

sections.heading: S7. Morfolojik gГ¶rГјntГј iЕҹlemenin genel fikri nedir? Structuring element nedir ve nasДұl kullanДұlДұr?
sections.text:
Morfolojik gГ¶rГјntГј iЕҹleme, gГ¶rГјntГјdeki Еҹekilleri yapДұsal bir elemanla (structuring element) Еҹekillendirmeye, bГјyГјtmeye, kГјГ§Гјltmeye ya da analiz etmeye yarar. Genellikle binary gГ¶rГјntГјler Гјzerinde Г§alДұЕҹДұr (beyaz nesne, siyah arka plan).

Structuring element (SE), belirli bir Еҹekil ve boyutta kГјГ§Гјk bir maskedir: kare, disk, Г§izgi gibi. Bu elemanДұn nasДұl seГ§ildiДҹi operasyonun hangi geometrik Г¶zelliklere duyarlДұ olacaДҹДұnДұ belirler.

Morfolojik operasyonlar SE'yi gГ¶rГјntГј Гјzerinde kaydДұrДұr ve her konumda SE ile Г¶rtГјЕҹen bГ¶lgeye gГ¶re karar verir. Г–rneДҹin dilation iЕҹleminde: SE merkezi her piksel Гјzerine yerleЕҹtirilir, SE ile Г¶rtГјЕҹen bГ¶lgede en az bir beyaz piksel varsa o merkez piksel beyaz yapДұlДұr. Bu nesneleri bГјyГјtГјr ve kГјГ§Гјk boЕҹluklarДұ doldurur. Erosion ise tersi вҖ” SE bГ¶lgesinin tamamДұnДұn beyaz olmasДұ gerekir, bu nesneleri kГјГ§ГјltГјr ve ince kГ¶prГјleri koparДұr.

sections.heading: S8. Binary gГ¶rГјntГјde erosion/dilation/opening/closing nasДұl hesaplanДұr? Etkileri neler?
sections.text:
DГ¶rt temel morfolojik operasyon:

Erosion (aЕҹДұndДұrma): SE'nin merkezi bir piksele gelince SE'nin tГјm alanДұ nesnenin iГ§inde kalДұyorsa o piksel beyaz kalДұr, deДҹilse siyah olur. Etki: nesneler kГјГ§ГјlГјr, ince baДҹlantДұlar kopar, kenarlardaki pikseller silinir. KГјГ§Гјk gГјrГјltГј nesnelerini yok edebilir.

Dilation (geniЕҹleme): SE merkezi gelince SE'nin herhangi bir bГ¶lgesi nesneyle Г¶rtГјЕҹГјyorsa o piksel beyaz olur. Etki: nesneler bГјyГјr, kГјГ§Гјk delikler ve Г§atlaklar kapanДұr, bГ¶lГјnmГјЕҹ parГ§alar birleЕҹebilir.

Opening (erosion вҶ’ dilation): Г¶nce aЕҹДұndДұr, sonra geniЕҹlet. Etki: kГјГ§Гјk gГјrГјltГј nesnelerini ve ince Г§ДұkДұntДұlarДұ siler, ama bГјyГјk nesnelerin genel Еҹeklini korur. Д°nce baДҹlantДұlarДұ koparДұr.

Closing (dilation вҶ’ erosion): Г¶nce geniЕҹlet, sonra aЕҹДұndДұr. Etki: kГјГ§Гјk delikleri ve boЕҹluklarДұ doldurur, yakДұn nesneleri birleЕҹtirir, ama genel Еҹekli korur.

Kolayca hatДұrlamak iГ§in: opening = dДұЕҹarДұdaki kГјГ§Гјkleri kaldДұr, closing = iГ§erideki kГјГ§Гјkleri doldur.

sections.heading: S9. Binary yapДұlardaki kГјГ§Гјk delikleri morfoloji ile nasДұl doldurursun? Connected component nasДұl Г§ДұkarДұlДұr? Dual operatГ¶rler neler?
sections.text:
KГјГ§Гјk delikleri doldurmak iГ§in closing operasyonu kullanДұlДұr: Г¶nce dilation ile delikler kapanДұr, ardДұndan erosion ile dДұЕҹarДұdan bГјyГјyen kДұsДұm geri alДұnДұr. Delik kГјГ§Гјkse structuring element'in bГјyГјklГјДҹГјne gГ¶re seГ§im yapДұlДұr.

Alternatif olarak flood fill (sel doldurma) algoritmasДұ kullanДұlabilir: nesnenin dДұЕҹДұndan baЕҹlayДұp arka planДұ boyarsДұn, boyayamadДұДҹДұn arka plan bГ¶lgeleri iГ§ deliklerdir.

Connected component labeling (baДҹlДұ bileЕҹen etiketleme): binary gГ¶rГјntГјdeki birbirine baДҹlДұ beyaz piksel gruplarДұnДұ bulur ve her birini farklДұ bir etiketle iЕҹaretler. 4-baДҹlantДұlДұlДұk (sadece yatay/dikey komЕҹular) veya 8-baДҹlantДұlДұlДұk (kГ¶Еҹegen komЕҹular da sayДұlДұr) kullanДұlabilir. BГ¶ylece gГ¶rГјntГјdeki her ayrДұ nesneyi sayabilir ve Гјzerinde ayrДұ ayrДұ Г¶lГ§Гјm yapabilirsin.

Dual operatГ¶rler, morfolojide birbirinin tamamlayДұcДұsДұ olan Г§ift operatГ¶rlerdir:
  Erosion вҶ” Dilation (biri diДҹerinin duali)
  Opening вҶ” Closing (biri diДҹerinin duali)

Dualite Еҹu anlama gelir: beyaz ve siyahДұ yer deДҹiЕҹtirip (gГ¶rГјntГјyГј tersine Г§evirip) bir operasyon uygularsan, orijinal gГ¶rГјntГјde diДҹer operasyonu uygulamakla aynДұ sonucu alДұrsДұn. Matematiksel olarak: erosion(complement(A)) = complement(dilation(A)).

ida02.title: Convolution and Linear Filtering
ida02.summary: Convolution is the fundamental operation in image processing: a kernel (filter) slides over the image, computing a weighted sum at each position. It is the basis for blurring, sharpening, edge detection, and is the mathematical heart of CNNs.

sections.heading: Discrete 2D Convolution
sections.text:
$(I * k)(x,y) = \sum_{u=-a}^{a} \sum_{v=-b}^{b} k(u,v) \cdot I(x-u, y-v)$

where $k$ is the kernel of size $(2a+1)\times(2b+1)$. Note the flip вҖ” convolution flips the kernel before sliding. Cross-correlation (used in CNNs) does NOT flip:

$(I \star k)(x,y) = \sum_{u,v} k(u,v) \cdot I(x+u, y+v)$

For symmetric kernels (Gaussian, Laplacian), convolution and cross-correlation are identical.

sections.heading: Separability and Efficiency
sections.text: A 2D Gaussian kernel $G(x,y) = G_x(x) \cdot G_y(y)$ is separable: it can be written as the outer product of two 1D Gaussians. This means a 2D convolution with an $n\times n$ kernel (cost $O(n^2)$ per pixel) can be replaced by two sequential 1D convolutions (cost $O(2n)$ per pixel) вҖ” a factor of $n/2$ speedup. For $n=15$: $225$ vs $30$ multiplications per pixel. Separability is why Gaussian blur is so fast in practice. All Gaussian-based operations (DoG, LoG, scale-space) exploit this property.

sections.heading: Convolution in CNNs vs Classical Processing
sections.text: In classical image processing, kernels are hand-designed (Gaussian for blur, Sobel for edges). In CNNs, the kernel weights are learned end-to-end from data via backpropagation. A conv layer with 64 output channels learns 64 different kernels simultaneously вҖ” each detecting a different low-level feature (edge direction, color gradient, texture). The key insight is that the same kernel applies everywhere in the image (weight sharing), exploiting the spatial stationarity of natural images.

ida02.title: Morphological Operations
ida02.summary: Morphological operations (erosion, dilation, opening, closing) manipulate binary or grayscale images using a structuring element. They are essential for noise removal, object separation, hole filling, and shape analysis in segmentation pipelines.
ida02.context: Lecture 02 В· 19.10.2023 вҖ” Shape-based image processing

sections.heading: Erosion and Dilation (Binary)
sections.text:
Structuring element $B$ (e.g. a disk or cross).

**Erosion** вҖ” keeps pixel only if ALL of $B$ fits within foreground:
$$(A \ominus B)(x,y) = 1 \iff B_{(x,y)} \subseteq A$$

**Dilation** вҖ” sets pixel to 1 if ANY of $B$ touches foreground:
$$(A \oplus B)(x,y) = 1 \iff B_{(x,y)} \cap A \neq \emptyset$$

**Opening** (erosion then dilation): $A \circ B = (A \ominus B) \oplus B$ вҖ” removes small protrusions.

**Closing** (dilation then erosion): $A \bullet B = (A \oplus B) \ominus B$ вҖ” fills small holes.

sections.heading: Intuition for Opening and Closing
sections.text: Opening removes structures smaller than the structuring element вҖ” if $B$ is a disk of radius 3, any foreground region narrower than 3 pixels gets eroded away and doesn't survive the subsequent dilation. This is used to remove thin filaments or isolated noise pixels while preserving larger structures. Closing fills holes and gaps smaller than $B$ вҖ” it's the dual operation. In practice: use opening to clean up binary segmentation masks (remove debris), use closing to fill gaps in cell boundaries. Both operations are idempotent: applying them twice gives the same result as once.

sections.heading: Erosion vs Dilation: Effect on Objects

left.title: Erosion
points: Removes thin protrusions and small noise blobs

ida02.title: Fourier Transform and Frequency Domain Analysis
ida02.summary: The Fourier Transform decomposes an image into sinusoidal components at different frequencies. Low frequencies encode global structure (background, large objects); high frequencies encode fine detail and edges. Frequency-domain filtering offers efficient implementation of large kernels.
ida02.context: Lecture 02 В· 19.10.2023 вҖ” Frequency domain methods

sections.heading: 2D Discrete Fourier Transform
sections.text:
Forward DFT:
$$F(u,v) = \sum_{x=0}^{M-1}\sum_{y=0}^{N-1} I(x,y)\, e^{-i2\pi(ux/M + vy/N)}$$

Inverse DFT:
$$I(x,y) = \frac{1}{MN}\sum_{u=0}^{M-1}\sum_{v=0}^{N-1} F(u,v)\, e^{i2\pi(ux/M + vy/N)}$$

**Convolution theorem**: $\mathcal{F}\{I * k\} = \mathcal{F}\{I\} \cdot \mathcal{F}\{k\}$

Convolution in spatial domain вҶ” pointwise multiplication in frequency domain.

sections.heading: Why the Convolution Theorem Matters
sections.text: Filtering a 1000Г—1000 image with a 50Г—50 kernel via direct convolution requires $1000^2 \times 50^2 = 2.5 \times 10^9$ multiplications. Using the FFT: transform both to frequency domain ($O(N^2 \log N)$ operations), pointwise multiply ($O(N^2)$), inverse FFT ($O(N^2 \log N)$) вҖ” dramatically faster for large kernels. The FFT is therefore the standard method for Gaussian blur at very large scales (sigma > 20 pixels), matched filtering, template matching, and computing image power spectra.

sections.heading: Low-Pass vs High-Pass Filtering
sections.text: In the Fourier domain, frequency $(u,v)=(0,0)$ is the DC component (average brightness). As $(u,v)$ moves away from the origin, frequency increases. Low-pass filtering: zero out high-frequency components (equivalent to Gaussian blur вҖ” smooth the image). High-pass filtering: subtract the low-pass version from the original (reveals edges and detail). Band-pass filtering: keep only a ring of frequencies (useful for texture analysis at a specific scale). The Gabor filter вҖ” a sinusoid windowed by a Gaussian вҖ” is a band-pass filter tuned to both a specific frequency and orientation, and has been linked to simple cells in the primary visual cortex.

ida03.title: Lecture 3 вҖ” Q & A
ida03.summary: IDA Lecture 3'Гјn tГјm sДұnav sorularДұ ve sade aГ§Дұklamalarla cevaplarДұ. MLP, gradient descent, CNN katmanlarДұ, backprop.
ida03.context: Lecture 03 В· 2.11.2023 вҖ” Deep Learning Crash Course

sections.heading: S1. MLP'nin bileЕҹenleri neler? AДҹДұrlДұklar verilince Г§ДұktДұyДұ nasДұl hesaplarsДұn?
sections.text:
MLP'de ГјГ§ Еҹey var: input layer (veriyi alДұr), hidden layer'lar (asДұl iЕҹlem burada),
output layer (sonuГ§ Г§Дұkar). Her nГ¶ron Г¶nceki katmanДұn tГјm nГ¶ronlarДұna baДҹlДұ
вҖ” bu yГјzden 'fully connected' denir. Her baДҹlantДұnДұn bir aДҹДұrlДұДҹДұ,
her nГ¶ronun bir bias'Дұ var.

ГҮДұktДұyДұ hesaplamak iГ§in katman katman ilerlersin:
Her katmanda W Г— girdi + bias hesaplarsДұn,
sonra ReLU ya da sigmoid gibi bir aktivasyon uygularsДұn.
Bunu son katmana kadar tekrarlarsДұn.
Son katmanda softmax varsa her sДұnДұf iГ§in bir olasДұlДұk Гјretirsin.

sections.heading: S2. MLP nasДұl eДҹitilir? Hangi bileЕҹenler deДҹiЕҹir? Hata nasДұl hesaplanДұr?
sections.text:
AДҹДұrlДұklar baЕҹta rastgele. Bir Г¶rnek verirsin, aДҹ tahmin Гјretir.
Bu tahmini doДҹru cevapla karЕҹДұlaЕҹtДұrДұrsДұn вҖ” bu fark loss.
SДұnДұflandДұrma iГ§in cross-entropy, sayДұ tahmini iГ§in MSE.

Sonra backprop devreye girer:
'Bu hataya hangi aДҹДұrlДұk ne kadar katkДұda bulundu?'
sorusunu zincir kuralДұyla geriye doДҹru hesaplarsДұn.
Her aДҹДұrlДұk iГ§in bir gradyan Г§Дұkar.
Gradient descent ile aДҹДұrlДұklarДұ gГјncellersin.

Sadece aДҹДұrlДұklar ve bias'lar deДҹiЕҹir.
KaГ§ katman, kaГ§ nГ¶ron var вҖ” bunlar sabit kalДұr.

sections.heading: S3. Gradient descent nedir? Learning rate neden kritik?
sections.text:
Loss fonksiyonunu engebeli bir arazi gibi dГјЕҹГјn, minimum noktayДұ bulmak istiyorsun.
Gradient descent: bulunduДҹun noktada zeminin hangi yГ¶ne eДҹimli olduДҹunu hesapla,
oradan ters yГ¶ne bir adДұm at. Bunu defalarca tekrarlayДұnca minimuma yaklaЕҹДұrsДұn.

Learning rate = her adДұmДұn bГјyГјklГјДҹГј.
  ГҮok bГјyГјk вҶ’ minimum'un Гјzerinden atlarsДұn, aДҹ stabil olmuyor.
  ГҮok kГјГ§Гјk вҶ’ Г§ok yavaЕҹ ilerlersin, yГјzlerce epoch gerekebilir.

Genellikle 0.001 ile baЕҹlanДұr.
Adam gibi adaptif optimizerlar bunu otomatik ayarlar.

sections.heading: S4. CNN ile klasik makine Г¶Дҹrenmesi arasДұndaki temel fark nedir?
sections.text:
Klasik ML'de iЕҹi ikiye bГ¶lersin:
  1. Elle Г¶zellik Г§Дұkar (kenar dedektГ¶rГј, renk histogramДұ, SIFT...)
  2. Bu Г¶zellikleri bir classifier'a ver
Г–zellikleri sen tasarlarsДұn.

CNN'de ise aДҹ Г¶zellikleri de kendisi Г¶Дҹreniyor.
Ham piksellerden direkt Г§ДұktДұya gidiyor,
aradaki her Еҹeyi backprop ile kendisi keЕҹfediyor.
Elle feature engineering yok.

Bu bГјyГјk avantaj: insan aklДұnДұn tasarlayamayacaДҹДұ Г¶zellikleri bulabiliyor
ve veriyle Г¶ДҹrendiДҹi iГ§in Г§ok daha iyi genelleme yapДұyor.

sections.heading: S5. Convolutional layer nasДұl Г§alДұЕҹДұyor? Filtreler nedir? Shared weights'te backprop?
sections.text:
KГјГ§Гјk bir filtre (Г¶rn. 3Г—3) tГјm gГ¶rГјntГјnГјn Гјzerinde kayar.
Her pozisyonda filtreyi o bГ¶lgeyle Г§arpДұp toplayarak tek bir sayДұ Гјretirsin.
Filtre = Г¶Дҹrenilen aДҹДұrlДұklar.
AynДұ filtre gГ¶rГјntГјnГјn her yerine aynДұ aДҹДұrlДұklarla uygulanДұr вҶ’ weight sharing.

Backprop'ta:
O filtre gГ¶rГјntГјnГјn 100 pozisyonunda kullanДұldДұysa,
filtrenin gradyanДұ bu 100 pozisyondan gelen gradyanlarДұn toplamДұdДұr.
Hepsini birleЕҹtirip filtreyi bir kez gГјncelliyorsun.

Weight sharing sayesinde parametre sayДұsДұ devasa azalДұr.

sections.heading: S6. Conv layer'Дұn hangi hyperparameter'larДұ var? Parametre sayДұsДұ nasДұl hesaplanДұr?
sections.text:
Hyperparameter'lar:
  kernel size  вҶ’ kГ—k  (kaГ§ piksellik filtre)
  out_channels вҶ’ kaГ§ farklДұ filtre (Г¶zellik) aranДұyor
  stride       вҶ’ filtre kaГ§ar piksel atlayarak kayacak
  padding      вҶ’ gГ¶rГјntГј kenarlarДұna kaГ§ piksel sДұfДұr ekleneceДҹi

Parametre sayДұsДұ = k Г— k Г— C_in Г— C_out + C_out  (son terim bias)

Г–rnek: 3Г—3 kernel, 3 input channel, 32 filtre
вҶ’ 3Г—3Г—3Г—32 + 32 = 896 parametre

Stride ve padding Г§ДұktДұ boyutunu etkiler, parametre sayДұsДұnДұ deДҹiЕҹtirmez.

sections.heading: S7. Pooling layer nedir? Neden kullanДұlДұyor? Hangi hyperparameter'larДұ var?
sections.text:
Spatial boyutu kГјГ§ГјltГјr. Max pooling en yaygДұnДұ:
  gГ¶rГјntГјyГј 2Г—2'lik bloklara bГ¶l,
  her bloktan sadece en bГјyГјk deДҹeri al.
  28Г—28'lik feature map вҶ’ stride=2 ile вҶ’ 14Г—14 olur.

Neden kullanДұlДұr?
  1. Parametre azaltma вҖ” sonraki katman daha kГјГ§Гјk girdi iЕҹler.
  2. Translation invariance вҖ” bir Г¶zellik 1-2 piksel kaydДұysa
     max pooling sonrasДұ aynДұ deДҹeri vermeye devam edebilir.

Hyperparams: pool size (kaГ§Г—kaГ§ blok) ve stride.

sections.heading: S8. FC layer CNN'de nerede kullanДұlДұyor? Multi-class output iГ§in hangi aktivasyon?
sections.text:
Conv+pool katmanlarДұ bittikten sonra, en sonda.
O noktada aДҹ gГ¶rГјntГјden yГјksek seviyeli Г¶zellikler Г§ДұkarmДұЕҹtДұr.
ArtДұk bunlarДұ birleЕҹtirip 'bu hangi sДұnДұf?' sorusunu cevaplama zamanДұ.

3D feature map'i dГјzleЕҹtirip (flatten) 1D vektГ¶re dГ¶nГјЕҹtГјrГјrsГјn,
sonra FC layer'lar gelir.

Multi-class Г§ДұktДұ iГ§in softmax:
  Her sДұnДұfa bir olasДұlДұk verir, hepsi toplamda 1 olur.
  '%80 kedi, %15 kГ¶pek, %5 kuЕҹ' gibi.
Binary sДұnДұflandДұrma iГ§in sigmoid da kullanДұlabilir.

sections.heading: S9. Tipik bir CNN nasДұl yapДұlandДұrДұlДұr? Katmanlar hangi sДұrayla geliyor?
sections.text:
[Conv вҶ’ ReLU вҶ’ Pool]  вҶҗ bu bloДҹu birkaГ§ kez tekrarla
        вҶ“
    Flatten          вҶҗ 3D feature map'i 1D vektГ¶re dГ¶nГјЕҹtГјr
        вҶ“
   FC layer(lar)     вҶҗ Г¶zellikleri birleЕҹtir
        вҶ“
     Softmax         вҶҗ sДұnДұf olasДұlДұklarДұnДұ Гјret

Her [Conv вҶ’ ReLU вҶ’ Pool] bloДҹunda:
  spatial boyut kГјГ§ГјlГјr, filtre sayДұsДұ artar.

Д°lk katmanlar: kenar, renk gibi basit Еҹeyler.
Derin katmanlar: Еҹekil parГ§alarДұ, nesneler вҖ” giderek daha soyut.
Bu hiyerarЕҹik Г¶Дҹrenme CNN'i gГјГ§lГј yapan Еҹey.

ida03.title: Perceptron, MLP, and Backpropagation
ida03.summary: The perceptron is the atomic building block: a linear threshold unit. Stacking layers creates an MLP. Backpropagation вҖ” efficient reverse-mode automatic differentiation through the computational graph вҖ” is the algorithm that makes training deep networks feasible.
ida03.context: Lecture 03 В· 26.10.2023 вҖ” Deep Learning Crash Course

sections.heading: MLP Forward Pass
sections.text:
For an $L$-layer MLP with weights $W^{(l)}$ and biases $b^{(l)}$:

$$a^{(0)} = x \quad (\text{input})$$
$$z^{(l)} = W^{(l)}\, a^{(l-1)} + b^{(l)}$$
$$a^{(l)} = \sigma(z^{(l)})$$
$$\hat{y} = a^{(L)} \quad (\text{output})$$

Common activations: ReLU $= \max(0,z)$, sigmoid $= 1/(1+e^{-z})$, softmax for classification.

Backprop chain rule: $\displaystyle\frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial W^{(l)}} = \frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial a^{(l)}} \cdot \frac{\partial a^{(l)}}{\partial z^{(l)}} \cdot \frac{\partial z^{(l)}}{\partial W^{(l)}}$

sections.heading: Why ReLU Over Sigmoid?
sections.text: Sigmoid $\sigma(z) = 1/(1+e^{-z})$ saturates at 0 and 1 вҖ” when $|z|$ is large, the gradient is near 0, causing vanishing gradients in deep networks. ReLU $= \max(0,z)$ has gradient exactly 1 for positive inputs, avoiding saturation. ReLU also produces sparse activations (many neurons output exactly 0), which has regularising effects. The downside is 'dying ReLU' вҖ” if a neuron consistently receives negative input, its gradient is always 0 and it never updates. Leaky ReLU and ELU fix this by providing a small non-zero gradient for $z < 0$.

sections.heading: Universal Approximation Theorem
sections.text: A single hidden layer MLP with a sufficient number of neurons can approximate any continuous function on a compact domain to arbitrary accuracy. However, 'sufficient' can mean exponentially many neurons. Deep networks exploit compositionality вҖ” layers learn hierarchical features (edges вҶ’ parts вҶ’ objects) вҖ” achieving the same approximation power with far fewer parameters. This is the key advantage of depth over width.

ida03.title: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs)
ida03.summary: CNNs replace fully-connected layers with local convolutions and pooling, exploiting two inductive biases of images: locality (pixels near each other are related) and translation equivariance (a cat detector should fire wherever the cat appears). This massively reduces parameters and improves generalisation.
ida03.context: Lecture 03 В· 26.10.2023 вҖ” CNN architecture essentials

sections.heading: Conv Layer Output Size and Parameter Count
sections.text:
Input: $H_{in} \times W_{in} \times C_{in}$, kernel size $k$, $C_{out}$ filters, stride $s$, padding $p$:

$$H_{out} = \lfloor (H_{in} + 2p - k)/s \rfloor + 1$$

Parameters: $k^2 \cdot C_{in} \cdot C_{out} + C_{out}$ (bias)

Receptive field after $L$ layers (stride 1): $R_L = k + (L-1)(k-1)$

Max Pooling: reduces spatial size by factor $s$, takes max in each $k\times k$ window вҖ” provides translation invariance.

sections.heading: Why Pooling and Why It Works
sections.text: A conv layer is equivariant to translation: if the input shifts by $(\Delta x, \Delta y)$, the output shifts by the same amount. Pooling makes the network invariant: the max over a $2\times 2$ region is the same whether the feature is at position $(0,0)$ or $(1,1)$ within that region. Max pooling is the standard because it retains the strongest activation вҖ” if a feature is present anywhere in the pooling window, the pool output reflects it. Average pooling retains overall energy but tends to blur features; global average pooling (GAP) collapses the entire spatial dimension to a single vector and is used in ResNet/VGG for classification.

sections.heading: Classic CNN Architectures
rows: Dense connections to all prior layers

ida03.title: Loss Functions and Regularisation for Image Tasks
ida03.summary: Choosing the right loss and regularisation strategy is crucial for image tasks. Cross-entropy for classification, MSE or L1 for regression. Dropout, weight decay, batch normalisation, and data augmentation prevent overfitting in data-limited image domains.
ida03.context: Lecture 03 В· 26.10.2023 вҖ” Practical training strategies

sections.heading: Common Loss Functions
sections.text:
**Cross-Entropy** (classification):
$$\mathcal{L}_{CE} = -\sum_{i} y_i \log(\hat{p}_i) \quad (\hat{p} = \mathrm{softmax}(z))$$

**Binary Cross-Entropy** (per-pixel segmentation):
$$\mathcal{L}_{BCE} = -[y \log(\hat{p}) + (1-y)\log(1-\hat{p})]$$

**Dice Loss** (handles class imbalance in segmentation):
$$\mathcal{L}_{Dice} = 1 - \frac{2|Y \cap \hat{Y}|}{|Y| + |\hat{Y}|}$$

**MSE** (regression, e.g. keypoint detection): $\mathcal{L}_{MSE} = \|y - \hat{y}\|^2$

sections.heading: Dice Loss vs Cross-Entropy in Segmentation
sections.text: In segmentation tasks with small objects (e.g. cell nuclei cover 2% of pixels), the cross-entropy loss is dominated by the majority background class вҖ” the network learns to predict background everywhere and gets low loss. Dice loss measures the overlap between prediction and ground truth, normalised by the total size of both, making it insensitive to class imbalance. In practice, combining both ($\mathcal{L} = \mathcal{L}_{CE} + \mathcal{L}_{Dice}$) gives the best results. Focal loss extends BCE by up-weighting hard-to-classify examples, also addressing imbalance.

sections.heading: Data Augmentation for Image Tasks
items: Random horizontal/vertical flips вҖ” free augmentation, preserves class
Random rotation (0вҖ“360В°) вҖ” important for rotationally unconstrained data (microscopy)
Random crop and resize вҖ” simulates different scales and viewpoints
Colour jitter (brightness, contrast, saturation, hue) вҖ” illumination robustness
Gaussian noise / blur вҖ” simulates sensor noise and out-of-focus regions
Elastic deformation вҖ” used in medical imaging (U-Net) to simulate tissue deformation
Mixup / CutMix вҖ” blend two images/labels for regularisation

ida04.title: Lecture 4 вҖ” Q & A
ida04.summary: IDA Lecture 4'Гјn tГјm sДұnav sorularДұ ve sade aГ§Дұklamalarla cevaplarДұ. Template matching, Hough transform, R-CNN ailesi, YOLO, SSD, FPN, Focal Loss.

sections.heading: S1. Template matching nasДұl Г§alДұЕҹДұyor? Benzerlik Г¶lГ§Гјtleri neler? KДұsДұtlamalarДұ nedir?
sections.text:
Bir Еҹablonu (template) alДұp tГјm gГ¶rГјntГј Гјzerinde kaydДұrДұrsДұn.
Her pozisyonda Еҹablonla o bГ¶lge arasДұndaki benzerliДҹi hesaplarsДұn.
En yГјksek benzerlik вҶ’ nesnenin orada olduДҹunu varsayarsДұn.

Benzerlik Г¶lГ§Гјtleri:
  SSD (Sum of Squared Differences) вҖ” dГјЕҹГјk = daha benzer
  Normalized Cross-Correlation вҖ” 1'e yakДұn = daha benzer
  Normalized correlation kullanmak iyi Г§ГјnkГј aydДұnlatma deДҹiЕҹimine karЕҹДұ daha saДҹlam.

Еһablonu nasДұl belirlersin? Bir Г¶rnek nesnenin crop'u ya da elle Г§izilmiЕҹ.

KДұsДұtlamalar:
  Rotasyon, scale deДҹiЕҹimi veya deformasyona karЕҹДұ hiГ§ dayanДұklДұ deДҹil.
  Her pozisyon iГ§in hesaplama gerekiyor вҖ” yavaЕҹ.
  Nesne gГ¶rГјnГјmГј deДҹiЕҹirse tamamen bozuluyor.

sections.heading: S2. Hough transform nedir? Еһekil tespiti nasДұl yapДұlДұyor? Accumulator matrix nedir?
sections.text:
Fikir: her kenar pikseli 'ben hangi Г§izginin (ya da dairenin) Гјzerinde olabilirim?'
sorusunu sorar ve buna gГ¶re oy kullanДұr.

ГҮizgi tespiti iГ§in (y = mx + b parametreleri):
  Her edge pikseli (x,y) iГ§in: bu noktadan geГ§en tГјm olasДұ Г§izgileri
  parametre uzayДұnda (m, b) birer nokta olarak iЕҹaretle.
  ГҮok sayДұda piksel aynДұ (m,b)'ye oy verdiyse вҶ’ orada bir Г§izgi var.

Accumulator matrix = parametre uzayДұndaki sayaГ§ tablosu.
  Her (m,b) kombinasyonu iГ§in kaГ§ piksel oy kullandДұДҹДұnДұ tutar.
  Zirve noktalar вҶ’ tespit edilen Еҹekiller.

Daireler iГ§in: (x0, y0, r) 3 parametre вҖ” 3D accumulator.

FaydalДұ: gГјrГјltГјye ve eksik piksellere karЕҹДұ dayanДұklДұ.
KДұsДұt: parametre sayДұsДұ arttДұkГ§a hesaplama Г§ok aДҹДұrlaЕҹДұyor.

sections.heading: S3. Hough transform'un avantajlarДұ ve kДұsДұtlamalarДұ nelerdir?
sections.text:
Avantajlar:
  GГјrГјltГјye dayanДұklДұ вҖ” Г§oДҹunluk oylamasДұ yapДұlДұyor.
  Eksik, tamamlanmamДұЕҹ kenarlarla da Г§alДұЕҹДұyor.
  Г–rtГјЕҹen Еҹekilleri tespit edebiliyor.
  Belirli geometrik Еҹekiller iГ§in Г§ok gГјvenilir.

KДұsДұtlamalar:
  Parametre sayДұsДұ arttДұkГ§a bellek ve hesaplama patlДұyor
  (Г§izgi 2D, daire 3D, genel Еҹekil daha fazla).
  Еһekli Г¶nceden bilmen gerekiyor вҖ” parametrik olmayan formlara uygulanamaz.
  GerГ§ek dГјnya nesnelerinin karmaЕҹДұk Еҹekillerine uymuyor.

sections.heading: S4. R-CNN nasДұl Г§alДұЕҹДұyor? Region proposal nedir? Neden yavaЕҹ?
sections.text:
R-CNN ГјГ§ adДұm:
  1. Selective Search ile ~2000 aday bГ¶lge Г¶ner (region proposals)
  2. Her bГ¶lgeyi ayrДұ ayrДұ CNN'den geГ§ir вҶ’ Г¶zellik vektГ¶rГј
  3. Her Г¶zellik vektГ¶rГјnГј SVM ile sДұnДұflandДұr + bounding box regress et

CNN sadece sДұnДұflandДұrma yapДұyor, Г¶zellik olarak kullanДұlДұyor.

Neden yavaЕҹ?
  2000 region proposal Г— CNN forward pass = 2000 ayrДұ CNN Г§alДұЕҹtДұrДұyorsun.
  Bir gГ¶rГјntГј iГ§in 47 saniye test sГјresi вҖ” gerГ§ek zamanlДұ kullanДұm imkansДұz.
  EДҹitmesi de zor: 3 ayrДұ parГ§ayДұ ayrДұ ayrДұ eДҹitmek gerekiyor
  (CNN, SVM, bbox regressor).

sections.heading: S5. Fast R-CNN ve Faster R-CNN farkДұ nedir? Neden daha hДұzlДұ?
sections.text:
Fast R-CNN:
  GГ¶rГјntГјyГј bir kez CNN'den geГ§ir вҶ’ feature map Гјret.
  Region proposal'larДұ doДҹrudan feature map Гјzerinde uygula (RoI Pooling).
  2000 ayrДұ CNN yerine 1 CNN вҶ’ Г§ok daha hДұzlДұ (0.3 saniye).
  Ama region proposal hГўlГў Selective Search ile вҖ” o hГўlГў yavaЕҹ.

Faster R-CNN:
  Region Proposal Network (RPN) ekliyor вҖ” proposal Гјretimi de CNN ile yapДұlДұyor.
  Feature map'i hem RPN hem de sДұnДұflandДұrma iГ§in paylaЕҹДұyor.
  UГ§tan uca eДҹitilebilir вҖ” tek bir modelde her Еҹey.
  ~0.2 saniye, ve Г§ok daha doДҹru.

Fark Г¶zeti: Her seferinde daha fazla iЕҹi CNN'e taЕҹДұdДұlar,
ve feature map'i paylaЕҹtДұrdДұlar.

sections.heading: S6. RoI Pooling nedir? RPN nedir? Anchor box nedir?
sections.text:
RoI Pooling:
  Feature map Гјzerinde bir region proposal bГ¶lgesini al,
  sabit boyutlu Г§ДұktДұya dГ¶nГјЕҹtГјr (Г¶rn. 7Г—7).
  BГ¶lge her boyutta olabilir, FC layer sabit boyut istiyor.

Region Proposal Network (RPN):
  Feature map Гјzerinde kayan bir pencere.
  Her pozisyonda 'burada nesne var mДұ?' ve bounding box tahmini yapar.
  CNN ile eДҹitildiДҹi iГ§in hДұzlДұ.

Anchor box:
  Her pozisyon iГ§in Г¶nceden tanДұmlanmДұЕҹ farklДұ boyut/oran kombinasyonlarДұ.
  Г–rn: kГјГ§Гјk kare, bГјyГјk kare, yatay dikdГ¶rtgen, dikey dikdГ¶rtgen...
  RPN bu anchor'larДұ referans alarak 'bu anchor ne kadar kaymДұЕҹ/bГјyГјmГјЕҹ?'
  sorusunu cevaplar вҖ” doДҹrudan koordinat deДҹil, fark tahmin eder.

sections.heading: S7. One-shot detector'lar neden daha hДұzlДұ? YOLO nasДұl Г§alДұЕҹДұyor?
sections.text:
R-CNN ailesi iki aЕҹamalДұ: Г¶nce region proposal, sonra sДұnДұflandДұrma.
One-shot (single-stage) detector'larda bu iki aЕҹama tek geГ§iЕҹte yapДұlДұyor.
AyrДұ bir proposal adДұmДұ yok вҶ’ Г§ok daha hДұzlДұ.

YOLO:
  GГ¶rГјntГјyГј SГ—S grid'e bГ¶l (Г¶rn. 7Г—7).
  Her grid hГјcresi: B tane bounding box tahmini + her box iГ§in confidence,
  ve tГјm sДұnДұflar iГ§in olasДұlДұk vektГ¶rГј.
  Tek bir CNN forward pass'inde tГјm bunlarДұ tahmin ediyor.
  GerГ§ek zamanlДұ: 45 fps.

Dezavantaj: grid yapДұsДұndan dolayДұ kГјГ§Гјk ve birbirine yakДұn
nesnelerde zorlanДұyor. Her grid hГјcresi sadece 1 nesne tespit edebiliyor.

sections.heading: S8. YOLO'nun dezavantajlarДұ neler? SSD bunlarДұ nasДұl Г§Г¶zГјyor?
sections.text:
YOLO'nun sorunlarДұ:
  KГјГ§Гјk nesnelerde zayДұf вҖ” grid Г§ok kaba kalДұyor.
  Her grid hГјcresi sadece 1 nesne tespit edebiliyor.
  Birbirine yakДұn nesneleri ayДұrt etmekte zorlanДұyor.
  Aspect ratio Г§eЕҹitliliДҹi sДұnДұrlДұ.

SSD (Single Shot Detector):
  Birden fazla feature map kullanДұr вҖ” farklДұ boyuttaki nesnelere uyum.
    BГјyГјk feature map (erken katman): kГјГ§Гјk nesneler.
    KГјГ§Гјk feature map (derin katman): bГјyГјk nesneler.
  Her feature map'te farklДұ boyut ve oranlarla anchor box'lar.
  BГ¶ylece Г§ok farklДұ Г¶lГ§eklerdeki nesneleri tek geГ§iЕҹte yakalДұyor.

SSD hДұzДұ YOLO ile karЕҹДұlaЕҹtДұrДұlabilir ama Г¶zellikle kГјГ§Гјk nesnelerde daha iyi.

sections.heading: S9. Feature Pyramid Network (FPN) nedir? Nesne tespitinde nasДұl kullanДұlДұr?
sections.text:
Problem: CNN'de derin katmanlar semantik aГ§Дұdan zengin ama dГјЕҹГјk Г§Г¶zГјnГјrlГјklГј.
Erken katmanlar yГјksek Г§Г¶zГјnГјrlГјklГј ama az semantik.
KГјГ§Гјk nesneler erken katmanlarda, bГјyГјk nesneler derin katmanlarda iyi.

FPN Г§Г¶zГјmГј:
  Bottom-up: normal CNN forward pass (her katmanda boyut kГјГ§ГјlГјr).
  Top-down: derin katmandan geri yukarДұ Г§Дұkarken lateral connection'larla
  Гјst katmanlarДұn semantik bilgisini alt katmanlara aktar.
  Her Г¶lГ§ekte hem semantik hem de detay bilgisi var.

Nesne tespitinde:
  Her FPN Г§ДұktДұsДұnДұ (farklДұ Г¶lГ§ek) iГ§in ayrДұ detection head kullan.
  KГјГ§Гјk nesneler bГјyГјk feature map'ten, bГјyГјk nesneler kГјГ§Гјk feature map'ten tespit.
  Faster R-CNN + FPN = Г§ok gГјГ§lГј kombinasyon.

sections.heading: S10. Focal Loss nedir? Nerede kullanДұlДұyor?
sections.text:
Problem: one-shot detector'larda binlerce anchor box var.
BunlarДұn bГјyГјk Г§oДҹunluДҹu boЕҹ alan (background) вҖ” nesne yok.
Standart cross-entropy'de bu kolay Г¶rnekler loss'u domine ediyor,
aДҹ asДұl zor Г¶rneklerden (gerГ§ek nesneler) yeterince Г¶Дҹrenemiyor.

Focal Loss:
  Kolay Г¶rneklerin (yГјksek confidence, doДҹru tahmin) katkДұsДұnДұ otomatik kГјГ§ГјltГјr.
  Zor Г¶rneklerin (dГјЕҹГјk confidence, yanlДұЕҹ tahmin) katkДұsДұnДұ bГјyГјtГјr.
  FL = -(1 - p_t)^Оі Г— log(p_t)
  Оі (gamma) ne kadar bГјyГјkse, kolay Г¶rneklerin etkisi o kadar azalДұr.

Nerede kullanДұlДұyor? RetinaNet'in classification head'inde.
FPN + Focal Loss kombinasyonu вҶ’ two-stage detector kadar doДҹru,
one-stage detector kadar hДұzlДұ.

ida04.title: Classical Object Detection: Template Matching and Hough Transform
ida04.summary: Before deep learning, object detection relied on template matching (slide a prototype over the image) and the Hough Transform (vote for parametric shapes). Both are still relevant for constrained industrial inspection and geometrically regular objects.

sections.heading: Normalised Cross-Correlation (Template Matching)
sections.text:
Template $T$ of size $m\times n$, image patch $I_{(x,y)}$ centred at $(x,y)$:

$$\mathrm{NCC}(x,y) = \frac{\sum_{u,v}[T(u,v)-\bar{T}][I(x+u,y+v)-\bar{I}_{(x,y)}]}{\sqrt{\sum_{u,v}[T-\bar{T}]^2 \cdot \sum_{u,v}[I_{(x,y)}-\bar{I}_{(x,y)}]^2}}$$

$\mathrm{NCC} \in [-1, 1]$. Value near 1 = strong match. Invariant to linear intensity changes.

Alternative: Sum of Squared Differences (SSD) вҖ” faster but not illumination invariant.

sections.heading: Hough Transform for Lines and Circles
sections.text: The Hough Transform detects parametric shapes by voting in parameter space. For lines: each edge pixel $(x,y)$ casts a vote for all lines passing through it, parameterised as $\rho = x\cos\theta + y\sin\theta$. Lines become peaks in the $\rho$-$\theta$ accumulator. For circles of known radius $r$: each edge pixel votes for all circle centres $(a,b)$ such that $(x-a)^2 + (y-b)^2 = r^2$ вҖ” a circle in $(a,b)$-space. Peaks in the accumulator locate circle centres. The Hough Transform is robust to partial occlusion (missing arc segments still vote for the correct centre) but slow for high-dimensional parameter spaces.

ida04.title: Region-Based Detection: R-CNN Family
ida04.summary: The R-CNN line of work brought deep learning to object detection by combining region proposal algorithms with CNN feature extraction. Each iteration вҖ” R-CNN, Fast R-CNN, Faster R-CNN вҖ” eliminated a bottleneck, ultimately reaching real-time performance.
ida04.context: Lecture 04 В· 09.11.2023 вҖ” Deep object detection evolution

sections.heading: R-CNN Family Comparison
rows: Shared CNN + RoI Pooling (1 forward pass)

sections.heading: RoI Pooling and RoI Align
sections.text:
**RoI Pooling**: given a region of arbitrary size from the feature map, divide into a fixed $k\times k$ grid and max-pool each cell. Problem: quantisation error from integer rounding distorts alignment.

**RoI Align** (Mask R-CNN): instead of rounding, use bilinear interpolation to compute feature values at exact (floating-point) grid points:
$$f(x,y) = \sum_{i,j} f(i,j) \cdot \max(0,1-|x-i|) \cdot \max(0,1-|y-j|)$$

RoI Align eliminates the misalignment error вҖ” critical for pixel-level tasks like instance segmentation where 1-pixel errors matter.

sections.heading: Region Proposal Network (RPN)
sections.text: The key innovation of Faster R-CNN is the Region Proposal Network (RPN), which shares the backbone CNN features with the detection head. The RPN slides a small network over the feature map and predicts, at each location, $k$ anchor boxes (rectangles of pre-defined aspect ratios and scales). For each anchor, the RPN outputs: (1) objectness score (foreground vs background), and (2) bounding-box regression offsets. Only anchors with IoU > 0.7 with a ground-truth box are treated as positive. This makes proposal generation essentially free вҖ” the RPN adds only ~10ms overhead. The proposals feed into the detection head (RoI Pooling + classifier + bbox regressor).

ida04.title: YOLO and Single-Stage Detection
ida04.summary: YOLO (You Only Look Once) reframes detection as a single regression problem: one forward pass predicts all bounding boxes and class probabilities simultaneously. It achieves real-time performance by eliminating the region proposal stage entirely.

sections.heading: YOLO Output Structure
sections.text:
Image divided into $S \times S$ grid. Each cell predicts $B$ bounding boxes and $C$ class probabilities.

Each box prediction: $(x, y, w, h, \text{confidence})$ where confidence $= \mathrm{Pr}(\text{object}) \times \mathrm{IoU}_{\text{pred/truth}}$

Output tensor: $S \times S \times (B \times 5 + C)$

For YOLO v1: $S=7, B=2, C=20$ (VOC) вҶ’ $7\times7\times30$ tensor.

**IoU (Intersection over Union)**:
$$\mathrm{IoU} = \frac{|A \cap B|}{|A \cup B|}$$
Threshold: IoU > 0.5 typically considered a positive detection (mAP metric).

sections.heading: NMS: Non-Maximum Suppression
sections.text: Detection models output many overlapping boxes for the same object. Non-Maximum Suppression (NMS) eliminates duplicates: (1) sort all boxes by confidence score; (2) keep the highest-confidence box; (3) remove all boxes with IoU > threshold (e.g. 0.5) against the kept box; (4) repeat. The result is one box per object. Soft-NMS improves this by decaying rather than removing overlapping boxes, better handling legitimate occlusion cases. NMS is the standard post-processing step for all detection models.

ida04.title: Fast R-CNN vs Faster R-CNN: ROI Pooling Derinlemesine
ida04.summary: R-CNN вҶ’ Fast R-CNN вҶ’ Faster R-CNN geГ§iЕҹi: ROI Pooling nasДұl Г§alДұЕҹДұyor, region proposal nereden geliyor, RPN ne deДҹiЕҹtiriyor, ROI Align neden gerekti.
ida04.context: Lecture 04 В· Object Detection вҖ” R-CNN ailesi

sections.heading: R-CNN вҶ’ Fast R-CNN
sections.text:
R-CNN'de problem: Her region proposal iГ§in CNN'i ayrДұ ayrДұ Г§alДұЕҹtДұrДұyorsun.
2000 proposal = 2000 forward pass. ГҮok yavaЕҹ.

Fast R-CNN'in Г§Г¶zГјmГј: Г–nce tГјm gГ¶rГјntГјyГј bir kez CNN'den geГ§irip feature map Г§Дұkar.
Sonra proposal'larДұ bu feature map Гјzerine yansДұt.

Ama problem Еҹu: Selective Search orijinal gГ¶rГјntГјde Г§alДұЕҹДұyor ve
"Еҹu koordinatlardaki kutu ilginГ§ gГ¶rГјnГјyor" diyor. Feature map ise orijinal
gГ¶rГјntГјnГјn kГјГ§ГјltГјlmГјЕҹ hali (Г¶rn. VGG'de 16x downscale). Yani orijinal
gГ¶rГјntГјdeki 160Г—80 piksellik bir kutu, feature map'te 10Г—5 hГјcreye denk geliyor.

sections.heading: ROI Pooling ne yapДұyor
sections.text:
Feature map'teki bu 10Г—5'lik (veya herhangi boyuttaki) bГ¶lgeyi sabit boyuta
getirmek lazДұm Г§ГјnkГј FC layer sabit boyut bekliyor.

ROI Pooling Еҹunu yapar:
  1. Feature map'teki o bГ¶lgeyi al (diyelim 10Г—5)
  2. Hedef Г§ДұktДұ boyutuna bГ¶l (diyelim 7Г—7 grid istiyoruz)
  3. Her grid hГјcresine dГјЕҹen feature deДҹerlerinin max'ДұnДұ al
  4. SonuГ§: her zaman 7Г—7 вҖ” boyutu ne olursa olsun

"Section neye gГ¶re seГ§iliyor" sorunun cevabДұ:
  Selective Search orijinal gГ¶rГјntГјde renk/doku/kenar benzerliДҹine bakarak
  ~2000 aday kutu Г¶neriyor. Bu kutularДұ feature map koordinatlarДұna Г§evirmek
  iГ§in stride'a bГ¶lГјyorsun. O kadar вҖ” seГ§im tamamen Selective Search'Гјn iЕҹi,
  CNN ile ilgisi yok.

ROI Pooling'in sorunu:
  10Г·7 = 1.42... Bu sayДұ tam sayДұ deДҹil, o yГјzden grid sДұnДұrlarДұnДұ yuvarlДұyorsun.
  BazДұ feature map hГјcreleri 2 kez sayДұlДұyor, bazДұlarДұ hiГ§ sayДұlmДұyor.
  KГјГ§Гјk nesneler veya maske gibi piksel hassasiyeti gereken iЕҹler iГ§in bu hata birikir.

sections.heading: Fast R-CNN вҶ’ Faster R-CNN
sections.text:
Fast R-CNN'de hГўlГў bir darboДҹaz var:
  Selective Search CPU'da Г§alДұЕҹДұyor ve ~2 saniye sГјrГјyor,
  aДҹДұn geri kalanДұ GPU'da ms cinsinden Г§alДұЕҹДұrken.

Faster R-CNN'in Г§Г¶zГјmГј: Selective Search'Гј tamamen at, onun yerine bir
Region Proposal Network (RPN) koy вҖ” ve bu RPN aynДұ feature map Гјzerinde Г§alДұЕҹsДұn.

RPN Еҹunu yapar:
  - Feature map Гјzerinde 3Г—3'lГјk bir window kayar
  - Her konumda farklДұ boyut/oran kombinasyonlarДұnda anchor box'lar tanДұmlar
    (Г¶rn. 9 farklДұ anchor: 3 boyut Г— 3 oran)
  - Her anchor iГ§in iki Еҹey tahmin eder: bu anchor bir nesneyi kapsДұyor mu
    (foreground/background score) + bbox offset
  - YГјksek skorlu anchor'lar proposal olarak Г§Дұkar

ArtДұk proposal Гјretimi de GPU'da, aynДұ feature map Гјzerinde, CNN aДҹДұrlДұklarДұyla
birlikte eДҹitilebilir. Bunun dДұЕҹДұnda sonrasДұ aynДұ:
  proposal вҶ’ ROI Pooling вҶ’ FC вҶ’ classification + bbox.

sections.heading: ROI Align (Mask R-CNN'de)
sections.text: ROI Pooling'deki yuvarlama hatasДұnДұ dГјzeltiyor вҖ” grid sДұnДұrlarДұnДұ tam sayДұya Г§evirmek yerine bilinear interpolation ile tam koordinattaki deДҹeri hesaplДұyor. Segmentasyon maskesi gibi piksel hassasiyeti gereken iЕҹler iГ§in Г¶nemli.

ida04.title: Feature Pyramid Networks and Focal Loss
ida04.summary: FPN builds a multi-scale feature pyramid from a single backbone, enabling detection of objects at all scales. Focal Loss solves the extreme foreground/background class imbalance in single-stage detectors that previously forced two-stage designs.
ida04.context: Lecture 04 В· 09.11.2023 вҖ” Advanced detection components

sections.heading: Feature Pyramid Network (FPN)
sections.text: CNNs produce hierarchical feature maps: shallow layers have high spatial resolution but low semantic content; deep layers have low resolution but high semantic content. FPN adds a top-down pathway with lateral connections: start from the deepest feature map, upsample it (2Г—, nearest-neighbour), and add it element-wise to the corresponding bottom-up feature map (after a 1Г—1 conv to match channels). This produces a set of feature maps at multiple scales, each enriched by both local detail (from the bottom-up path) and global context (from the top-down path). Each level of the pyramid detects objects at its corresponding scale.

sections.heading: Focal Loss for Class Imbalance
sections.text:
Standard cross-entropy: $\mathcal{L}_{CE} = -\log(p_t)$

Focal Loss (Lin et al., 2017):
$$\mathcal{L}_{FL}(p_t) = -\alpha_t (1-p_t)^\gamma \log(p_t)$$

$(1-p_t)^\gamma$: modulating factor. For easy examples ($p_t \to 1$): factor $\to 0$ вҶ’ loss suppressed. For hard examples ($p_t$ small): factor $\approx 1$ вҶ’ loss unchanged.

$\alpha_t$: class balancing weight. $\gamma=2$ typical. This allows single-stage detectors (RetinaNet) to match two-stage accuracy by focusing training on hard negatives.

sections.heading: Why Focal Loss Enabled Single-Stage Detectors
sections.text: In a single-stage detector over an image, there may be $100,000+$ anchor boxes, of which only a handful overlap with actual objects. The vast majority are easy negatives (clearly background). With standard CE loss, gradients from easy negatives overwhelm the signal from hard positives and hard negatives. Hard negative mining (hand-selecting a 3:1 ratio of hard negatives) was the two-stage approach. Focal loss achieves the same effect automatically вҖ” easy negatives are down-weighted by $(1-p_t)^\gamma$, and their total contribution becomes negligible even though they are numerous.

ida05.title: Lecture 5 вҖ” Q & A
ida05.summary: IDA Lecture 5'in tГјm sДұnav sorularДұ ve sade aГ§Дұklamalarla cevaplarДұ. Semantic segmentation, active learning, U-Net, Mask R-CNN, RoIAlign, StarDist, Cellpose.
ida05.context: Lecture 05 В· 16.11.2023 вҖ” Machine Learning-based Image Segmentation

sections.heading: S1. ML semantic segmentation iГ§in nasДұl kullanДұlДұr? Random forest neden iyi?
sections.text:
Her pikseli bir sДұnДұfa atamak istiyorsun.
Bunun iГ§in her pikselden Г¶zellikler Г§ДұkarДұrsДұn:
  renk, gradyan yГ¶nГј, kenar yoДҹunluДҹu, doku, komЕҹu piksel istatistikleri...
Sonra bu Г¶zellikleri bir classifier'a verirsin.

Random forest neden iyi seГ§im?
  HДұzlДұ eДҹitim ve tahmin.
  Hangi Г¶zelliklerin Г¶nemli olduДҹunu gГ¶sterebiliyor.
  Overfitting'e karЕҹДұ dayanДұklДұ (ensemble yГ¶ntemi).
  Az veriyle bile makul sonuГ§ verir.
  Sliding window ile piksel piksel uygulanabilir.

sections.heading: S2. Active learning nedir? KullanДұcДұyДұ dГ¶ngГјye dahil etmek neden mantДұklДұ?
sections.text:
Etiketleme pahalДұ ve zaman alДұcДұ, Г¶zellikle medikal gГ¶rГјntГјlerde.
Active learning Еҹunu yapar:
  Model Г¶nce az etiketli veriyle eДҹitilir.
  Sonra 'hangi Г¶rnekleri bilmiyorum?' diye sorar.
  En emin olmadДұДҹДұ pikselleri/bГ¶lgeleri kullanДұcДұya gГ¶sterir.
  KullanДұcДұ sadece bu zor Г¶rnekleri etiketler.
  Model tekrar eДҹitilir, dГ¶ngГј devam eder.

Neden mantДұklДұ?
  Zaten emin olunan Г¶rnekleri etiketlemek zaman kaybДұ.
  Az etiketle maksimum Г¶Дҹrenme.
  KullanДұcДұ ekspertizi en kritik yerlere odaklanДұyor.

sections.heading: S3. CNN semantic segmentation iГ§in nasДұl kullanДұlДұr? Sliding window'un dezavantajДұ?
sections.text:
CNN her piksele bir sДұnДұf etiketi vermek iГ§in kullanДұlДұr.

Stepping window yaklaЕҹДұmДұ:
  Her piksel etrafДұnda bir pencere al вҶ’ CNN'e ver вҶ’ o pikselin sДұnДұfДұnДұ tahmin et.
  TГјm pikseller iГ§in tekrarla.

DezavantajДұ:
  Her piksel iГ§in ayrДұ CNN forward pass вҶ’ Г§ok yavaЕҹ.
  Pencereler bГјyГјk Г¶lГ§Гјde Г¶rtГјЕҹГјyor вҖ” aynДұ piksel yГјzlerce kez iЕҹleniyor.
  GerГ§ek zamanlДұ ya da bГјyГјk gГ¶rГјntГјler iГ§in kullanДұlamaz.

ГҮГ¶zГјm: Fully Convolutional Network (FCN) вҖ” tГјm gГ¶rГјntГјyГј bir kez iЕҹle,
her pozisyon iГ§in aynДұ anda tahmin Гјret.

sections.heading: S4. Unpooling / up-convolution nedir? 1Г—1 conv ne iЕҹe yarar? Batch norm nasДұl Г§alДұЕҹДұr?
sections.text:
Unpooling / transposed convolution:
  Pooling spatial boyutu kГјГ§ГјltГјyordu.
  Segmentasyon iГ§in orijinal boyuta geri dГ¶nmek gerekiyor.
  Transposed conv (up-conv): Г¶Дҹrenilen aДҹДұrlДұklarla boyutu bГјyГјtГјr.
  Nearest neighbor unpooling: sadece deДҹeri kopyalar, Г¶Дҹrenme yok.

1Г—1 convolution:
  Spatial boyutu deДҹiЕҹtirmez.
  Sadece channel sayДұsДұnДұ deДҹiЕҹtirir (azaltДұr veya artДұrДұr).
  Parametre azaltmak ve channel'lar arasДұ iliЕҹki kurmak iГ§in.

Batch normalization:
  Her mini-batch iГ§in aktivasyonlarДұ normalize et (ortalama=0, std=1).
  Sonra Оі ve ОІ ile Г¶lГ§ekle вҖ” bunlar Г¶Дҹrenilen parametreler.
  EДҹitimi stabilize eder, daha bГјyГјk learning rate kullanДұlabilir,
  hafif regularization etkisi var.

sections.heading: S5. U-Net mimarisi nedir? Skip connection neden Г¶nemli? 3D'ye nasДұl taЕҹДұnДұr?
sections.text:
U-Net: encoder-decoder mimarisi, U Еҹeklinde.

Encoder (sol kol): [Conv вҶ’ ReLU вҶ’ Pool] Г— 4
  Her adДұmda spatial boyut yarДұya iner, channel sayДұsДұ iki katДұna Г§Дұkar.
  YГјksek seviyeli Г¶zellikler Г¶Дҹrenilir.

Decoder (saДҹ kol): transposed conv ile boyutu bГјyГјt, channel azalt.
  Her decoder adДұmДұnda karЕҹДұ encoder adДұmДұndan skip connection gelir.

Skip connection neden Г¶nemli?
  Encoder'daki ince detaylar (kenarlar, dokular) pooling sДұrasДұnda kaybolur.
  Skip connection bu detaylarДұ direkt decoder'a aktarДұr.
  Segmentation hem semantik bilgiyi hem de konumsal detayДұ kullanabilir.

3D'ye taЕҹДұma: 2D conv ve pool yerine 3D versiyonlarДұnДұ kullan.
Sparse 2D annotation ile 3D eДҹitim:
  Her 3D volume iГ§in birkaГ§ 2D slice etiketli yeterli,
  aДҹ interpolasyonla 3D Г¶Дҹrenebiliyor.

sections.heading: S6. Mask R-CNN nasДұl Г§alДұЕҹДұyor? Mask branch'in Г§ДұktДұsДұ nedir?
sections.text:
Faster R-CNN'e bir Еҹube daha ekliyorsun.

Faster R-CNN zaten yapДұyor:
  RPN вҶ’ region proposals
  RoI Pooling вҶ’ sabit boyutlu Г¶zellik
  Classification head вҶ’ sДұnДұf tahmini
  BBox regression head вҶ’ bounding box dГјzeltmesi

Mask R-CNN ekliyor:
  Mask head: kГјГ§Гјk bir FCN (fully conv network)
  Her RoI iГ§in 28Г—28'lik binary mask tahmin ediyor.
  Her sДұnДұf iГ§in ayrДұ mask Г§ДұktДұsДұ var (Г¶rn. 80 sДұnДұf вҶ’ 80 adet 28Г—28 mask).
  SДұnДұf tahminine gГ¶re doДҹru mask seГ§ilip orijinal boyuta Г¶lГ§ekleniyor.

SonuГ§: her nesne iГ§in ayrДұ piksel maskesi вҶ’ instance segmentation.

sections.heading: S7. RoIAlign nedir? RoI Pooling'in instance segmentation iГ§in sorunu ne?
sections.text:
RoI Pooling sorunu:
  Region proposal koordinatlarДұ float вҖ” feature map piksel hizalamasДұ integer.
  Yuvarlama yapДұlДұyor вҶ’ pikseller yanlДұЕҹ hizalanДұyor.
  Bounding box iГ§in yeterli ama piksel hassasiyeti gerektiren
  mask iГ§in bГјyГјk sorun вҖ” mask kayДұyor.

RoIAlign Г§Г¶zГјmГј:
  Yuvarlamak yerine bilinear interpolation kullanДұr.
  Float koordinatlarda tam olarak istenen konumu hesaplar.
  Her RoI Г§ДұktДұ hГјcresi iГ§in 4 nokta Г¶rnekle, bilinear interpole et.
  HiГ§ quantization yok вҶ’ piksel hassas hizalama.

SonuГ§: Mask R-CNN RoI Pooling yerine RoIAlign kullanДұnca
mask kalitesi Г¶nemli Г¶lГ§Гјde artДұyor.

sections.heading: S8. StarDist (star-convex polygon) yaklaЕҹДұmДұ nedir?
sections.text:
Fikir: her hГјcre merkezi etrafДұnda star-convex polygon kullanarak sДұnДұrДұ temsil et.

NasДұl Г§alДұЕҹДұr?
  Her piksel iГ§in aДҹ iki Еҹey tahmin eder:
    1. Bu piksel bir nesne merkezine ne kadar yakДұn? (distance map)
    2. Bu noktadan N yГ¶nde (Г¶rn. 32 ДұЕҹДұn) nesne sДұnДұrДұna uzaklДұk.
  Bu uzaklДұklardan star-convex polygon oluЕҹturulur.

EДҹitim hedefleri: her piksel iГ§in uzaklДұk deДҹerleri.
SДұnДұrda olan nesneler: sadece gГ¶rГјnen kДұsДұm tahmin edilir.
ГҮДұktДұ katmanlarДұ: 1 distance + N radial distance map.

Neden iyi?
  Yuvarlak/oval nesneler (hГјcreler) iГ§in Г§ok uygun.
  Г–rtГјЕҹen nesneleri NMS ile ayДұrДұyor.
  U-Net gibi encoder-decoder mimari kullanДұyor.

sections.heading: S9. Cellpose nasДұl Г§alДұЕҹДұyor? Gradient flow nedir? 3D segmentasyon nasДұl?
sections.text:
Cellpose'un fikri: her piksele hГјcre merkezine doДҹru bir akДұЕҹ vektГ¶rГј Г¶Дҹret.

Ground truth dГ¶nГјЕҹГјmГј:
  Her etiketli hГјcre iГ§in simГјle edilmiЕҹ gradient flow hesaplanДұr.
  Sanki merkeze doДҹru akan bir sДұvДұ var gibi вҖ” difГјzyon simulasyonu.
  Her piksel: 'hangi yГ¶ne akmalДұyДұm?' vektГ¶rГј alДұyor.

AДҹ mimarisi:
  U-Net benzeri.
  ГҮДұktДұ: yatay akДұЕҹ + dikey akДұЕҹ + hГјcre olasДұlДұДҹДұ (3 kanal).

HГјcre instance'Дұ nasДұl elde edilir?
  Her pikseli gradient flow boyunca akДұt.
  AynДұ noktaya yakДұnsayan pikseller aynДұ hГјcreye ait.
  KГјmeleme ile instance'larДұ belirle.

3D iГ§in:
  SeГ§enek 1: her eksende (XY, XZ, YZ) 2D Г§alДұЕҹtДұr, sonuГ§larДұ birleЕҹtir.
  SeГ§enek 2: direkt 3D aДҹ eДҹit.

ida05.title: Semantic vs Instance Segmentation
ida05.summary: Segmentation assigns a label to every pixel. Semantic segmentation gives each pixel a class label (all cells = class 1). Instance segmentation further distinguishes individual objects (cell A vs cell B). Panoptic segmentation combines both.

left.title: Semantic Segmentation
points: Each pixel gets one class label
All instances of same class are merged

right.title: Instance Segmentation
points: Each pixel gets a class + instance ID

sections.heading: Segmentation Evaluation Metrics
sections.text:
**Intersection over Union (IoU / Jaccard)** for class $c$:
$$\mathrm{IoU}_c = \frac{|P_c \cap G_c|}{|P_c \cup G_c|}$$

**Mean IoU (mIoU)**: average IoU across all classes.

**Dice Coefficient (F1 for pixel sets)**:
$$\mathrm{Dice} = \frac{2|P \cap G|}{|P| + |G|}$$

For instance segmentation: **AP** (Average Precision) at IoU thresholds 0.5вҖ“0.95 (COCO metric).

ida05.title: U-Net: Encoder-Decoder for Biomedical Segmentation
ida05.summary: U-Net (Ronneberger et al. 2015) introduced the encoder-decoder with skip connections for biomedical image segmentation. The skip connections preserve fine spatial detail lost during downsampling, achieving state-of-the-art results with very limited training data.

sections.heading: U-Net Architecture: Encoder, Bottleneck, Decoder
sections.text: The encoder (contracting path) applies repeated 3Г—3 conv + ReLU + max-pool blocks, halving spatial resolution at each step while doubling channels: 64вҶ’128вҶ’256вҶ’512вҶ’1024. This builds a hierarchical feature representation. The decoder (expansive path) mirrors the encoder: transposed convolutions upsample the feature map, and skip connections concatenate the corresponding encoder feature map (same resolution). The concatenated features are then processed by 3Г—3 convolutions. Finally, a 1Г—1 convolution produces the class probability map. The skip connections are the key innovation: they pass fine-grained spatial information (exact cell boundaries, texture) directly to the decoder, bypassing the lossy bottleneck.

sections.heading: Why U-Net Excels with Limited Data
sections.text: The original U-Net paper achieved state-of-the-art results on the ISBI cell segmentation challenge using only 30 training images. Two factors: (1) The architecture itself encodes strong inductive biases for segmentation вҖ” locality, multi-scale context, skip connections. (2) Extensive data augmentation, especially elastic deformations that simulate the deformable nature of biological tissue. The weighted cross-entropy loss additionally up-weights pixels near cell borders, helping the network separate touching cells. Modern variants (ResUNet, Attention U-Net, TransUNet) further improve performance but the core encoder-decoder-skip connection design remains standard.

sections.heading: Weighted Loss for Cell Border Separation
sections.text:
U-Net uses a weighted binary cross-entropy to emphasise boundaries between touching cells:

$$\mathcal{L} = \sum_{x} w(x) \cdot \mathcal{L}_{CE}(\hat{p}(x), y(x))$$

where $w(x) = w_c(x) + w_0 \exp\!\left(-\frac{(d_1(x)+d_2(x))^2}{2\sigma^2}\right)$

$d_1(x)$: distance from $x$ to nearest cell, $d_2(x)$: distance to second nearest. The exponential peaks in the narrow gap between touching cells, encouraging the network to predict background (border) between them.

ida05.title: Mask R-CNN and Instance Segmentation
ida05.summary: Mask R-CNN extends Faster R-CNN by adding a small FCN mask head parallel to the box/class head. Combined with RoI Align (replacing RoI Pooling), it predicts per-instance binary masks without interfering with classification performance.
ida05.context: Lecture 05 В· 16.11.2023 вҖ” Instance segmentation with Mask R-CNN

sections.heading: The Mask Head: Parallel to Detection
sections.text: Mask R-CNN adds a third branch to Faster R-CNN alongside the class and box regression heads. After RoI Align produces a fixed-size feature for each region proposal, the mask head applies a series of 3Г—3 convolutions followed by a transposed convolution to upsample to a 28Г—28 binary mask per class. Crucially, the mask head predicts a mask for EACH class independently вҖ” there is no competition between classes. The final binary mask for a detected object is the mask of its predicted class. This decoupling prevents the mask quality from being affected by classification difficulty.

left.title: RoI Pooling
points: Quantisation error: up to 1 pixel misalignment

right.title: RoI Align
points: Mask AP improves ~3% vs RoI Pooling

ida05.title: Cellpose and StarDist: Biology-Inspired Segmentation
ida05.summary: Cellpose and StarDist are purpose-built instance segmentation methods for biological cells. Cellpose predicts vector flows toward each cell centre; StarDist represents cells as star-convex polygons. Both dramatically outperform generic methods on biological imagery.
ida05.context: Lecture 05 В· 16.11.2023 вҖ” Specialised cell segmentation

sections.heading: Cellpose: Flow-Based Cell Segmentation
sections.text: Cellpose (Stringer et al. 2021) is motivated by the observation that cells come in highly variable shapes, but they all have a centre. The network predicts two 'flow fields': horizontal and vertical gradients pointing toward each cell's centre (like a vector field pointing inward). A pixel at the boundary of a cell receives a flow vector pointing toward that cell's centre. After prediction, simulated particles are placed at each pixel and follow the flow field вҖ” particles belonging to the same cell converge to the same centre. Clustering these trajectories gives instance segmentation. This representation is much more robust to shape variability than explicit mask prediction.

sections.heading: StarDist: Star-Convex Polygon Representation
sections.text: StarDist (Schmidt et al. 2018) represents each cell as a star-convex polygon вҖ” a shape where every point on the boundary is visible from the centre. The network predicts, for each pixel: (1) the probability that it is a cell centre, and (2) $n$ radial distances $r_1, \ldots, r_n$ in $n$ equally-spaced directions. These $n$ radial distances define the polygon boundary from the predicted centre. Non-maximum suppression selects the best polygon proposals. Star-convex polygons work extremely well for roundish or elliptical cells (most common in 2D histology and microscopy) but fail for highly elongated or branching shapes where star-convexity doesn't hold.

ida06.title: Lecture 6 вҖ” Q & A
ida06.summary: IDA Lecture 6'nДұn tГјm sДұnav sorularДұ ve sade aГ§Дұklamalarla cevaplarДұ. Nesne takibi, nearest neighbor, supervoxel+GMM, Viterbi, GOTURN, optical flow, FlowNet.

sections.heading: S1. Nesne takibinin genel amacДұ nedir? 'Tracking by detection' ile 'tracking by contour evolution' farkДұ?
sections.text:
Genel amaГ§: video ya da 3D+t veri iГ§inde nesneleri zaman boyunca takip et.
Her zaman noktasДұnda nesnenin kimliДҹini koru, trajektori Г§Дұkar.

Mikroskopi verisindeki zorluklar:
  ГҮok sayДұda birbirine benzeyen nesne (hГјcreler).
  BГ¶lГјnme ve Г¶lГјm olaylarДұ.
  GГјrГјltГј, dГјЕҹГјk kontrast.
  3D+t verisi вҶ’ bГјyГјk veri boyutu.

Tracking by detection:
  Her zaman noktasДұnda nesneleri tespit et.
  Tespitleri zaman boyunca birbirine baДҹla (assignment problemi).
  Tespit ve takip ayrДұ ayrДұ yapДұlДұyor.

Tracking by contour evolution:
  Aktif kontur (snake) ile nesne sДұnДұrДұnДұ doДҹrudan takip et.
  SДұnДұr bir sonraki frame'e taЕҹДұnДұp gГјncelleniyor.
  Tespit ve takip birleЕҹik.

sections.heading: S2. Nearest neighbor tracking nasДұl Г§alДұЕҹДұyor?
sections.text:
Her zaman noktasДұnda tespitler var (bounding box merkezi ya da segmentasyon merkezi).

Algoritma:
  t anДұndaki her nesne iГ§in t+1 anДұndaki en yakДұn tespiti bul.
  Mesafe genellikle Г–klid mesafesi.
  EЕҹleЕҹtirme greedy вҖ” en yakДұna git.

Sorunlar:
  Nesneler Г§aprazlaЕҹДұrsa yanlДұЕҹ eЕҹleЕҹtirme.
  Yeni nesneler / kaybolan nesneler iГ§in Г¶zel mantДұk gerekiyor.
  Sadece bir frame geriye bakДұyor вҖ” kГјresel optimum deДҹil.

Tespitler nereden geliyor?
  Г–nceki lecture'larda Г¶ДҹrendiДҹimiz yГ¶ntemler:
  threshold + connected components, U-Net, StarDist, Cellpose...

sections.heading: S3. Supervoxel + Gaussian Mixture Model ile tracking nasДұl yapДұlДұyor?
sections.text:
AdДұmlar:

1. Supervoxel oluЕҹtur:
   Birbirine benzer ve yakДұn pikselleri grupla вҶ’ supervoxel.
   Her nesne birkaГ§ supervoxel'den oluЕҹuyor.

2. GMM ile nesne modeli:
   Her nesnenin konumu ve Еҹekli Gaussian ile modellenir.
   Gaussian'Дұn merkezi = nesne konumu.

3. Zaman boyunca gГјncelleme:
   t anДұndaki Gaussian'Дұ t+1'e tahmin et (hareket modeli).
   Yeni supervoxel'leri mevcut Gaussian'lara ata.
   EM algoritmasДұ ile Gaussian parametrelerini gГјncelle.

Avantaj: bГ¶lГјnme olaylarДұnДұ modelleyebilir
(bir Gaussian iki Gaussian'a ayrДұlabilir).

sections.heading: S4. Tracking algoritmalarДұna hangi kДұsДұt ve heuristikler eklenebilir?
sections.text:
Biyolojik olarak makul sonuГ§lar iГ§in:

  Maksimum hareket hДұzДұ:
    Bir hГјcre bir frame'de Г§ok fazla hareket edemez.
    Belirli bir mesafenin Гјzerindeki eЕҹleЕҹtirmeleri reddet.

  BГ¶lГјnme kДұsДұtДұ:
    Bir hГјcre en fazla 2'ye bГ¶lГјnebilir.
    BГ¶lГјnme sonrasДұ her iki yavru hГјcreyi de takip et.

  Г–lГјm/doДҹum yГ¶netimi:
    GГ¶rГјntГј sДұnДұrДұndan Г§Дұkan nesne = kayДұp (normal).
    Ortada kaybolan = Г¶lГјm (Г¶zel olay olarak iЕҹaretle).

  Hacim/boyut sГјrekliliДҹi:
    HГјcre boyutu ani deДҹiЕҹmez.
    ГҮok farklДұ boyuttaki eЕҹleЕҹtirmeleri reddet.

Bu kДұsДұtlar assignment problemine maliyet matrisi olarak eklenir.

sections.heading: S5. Global track linking ve Viterbi algoritmasДұ nedir? State-space diyagramДұ nasДұl?
sections.text:
Nearest neighbor sadece bir sonraki frame'e bakДұyor.
Global track linking tГјm zaman boyutuna bakДұp en iyi trajektori seti buluyor.

State-space diyagramДұ:
  DГјДҹГјmler: her zaman noktasДұndaki tespitler + baЕҹlangДұГ§/bitiЕҹ dГјДҹГјmleri.
  Kenarlar: olasДұ eЕҹleЕҹtirmeler arasДұ geГ§iЕҹler.
  Her kenarДұn maliyeti var (mesafe, boyut farkДұ vs.).

Viterbi algoritmasДұ:
  Bu diyagramda en dГјЕҹГјk maliyetli yolu bul.
  Dynamic programming ile verimli Г§Г¶zГјm.
  TГјm zaman noktalarДұnДұ bir arada optimize ediyor.
  Lokal optimum yerine global optimum.

DГјДҹГјmler = o zaman noktasДұndaki tespit.
Kenarlar = 'bu nesne bir sonraki frame'de Еҹu nesne olabilir' iliЕҹkisi.

sections.heading: S6. GOTURN nasДұl Г§alДұЕҹДұyor? Laplace daДҹДұlДұmДұ neden kullanДұlДұyor?
sections.text:
GOTURN: CNN tabanlДұ single-object tracker.

Girdi: Г¶nceki frame'deki nesnenin crop'u + mevcut frame'in geniЕҹ alanДұ.
CNN bu iki girdiyi birlikte iЕҹleyip nesnenin yeni konumunu tahmin ediyor.
Iki ayrДұ CNN kolu (Siamese benzeri), FC'de birleЕҹiyor.

Laplace daДҹДұlДұmДұ neden?
  EДҹitim verisi Гјretmek iГ§in nesneler rastgele konuma yerleЕҹtiriliyor.
  Laplace daДҹДұlДұmДұndan hareket Г¶rnekleniyor.
  Laplace вҶ’ kalДұn kuyruklar вҶ’ hem kГјГ§Гјk hem bГјyГјk hareketler.
  Normal daДҹДұlДұm kГјГ§Гјk hareketlere yГ¶nelirdi.
  Laplace ile aДҹ daha Г§eЕҹitli hareket bГјyГјklГјklerine hazДұrlanДұyor.

Potansiyel kДұsДұt:
  Tek nesne takip ediyor.
  HДұzlДұ kamera hareketi veya tamamen yeni gГ¶rГјnГјmlerde bozulabilir.

sections.heading: S7. Optical flow nedir? Д°ki frame verilince ne tahmin edilir?
sections.text:
Optical flow: iki ardДұЕҹДұk frame arasДұnda her pikselin
nerede olduДҹunu gГ¶steren 2D hareket vektГ¶rГј alanДұ.

Her piksel (x, y) iГ§in:
  Bu piksel bir sonraki frame'de (x + u, y + v) konumuna gitti.
  u ve v вҶ’ yatay ve dikey hareket miktarДұ.
  TГјm gГ¶rГјntГј iГ§in bu vektГ¶rler вҶ’ dense optical flow field.

Ne iЕҹe yarДұyor?
  Nesne hareketi tahmini.
  Video stabilizasyonu.
  Action recognition.
  Tracking iГ§in Г¶zellik olarak kullanДұlabilir.

Zor durumlar:
  BГјyГјk hareketler, aydДұnlatma deДҹiЕҹimi,
  transparan yГјzeyler, motion blur.

sections.heading: S8. FlowNet nasДұl Г§alДұЕҹДұyor? Correlation layer nedir? FlowNet2 ne getiriyor?
sections.text:
FlowNet: CNN ile dense optical flow tahmini.

Д°ki frame'i birleЕҹtirip encoder-decoder CNN'den geГ§iriyorsun.
Decoder Г§ДұktДұsДұ: her piksel iГ§in (u, v) hareket vektГ¶rГј.

Correlation layer:
  Д°ki feature map arasДұndaki benzerliДҹi hesaplar.
  'Frame 1'deki bir patch, Frame 2'nin hangi bГ¶lgesine benziyor?'
  Her konum iГ§in kГјГ§Гјk bir arama penceresi iГ§inde karЕҹДұlaЕҹtДұrma yapДұlДұr.
  AДҹДұn iki frame'i iliЕҹkilendirmesine yardДұmcДұ olur.
  CNN'in hangi bГ¶lgelerin eЕҹleЕҹtiДҹini daha kolay Г¶Дҹrenmesini saДҹlar.

FlowNet2 iyileЕҹtirmeleri:
  Birden fazla FlowNet modГјlГјnГј seri baДҹladДұ.
  Г–nce kaba tahmin, sonra ince dГјzeltme.
  KГјГ§Гјk ve bГјyГјk hareketler iГ§in ayrДұ dal.
  ГҮok daha iyi doДҹruluk.

EДҹitim verisi nasДұl elde edilir?
  GerГ§ek video + bilinen hareket вҶ’ sentetik data.
  3D render edilmiЕҹ sahneler (FlyingChairs, FlyingThings3D).

ida06.title: Kalman Filter for Object Tracking
ida06.summary: The Kalman filter is the optimal linear estimator for tracking objects under Gaussian noise. It alternates between a predict step (propagate state estimate forward using a motion model) and an update step (correct with new observations). Essential for multi-object tracking (MOT).

sections.heading: Kalman Filter: Predict and Update
sections.text:
State vector $\mathbf{x}_t = [x, y, v_x, v_y]^\top$ (position + velocity).

**Predict step** (time update):
$$\hat{\mathbf{x}}_{t|t-1} = F\, \mathbf{x}_{t-1|t-1}$$
$$P_{t|t-1} = F\, P_{t-1|t-1}\, F^\top + Q$$

**Update step** (measurement update) given observation $\mathbf{z}_t$:
$$K_t = P_{t|t-1} H^\top (H P_{t|t-1} H^\top + R)^{-1} \quad \text{(Kalman gain)}$$
$$\mathbf{x}_{t|t} = \hat{\mathbf{x}}_{t|t-1} + K_t(\mathbf{z}_t - H\hat{\mathbf{x}}_{t|t-1})$$
$$P_{t|t} = (I - K_t H)\, P_{t|t-1}$$

$F$: state transition, $H$: observation model, $Q$: process noise, $R$: measurement noise.

sections.heading: Intuition: Predict then Correct
sections.text: Prediction: using the motion model (e.g. constant velocity), predict where the object will be at time $t$ without any new measurement. This gives a Gaussian estimate with growing uncertainty ($P$ increases by $Q$ at each step). Update: when a detection arrives, the Kalman gain $K_t$ decides how much to trust the prediction vs the measurement. If measurement noise $R$ is small (accurate detector), $K_t \approx 1$ and the estimate snaps to the measurement. If $R$ is large (noisy detector), $K_t \approx 0$ and the prediction dominates. The Kalman filter is optimal (minimum variance unbiased estimator) under the Gaussian linear assumptions.

left.title: Kalman Filter
points: Optimal for linear systems + Gaussian noise

right.title: Particle Filter
points: Represents distribution as N weighted particles
Expensive: O(N) per step, N ~1000вҖ“10000 particles

ida06.title: Hungarian Algorithm and Multi-Object Tracking
ida06.summary: Multi-object tracking (MOT) is a two-step process: detect objects in each frame, then associate detections across frames. The Hungarian algorithm solves the bipartite matching assignment problem optimally вҖ” matching predicted track positions to new detections.
ida06.context: Lecture 06 В· 23.11.2023 вҖ” Multi-object tracking pipeline

sections.heading: The Tracking-by-Detection Paradigm
sections.text: Modern multi-object tracking follows the tracking-by-detection paradigm: (1) run a detector on each frame independently to get a set of bounding boxes; (2) associate detections across frames using the Hungarian algorithm. The association cost between track $i$ and detection $j$ is typically the IoU between the predicted box (from Kalman filter) and the detected box вҖ” lower IoU = higher cost. The Hungarian algorithm finds the globally optimal assignment (minimising total cost) in $O(n^3)$ time. Unmatched tracks are marked as 'lost' (and deleted after $T$ frames); unmatched detections initialise new tracks. This is the core of SORT (Simple Online and Realtime Tracking).

sections.heading: Assignment Cost Matrix
sections.text:
With $M$ tracks and $N$ detections, cost matrix $C \in \mathbb{R}^{M \times N}$:

$$C_{ij} = 1 - \mathrm{IoU}(\hat{b}_i, d_j)$$

where $\hat{b}_i$ is the Kalman-predicted box for track $i$, $d_j$ is detection $j$.

Hungarian algorithm finds assignment $\sigma$ minimising $\sum_i C_{i,\sigma(i)}$.

**DeepSORT** extends SORT by also using appearance features (CNN embedding of the detection crop) as an additional cost:
$$C_{ij} = \lambda \cdot (1-\mathrm{IoU}) + (1-\lambda) \cdot d_{\cos}(f_i, f_j)$$

ida06.title: Optical Flow and Deep Learning Trackers
ida06.summary: Optical flow estimates per-pixel motion between frames. FlowNet introduced end-to-end deep optical flow estimation. GOTURN was among the first deep learning object trackers, framing tracking as regression from a siamese CNN.
ida06.context: Lecture 06 В· 23.11.2023 вҖ” Deep approaches to motion estimation

sections.heading: Optical Flow Constraint Equation
sections.text:
Assuming brightness constancy: pixel intensity does not change between frames:
$$I(x, y, t) = I(x+u, y+v, t+1)$$

Taylor expansion gives the **Optical Flow Constraint**:
$$I_x \cdot u + I_y \cdot v + I_t = 0$$

where $I_x, I_y$ are spatial gradients and $I_t$ is the temporal gradient. This is one equation with two unknowns $(u,v)$ вҖ” the **aperture problem**. Additional constraints needed: smoothness (Horn-Schunck: minimise $|\nabla u|^2 + |\nabla v|^2$) or local neighbourhood (Lucas-Kanade: assume constant flow in a patch).

sections.heading: FlowNet: End-to-End Deep Optical Flow
sections.text: FlowNet (Dosovitskiy et al. 2015) concatenated two consecutive frames as a 6-channel input to a CNN encoder-decoder (with skip connections like U-Net). FlowNetC added a correlation layer: compute the dot product between feature maps at different spatial offsets, producing an explicit cost volume of matching costs for each displacement. The network learns to produce a dense flow field ($u,v$ per pixel). Training required a synthetic dataset (FlyingChairs вҖ” CGI chairs flying over random backgrounds) because ground-truth optical flow is impossible to measure in real scenes. FlowNet2 stacked multiple FlowNets for refinement. RAFT (2020) replaced the architecture with iterative updates on a 4D cost volume, dramatically improving accuracy.

ida07.title: Lecture 7 вҖ” Q & A
ida07.summary: IDA Lecture 7'nin tГјm sДұnav sorularДұ ve sade aГ§Дұklamalarla cevaplarДұ. RNN, attention mekanizmasДұ, Transformer mimarisi, ViT, DETR.
ida07.context: Lecture 07 В· 30.11.2023 вҖ” Attention and Transformers

sections.heading: S1. MLP sequential data iГ§in neden uygun deДҹil? CNN iЕҹe yarДұyor mu?
sections.text:
MLP'nin sorunu:
  Sabit boyutlu girdi bekliyor вҖ” farklДұ uzunlukta diziler iГ§in uygulanamaz.
  Her pozisyon iГ§in ayrДұ aДҹДұrlДұklar olurdu вҖ” parametre patlamasДұ.
  Г–nceki adДұmlarДұn bilgisini tutmuyor, her girdiyi baДҹДұmsДұz iЕҹliyor.

CNN ne kadar iЕҹe yarДұyor?
  1D conv ile kДұsa baДҹДұmlДұlДұklarДұ yakalayabilir.
  BirkaГ§ katman ile biraz daha uzun baДҹДұmlДұlДұklar.
  Ama Г§ok uzun mesafeli iliЕҹkiler iГ§in Г§ok derin aДҹ gerekiyor.
  AyrДұca deДҹiЕҹken uzunluklu dizilerle baЕҹa Г§ДұkmasДұ zor.
  RNN'e gГ¶re daha az doДҹal bir fit.

sections.heading: S2. RNN'in temel fikri nedir? Sequential data iГ§in neden uygun?
sections.text:
Fikir: Г¶nceki adДұmДұn bilgisini bir 'hidden state' olarak tut
ve her adДұmda hem yeni girdi hem de bu state'i kullan.

  h_t = f(W_hh Г— h_{t-1} + W_xh Г— x_t + b)
  y_t = W_hy Г— h_t

Her adДұmda:
  Г–nceki state (h_{t-1}) + mevcut girdi (x_t) вҶ’ yeni state (h_t).
  State geГ§miЕҹ bilgisini taЕҹДұyor.

Neden sequential data iГ§in uygun?
  DeДҹiЕҹken uzunluklu dizileri doДҹal iЕҹliyor.
  AДҹДұrlДұklar tГјm zaman adДұmlarДұnda paylaЕҹДұlДұyor (weight sharing).
  GeГ§miЕҹ baДҹДұmlДұlДұklarДұ teorik olarak sonsuz uzaДҹa taЕҹДұyabilir.

sections.heading: S3. Sequential data iГ§in tipik uygulama alanlarДұ neler? Mimariler nasДұl deДҹiЕҹiyor?
sections.text:
Uygulama alanlarДұ ve karЕҹДұlДұk gelen mimariler:

  ГҮoka-bire (many-to-one):
    Sentiment analysis: tГјm cГјmleyi oku вҶ’ tek etiket.
    Son adДұmДұn hidden state'i Г§ДұktДұ olarak kullanДұlДұr.

  Bire-Г§oka (one-to-many):
    Image captioning: tek gГ¶rГјntГј вҶ’ kelime dizisi.
    Girdi bir kez verilir, RNN metin Гјretir.

  ГҮoka-Г§oka (many-to-many), eЕҹit uzunluk:
    Video classification, her frame iГ§in etiket.

  ГҮoka-Г§oka, farklДұ uzunluk (Seq2Seq):
    Makine Г§evirisi: TГјrkГ§e cГјmle вҶ’ Д°ngilizce cГјmle.
    Encoder tГјm girdiyi okur, decoder Г§ДұktДұyДұ Гјretir.

sections.heading: S4. RNN'deki internal state'in mantДұДҹДұ nedir?
sections.text:
Hidden state = aДҹДұn belleДҹi.

Her adДұmda iki bilgi birleЕҹiyor:
  1. Г–nceki adДұmdan gelen hidden state (geГ§miЕҹ ne sГ¶ylГјyor?)
  2. Mevcut adДұmДұn girdisi (Еҹu an ne gГ¶rГјyorum?)

Bu ikisi aДҹДұrlДұklarla birleЕҹtirilip aktivasyondan geГ§iyor вҶ’ yeni hidden state.

Analooji: bir cГјmleyi okurken zihinde Г¶zet tutmak gibi.
Her kelimeyi okuyunca Г¶zeti gГјncelliyorsun.

Sorun: Г§ok uzun dizilerde ilk adДұmlarДұn bilgisi
giderek 'seyreltiliyor' вҖ” vanishing gradient problemi.
LSTM ve GRU bu sorunu Г¶zel gate mekanizmalarДұyla Г§Г¶zГјyor.

sections.heading: S5. RNN'in aГ§ДұlmДұЕҹ hesaplama grafiДҹi nasДұl gГ¶rГјnГјr? EДҹitim nasДұl yapДұlДұr? Sorunlar neler?
sections.text:
AГ§ДұlmДұЕҹ hesaplama grafiДҹi (unrolled):
  RNN'i zaman boyunca dГјz bir aДҹ gibi Г§iz.
  Her zaman adДұmДұ = ayrДұ bir katman, ama aynДұ aДҹДұrlДұklar.
  h0 вҶ’ h1 вҶ’ h2 вҶ’ ... вҶ’ hT  (her adДұmda x_t de giriyor)

EДҹitim: Backpropagation Through Time (BPTT).
  AГ§ДұlmДұЕҹ grafik Гјzerinde normal backprop.
  Gradyanlar zamanda geriye doДҹru akar.

Sorunlar:
  Vanishing gradient: gradyanlar Г§ok adДұm geriye gidince sДұfДұra yaklaЕҹДұyor.
    вҶ’ Uzak baДҹДұmlДұlДұklar Г¶Дҹrenilemiyor.
  Exploding gradient: gradyanlar Г§ok bГјyГјyor.
    вҶ’ Gradient clipping ile Г¶nleniyor.
  Uzun diziler iГ§in Г§ok yavaЕҹ eДҹitim (sequential вҖ” paralelleЕҹtirilemiyor).

AДҹДұrlДұklar tГјm zaman adДұmlarДұnda paylaЕҹДұldДұДҹДұ iГ§in (weight sharing)
her adДұm iГ§in ayrДұ parametre Г¶Дҹrenmek zorunda deДҹiliz.

sections.heading: S6. RNN'de parametre matrisleri her zaman adДұmДұ iГ§in ayrДұ mДұ Г¶Дҹrenilir?
sections.text:
HayДұr вҖ” tГјm zaman adДұmlarДұnda aynДұ aДҹДұrlДұklar kullanДұlДұr.

W_hh, W_xh, W_hy matrisleri paylaЕҹДұlДұyor.
Her adДұmda aynДұ dГ¶nГјЕҹГјmГј yapДұyorsun, farklДұ girdi ve state ile.

Neden bu iyi?
  Parametre sayДұsДұ diziyi uzunluДҹundan baДҹДұmsДұz sabit kalДұyor.
  FarklДұ uzunlukta dizilere uygulanabilir.
  Her adДұmda aynДұ 'kural' geГ§erli вҖ” tutarlДұ iЕҹleme.

sections.heading: S7. Attention mekanizmasДұ nedir? Key, value, query nedir? Encoder-decoder attention?
sections.text:
RNN'in sorunu: uzun dizilerde bilgi tek bir vektГ¶rde sДұkДұЕҹДұyor.
Attention: decoder her adДұmda tГјm encoder Г§ДұktДұlarДұna bakabilsin.

Key, Query, Value analojisi:
  Query (Q): 'Еһu an ne arДұyorum?' вҖ” decoder'Дұn mevcut durumu.
