  Key (K): 'Bende ne var?' вҖ” encoder Г§ДұktДұlarДұnДұn temsili.
  Value (V): 'AsДұl iГ§erik ne?' вҖ” encoder Г§ДұktДұlarДұnДұn bilgisi.

  Attention weight = softmax(Q Г— K^T / вҲҡd_k)
  ГҮДұktДұ = Attention weight Г— V

Encoder-decoder attention:
  Query decoder'dan geliyor.
  Key ve Value encoder'dan geliyor.
  Decoder her adДұmda hangi encoder Г§ДұktДұsДұna bakmasДұ gerektiДҹini Г¶Дҹreniyor.
  ГҮeviri iГ§in: TГјrkГ§e kelime Гјretirken hangi Д°ngilizce kelimeye bak?

sections.heading: S8. Transformer mimarisi nedir? Multi-head attention neden daha iyi?
sections.text:
Transformer RNN'i tamamen bДұraktДұ вҖ” sadece attention kullanДұyor.

Encoder bloДҹu (N kez tekrar):
  Multi-head self-attention вҶ’ Add & Norm вҶ’ Feed-Forward вҶ’ Add & Norm

Decoder bloДҹu (N kez tekrar):
  Masked self-attention вҶ’ Add & Norm
  вҶ’ Cross-attention (encoder'a bakДұyor) вҶ’ Add & Norm
  вҶ’ Feed-Forward вҶ’ Add & Norm

Fayda:
  TГјm pozisyonlar birbirine direkt baДҹlanДұyor вҖ” uzun baДҹДұmlДұlДұk sorunu yok.
  ParalelleЕҹtirilebilir вҖ” RNN gibi sДұralДұ iЕҹlem yok, GPU'yu tam kullanДұyor.

Multi-head attention neden daha iyi?
  Her head farklДұ bir iliЕҹkiyi Г¶Дҹrenebilir.
  Bir head sГ¶zdizimsel iliЕҹkiyi, diДҹeri anlamsal iliЕҹkiyi yakalayabilir.
  Tek head tГјm iliЕҹkileri aynДұ anda yakalamak zorunda kalДұyor.

sections.heading: S9. Transformer gГ¶rГјntГј sДұnДұflandДұrma ve nesne tespitine nasДұl uygulanДұr?
sections.text:
GГ¶rГјntГјler iГ§in sorun: Transformer dizi bekliyor, gГ¶rГјntГј 2D.

Vision Transformer (ViT) Г§Г¶zГјmГј:
  GГ¶rГјntГјyГј sabit boyutlu patch'lere bГ¶l (Г¶rn. 16Г—16 piksel).
  Her patch'i dГјzleЕҹtir ve linear projection ile embedding'e dГ¶nГјЕҹtГјr.
  Positional encoding ekle вҖ” patch'in konumunu bilsin.
  Sequence olarak Transformer encoder'dan geГ§ir.
  BaЕҹa Г¶zel [CLS] token ekle вҶ’ sДұnДұflandДұrma iГ§in.

Nesne tespiti (DETR):
  CNN ile gГ¶rГјntГјden feature map Г§Дұkar.
  Feature map'i dГјzleЕҹtir + positional encoding вҶ’ Transformer encoder.
  Decoder: object query'ler ile encoder Г§ДұktДұsДұna cross-attention.
  Her query bir nesne tahmin ediyor (class + bounding box).
  FarklДұ sayДұda nesne: 'no object' sДұnДұfДұ ile hallediliyor.

sections.heading: S10. ViT'te final classification nasДұl elde ediliyor?
sections.text:
ViT'te baЕҹa Г¶zel bir [CLS] token ekleniyor.
Bu token patch embedding'leriyle birlikte Transformer'dan geГ§iyor.
Self-attention sayesinde tГјm patch'lerin bilgisini topluyor.

Encoder'Дұn sonunda [CLS] token'ДұnДұn Г§ДұktДұsДұ alДұnДұyor.
Bu vektГ¶r bir MLP classification head'e veriliyor.
Sonunda sДұnДұf olasДұlДұklarДұ Г§ДұkДұyor.

Neden [CLS] token?
  Hangi patch'e bakacaДҹДұnДұ Г¶nceden bilmiyorsun.
  [CLS] token tГјm patch'lerle etkileЕҹime giriyor
  ve 'genel gГ¶rГјntГј Г¶zeti' Г¶Дҹreniyor.

sections.heading: S11. DETR'de hangi adДұmlar var? FarklДұ sayДұda nesne nasДұl yГ¶netiliyor?
sections.text:
DETR adДұmlarДұ:
  1. CNN backbone ile gГ¶rГјntГјden feature map Г§Дұkar.
  2. Feature map'i dГјzleЕҹtir + 2D positional encoding ekle.
  3. Transformer encoder: feature map'teki global baДҹДұmlДұlДұklarДұ iЕҹle.
  4. Transformer decoder: N tane object query (Г¶rn. 100 sabit sorgu).
     Her query cross-attention ile encoder Г§ДұktДұsДұna bakДұyor.
  5. Her query iГ§in prediction head:
     SДұnДұf tahmini + bounding box koordinatlarДұ.

FarklДұ sayДұda nesne:
  N sorgu sabit ama gГ¶rГјntГјde bazen 3, bazen 50 nesne olabilir.
  'No object' Г¶zel sДұnДұfДұ ekleniyor.
  GerГ§ek nesne olmayan sorgularДұn Г§ДұktДұsДұ 'no object' oluyor.
  Hungarian matching ile eДҹitim sДұrasДұnda hangi sorgunun
  hangi gerГ§ek nesneye karЕҹДұlДұk geldiДҹi optimize ediliyor.

ida07.title: From RNNs to Self-Attention in Vision
ida07.summary: RNNs modelled sequential data but struggled with long-range dependencies and couldn't parallelise. Self-attention allows every pixel/patch to directly attend to every other, enabling global context modelling in a single layer. This is the key motivation for applying Transformers to images.
ida07.context: Lecture 07 В· 30.11.2023 вҖ” Attention and Transformers

sections.heading: Why Not Use RNNs for Images?
sections.text: Images processed as sequences (raster scan) impose an artificial 1D ordering on 2D data. RNNs then struggle with both local 2D spatial relationships (nearby pixels in 2D may be far apart in the sequence) and long-range dependencies (gradients vanish over 100s of steps). CNNs solve locality via local receptive fields, but building a global receptive field requires many layers. Self-attention has $O(1)$ path length between any two positions вҖ” every patch can directly access context from any other patch in the image in a single layer, regardless of distance. The cost is $O(n^2)$ in the number of patches, which is why image patches (not individual pixels) are the standard unit of computation.

sections.heading: Self-Attention for Visual Features
sections.text:
Given feature map $X \in \mathbb{R}^{N\times d}$ (N positions, d-dim features):

$$Q = XW_Q,\quad K = XW_K,\quad V = XW_V$$

$$\mathrm{Attention}(Q,K,V) = \mathrm{softmax}\!\left(\frac{QK^\top}{\sqrt{d_k}}\right)V$$

Attention weight $\alpha_{ij}$ = how much position $i$ attends to position $j$.

For images: $N = H/P \times W/P$ patches of size $P\times P$. ViT uses $P=16$, so a $224\times224$ image becomes $14\times14=196$ tokens.

ida07.title: Vision Transformer (ViT)
ida07.summary: ViT (Dosovitskiy et al. 2020) applies the standard NLP Transformer directly to sequences of image patches. With sufficient data and scale, it matches or surpasses CNN performance, showing that the inductive biases of CNNs (locality, equivariance) can be learned rather than hard-coded.
ida07.context: Lecture 07 В· 30.11.2023 вҖ” Vision Transformer architecture

sections.heading: ViT Patch Embedding and Class Token
sections.text:
Image $x \in \mathbb{R}^{H\times W\times C}$ split into $N$ patches of size $P\times P$:
$$x_p \in \mathbb{R}^{N\times(P^2C)},\quad N = HW/P^2$$

Linear projection to $d$-dim embeddings:
$$z_0 = [x_{\mathrm{cls}};\; x_p^1 E;\; x_p^2 E;\; \ldots;\; x_p^N E] + E_{\mathrm{pos}}$$

$x_{\mathrm{cls}}$: learnable classification token. $E_{\mathrm{pos}}$: positional embedding (learnable 1D positions).

Standard Transformer encoder $L$ times вҶ’ $z_L$. Classification: $y = \mathrm{MLP}(z_L^0)$ (class token output).

sections.heading: Data Hunger and Pretraining
sections.text: The fundamental limitation of ViT is data efficiency: CNNs encode translation equivariance and locality as inductive biases, enabling learning from tens of thousands of images. ViT must learn these from scratch, requiring millions of images (JFT-300M, ImageNet-21k). On ImageNet-1k alone, ViT underperforms ResNet. This motivated DeiT (Data-efficient Image Transformers), which uses knowledge distillation from a CNN teacher + heavy augmentation to match ViT/JFT-300M performance with only ImageNet-1k. Later work (MAE вҖ” Masked Autoencoders) showed that masking 75% of patches and predicting the masked content is an extremely effective self-supervised pretraining strategy, making ViT competitive even with limited labeled data.

left.title: CNN
points: Global context only after many layers

right.title: ViT
points: Scales better: performance вҲқ data and params

ida07.title: DETR: Detection with Transformers
ida07.summary: DETR (Carion et al. 2020) reformulates object detection as a set prediction problem. It eliminates hand-crafted anchors and NMS entirely, using learned object queries and bipartite matching to produce exactly N detections per image.

sections.heading: Set Prediction with Bipartite Matching
sections.text: DETR predicts a fixed set of $N=100$ object hypotheses (most will be 'no object'). Training requires matching predictions to ground truth annotations вҖ” but unlike standard detectors with anchors, there's no natural correspondence. DETR uses the Hungarian algorithm to find the optimal bipartite matching $\hat{\sigma}$ minimising the total matching cost: $\hat{\sigma} = \arg\min_{\sigma} \sum_i [\mathcal{L}_{cls}(p_i, c_{\sigma(i)}) + \mathcal{L}_{box}(b_i, b_{\sigma(i)})]$. Each ground-truth object is matched to exactly one prediction. The matched pairs then compute standard cross-entropy (class) and L1+GIoU (box) losses. Unmatched predictions are assigned the 'no object' class.

sections.heading: Object Queries and Cross-Attention
sections.text:
DETR architecture:
1. CNN backbone вҶ’ feature map $\in \mathbb{R}^{H'\times W' \times C}$
2. Flatten + Transformer encoder (self-attention over spatial positions)
3. $N$ learnable **object queries** $q \in \mathbb{R}^{N\times d}$ attend to encoder output via cross-attention
4. Each query output вҶ’ class prediction + bounding box ($x_c, y_c, w, h$)

The object queries specialise during training: some queries learn to detect large objects, others small, others at specific locations. They are not anchors вҖ” they have no pre-defined size or position.

ida07.title: Swin Transformer: Hierarchical Window Attention
ida07.summary: Swin Transformer (Liu et al. 2021) restores the hierarchical multi-scale feature maps of CNNs while using self-attention. It computes attention within local windows (not globally), reducing complexity from O(NВІ) to O(N), and shifts windows between layers for cross-window communication.
ida07.context: Lecture 07 В· 30.11.2023 вҖ” Efficient vision transformers

sections.heading: Window Attention and Shifted Windows
sections.text: Global self-attention on a high-resolution image is computationally prohibitive: a 224Г—224 image with 4Г—4 patches has $56^2 = 3136$ tokens, giving $3136^2 \approx 10M$ attention pairs per head. Swin partitions the feature map into non-overlapping windows of size $M\times M$ (e.g. $M=7$) and computes self-attention within each window independently. Complexity is now $O(M^2 N)$ вҖ” linear in the number of tokens $N$. But windows don't communicate! The 'shifted window' scheme (SW-MSA) shifts the partition by $(M/2, M/2)$ in alternating layers, ensuring that tokens near window boundaries get to attend to tokens across the boundary in the next layer.

sections.heading: Hierarchical Feature Maps for Dense Prediction
sections.text: Unlike ViT (single resolution output), Swin produces multi-scale feature maps: Stage 1 (56Г—56), Stage 2 (28Г—28), Stage 3 (14Г—14), Stage 4 (7Г—7) by patch merging (concatenating 2Г—2 neighbouring patches and applying a linear layer to halve the spatial dimension while doubling channels). This makes Swin a drop-in backbone for FPN-based detection (Swin + FPN + Cascade R-CNN achieves 58.7 box AP on COCO) and U-Net-style segmentation (Swin-UNet), tasks that require multi-scale features. Swin has become the standard backbone for many state-of-the-art image analysis systems.

ida08.title: Lecture 8 вҖ” Q & A
ida08.summary: IDA Lecture 8'in tГјm sДұnav sorularДұ ve sade aГ§Дұklamalarla cevaplarДұ. DeTr, UNETR, Swin Transformer, SAM, masked autoencoder, TrackFormer.
ida08.context: Lecture 08 В· 7.12.2023 вҖ” Image Analysis with Transformers

sections.heading: S1. DeTr gГ¶rГјntГј Г¶zelliklerini nasДұl Г§ДұkarДұyor? Transformer nesne tespitine nasДұl uygulanДұyor?
sections.text:
DeTr (Detection Transformer) iki aЕҹama:

1. CNN backbone (genellikle ResNet):
   GГ¶rГјntГјden feature map Г§ДұkarДұr.
   Transformer tam boyutlu gГ¶rГјntГјyle baЕҹa Г§Дұkamaz вҖ” CNN Г¶n iЕҹlem yapДұyor.

2. Transformer encoder-decoder:
   Feature map dГјzleЕҹtirilip positional encoding eklenerek
   Transformer encoder'a veriliyor.
   Decoder: sabit sayДұda object query вҶ’ encoder Г§ДұktДұsДұna cross-attention.
   Her query bir nesne tahmin ediyor (sДұnДұf + bbox).

End-to-end eДҹitim вҖ” NMS gibi el iЕҹi post-processing yok.
Hungarian matching ile en iyi query-nesne eЕҹleЕҹtirmesi bulunuyor.

sections.heading: S2. CNN tabanlДұ Г¶zellik Г§ДұkarДұmДұ sonrasДұ konumsal bilgi nasДұl korunuyor?
sections.text:
CNN feature map Гјrettikten sonra dГјzleЕҹtirince
hangi Г¶zelliДҹin nerede olduДҹu bilgisi kaybolabilir.

ГҮГ¶zГјm: 2D positional encoding.
  Feature map'teki her konum (satДұr, sГјtun) iГ§in
  bir pozisyon vektГ¶rГј hesaplanДұr.
  Bu vektГ¶r feature embedding'e eklenir.
  Transformer'a verilen her token hem 'ne' hem 'nerede' bilgisi taЕҹДұr.

1D positional encoding (ViT'teki) yeterli olmaz вҖ”
gГ¶rГјntГјde hem x hem y ekseni Г¶nemli.
DeTr'de sinГјs/kosinГјs tabanlДұ 2D encoding kullanДұlДұyor.

sections.heading: S3. Object query nedir? Prediction head ne Гјretiyor? 'No object' sДұnДұfДұ neden gerekli?
sections.text:
Object query:
  Decoder'a verilen N tane Г¶Дҹrenilebilir vektГ¶r (Г¶rn. 100 sorgu).
  Her query 'ben bir nesne arДұyorum' diye encoder Г§ДұktДұsДұna bakДұyor.
  Cross-attention ile gГ¶rГјntГјnГјn ilgili bГ¶lgelerine odaklanДұyor.
  Her query baДҹДұmsДұz bir nesne tahmin etmeye Г§alДұЕҹДұyor.

Prediction head Г§ДұktДұlarДұ (her query iГ§in):
  SДұnДұf daДҹДұlДұmДұ (softmax) + bounding box koordinatlarДұ (cx, cy, w, h).

'No object' sДұnДұfДұ neden gerekli?
  GГ¶rГјntГјde 100 nesne olmayabilir вҖ” 3-4 nesne olabilir.
  Geri kalan query'lerin Г§ДұktДұsДұ 'boЕҹ' olmalДұ.
  'No object' sДұnДұfДұ bu durumu temsil ediyor.
  EДҹitimde Hungarian matching ile hangi query'nin hangi nesneye
  ya da 'no object'e karЕҹДұlДұk geleceДҹi optimize ediliyor.

sections.heading: S4. UNETR 3D gГ¶rГјntГјyГј Transformer'a nasДұl veriyor? Embedding ve positional encoding nasДұl birleЕҹiyor?
sections.text:
3D gГ¶rГјntГј doДҹrudan Transformer'a verilemez вҖ” Г§ok bГјyГјk.

Г–n iЕҹlem:
  3D hacmi eЕҹit boyutlu 3D patch'lere bГ¶l (Г¶rn. 16Г—16Г—16 vokseller).
  Her patch'i dГјzleЕҹtir вҶ’ 1D vektГ¶r.
  Linear projection ile sabit boyutlu embedding'e dГ¶nГјЕҹtГјr.

Positional encoding:
  Her 3D patch pozisyonu iГ§in Г¶Дҹrenilebilir ya da sinГјs tabanlДұ encoding.
  Embedding + positional encoding вҶ’ Transformer encoder'a giriyor.

Transformer kДұsmДұ:
  Encoder tГјm patch'ler arasДұ global iliЕҹkileri Г¶Дҹreniyor.
  ГҮДұktДұ: her patch iГ§in zenginleЕҹtirilmiЕҹ Г¶zellik vektГ¶rГј.
  Bu vektГ¶rler CNN decoder'a skip connection olarak gГ¶nderiliyor.

sections.heading: S5. UNETR encoder Г¶zellikleri orijinal Г§Г¶zГјnГјrlГјДҹe nasДұl Г§ДұkarДұlДұyor? Loss fonksiyonu nasДұl?
sections.text:
Encoder Г§ДұktДұlarДұ dГјЕҹГјk Г§Г¶zГјnГјrlГјklГј patch Г¶zellikler.
Orijinal boyuta dГ¶nmek iГ§in CNN decoder kullanДұlДұyor.

Decoder:
  FarklДұ Transformer katmanlarДұnДұn Г§ДұktДұlarДұnДұ skip connection olarak al.
  Her birini yeniden Еҹekillendir (reshape) вҖ” 1D token вҶ’ 3D feature map.
  Transposed convolution ile boyutu kademeli bГјyГјt.
  U-Net gibi encoder Г§ДұktДұlarДұnДұ decoder'a birleЕҹtir.

Loss fonksiyonu:
  Segmentasyon iГ§in genellikle:
  Cross-entropy loss + Dice loss kombinasyonu.
  Cross-entropy piksel baЕҹДұ sДұnДұf olasДұlДұДҹДұ.
  Dice loss sДұnДұf dengesizliДҹine karЕҹДұ (kГјГ§Гјk nesneler).

sections.heading: S6. Swin Transformer nedir? Neden lineer complexity? Neden Г¶nemli?
sections.text:
Standart ViT sorunu:
  Her patch her diДҹer patch'e attention hesaplДұyor вҶ’ O(nВІ) complexity.
  GГ¶rГјntГј bГјyГјdГјkГ§e hesaplama patlДұyor.

Swin Transformer Г§Г¶zГјmГј:
  Attention'Дұ tГјm gГ¶rГјntГјde deДҹil, kГјГ§Гјk local window'larda hesapla.
  Г–rn. 7Г—7'lik pencereler iГ§inde self-attention.
  Her pencere baДҹДұmsДұz вҶ’ O(n) complexity (pencere sayДұsДұyla lineer).

Neden Г¶nemli?
  YГјksek Г§Г¶zГјnГјrlГјklГј ve 3D medikal gГ¶rГјntГјler iГ§in uygulanabilir.
  GPU belleДҹi ve hesaplama aГ§ДұsДұndan Г§ok daha verimli.
  Swin-UNETR gibi modeller 3D beyin MRI'larДұna uygulanabiliyor.

sections.heading: S7. Patch merging ve SW-MSA nedir? Neden shifted window?
sections.text:
Patch merging:
  Her adДұmda komЕҹu patch'leri birleЕҹtir вҶ’ spatial boyut kГјГ§ГјlГјr, channel artar.
  CNN'deki pooling gibi вҖ” hiyerarЕҹik Г¶zellik Г¶Дҹrenimi.
  Derin katmanlar daha bГјyГјk alana, daha az detaya bakДұyor.

Window-based self-attention (W-MSA):
  GГ¶rГјntГјyГј sabit pencereler bГ¶l, her pencerede kendi iГ§inde attention.
  Sorun: pencere sДұnДұrlarДұ boyunca hiГ§ bilgi aktarДұmДұ yok.

Shifted Window (SW-MSA):
  Bir sonraki katmanda pencereleri yarДұm pencere kaydДұr.
  Г–nceki katmanДұn pencere sДұnДұrlarДұ artДұk pencerelerin ortasДұna geliyor.
  FarklДұ pencereler arasДұnda dolaylДұ bilgi akДұЕҹДұ saДҹlanДұyor.

Verimli uygulama: cyclic shift + masking ile SW-MSA
normal W-MSA kadar hДұzlДұ hesaplanabiliyor.

sections.heading: S8. Swin-UNETR, vanilla UNETR'dan nasДұl farklДұ? SW-MSA 3D'ye nasДұl taЕҹДұnДұyor?
sections.text:
Vanilla UNETR: ViT encoder (global attention, O(nВІ)) + CNN decoder.
Swin-UNETR: Swin Transformer encoder (local window attention, O(n)) + CNN decoder.

Fark:
  Swin-UNETR Г§ok daha bГјyГјk ve yГјksek Г§Г¶zГјnГјrlГјklГј 3D gГ¶rГјntГјleri iЕҹleyebiliyor.
  Vanilla UNETR 3D'de patch sayДұsДұ Г§ok artДұnca bellek yetersiz kalДұyor.
  Swin hierarchical yapДұsДұ sayesinde multi-scale Г¶zellikler Гјretiyor.

SW-MSA 3D'ye taЕҹДұma:
  2D'de x ve y ekseninde kaydДұrma vardДұ.
  3D'de x, y, z eksenlerinde kaydДұrma yapДұlДұyor.
  3D pencereler (Г¶rn. 7Г—7Г—7) iГ§inde 3D self-attention.
  Cyclic shift 3D'de de aynДұ Еҹekilde uygulanДұyor.

sections.heading: S9. SAM (Segment Anything Model) nasДұl Г§alДұЕҹДұyor? ГңГ§ encoder neden ayrДұ?
sections.text:
SAM ГјГ§ bileЕҹen:

1. Image encoder (ViT-H):
   GГ¶rГјntГјyГј bir kez encode eder вҶ’ image embedding.
   AДҹДұr model, ama gГ¶rГјntГј baЕҹДұna bir kez Г§alДұЕҹДұyor.

2. Prompt encoder:
   Nokta, kutu, maske veya metin gibi ipuГ§larДұnДұ encode eder.
   Hafif вҖ” her tДұklamada hДұzlДұ Г§alДұЕҹmasДұ gerekiyor.

3. Mask decoder:
   Image embedding + prompt embedding вҶ’ segmentasyon maskesi.
   ГҮok hafif ve hДұzlДұ.

Neden ayrДұ?
  Image encoder gГ¶rГјntГј baЕҹДұna bir kez вҖ” pahalДұ ama tekrar kullanДұlabilir.
  KullanДұcДұ her tДұkladДұДҹДұnda sadece hafif prompt encoder + decoder Г§alДұЕҹДұyor.
  Д°nteraktif kullanДұm iГ§in gerekli вҖ” her tДұklamada tam ViT Г§alДұЕҹtДұrДұlamaz.

Image encoder pre-training: masked autoencoder ile.

sections.heading: S10. SAM neden birden fazla maske tahmini yapДұyor? Prompt nasДұl gГ¶rГјnГјyor?
sections.text:
Neden birden fazla maske?
  Tek bir nokta belirsiz вҖ” hangi nesneyi kastettiДҹini bilmiyor.
  Г–rn. bir noktaya tДұkladДұnda: hГјcre mi, hГјcre grubu mu, tГјm doku mu?
  SAM 3 farklДұ Г¶lГ§ekte maske Гјretiyor (kГјГ§Гјk/orta/bГјyГјk).
  KullanДұcДұ hangisini istediДҹini seГ§iyor.

Prompt tГјrleri:
  Nokta (pozitif/negatif): 'Bu piksel nesnede / nesnede deДҹil'
  Kutu: bounding box iГ§indeki nesneyi segmentele
  Maske: kaba bir maske hint olarak ver
  (Metin: SAM'Дұn orijinal versiyonunda yok, sonraki versiyonlarda eklendi)

Д°nteraktivite:
  KullanДұcДұ tДұklДұyor вҶ’ prompt encoder Г§alДұЕҹДұyor вҶ’ mask decoder Г§alДұЕҹДұyor.
  Milisaniyeler iГ§inde sonuГ§ вҖ” gerГ§ek zamanlДұ.

sections.heading: S11. Masked autoencoder nedir? SAM'Дұn eДҹitiminde hangi loss kullanДұlДұyor?
sections.text:
Masked Autoencoder (MAE):
  Self-supervised pretraining yГ¶ntemi вҖ” etiket gerekmez.
  GГ¶rГјntГјyГј patch'lere bГ¶l.
  Patch'lerin bГјyГјk bir kДұsmДұnДұ (%75) rastgele maskele (gizle).
  Sadece gГ¶rГјnen patch'lerle Transformer encoder Г§alДұЕҹtДұr.
  Decoder gizlenen patch'leri yeniden oluЕҹturmaya Г§alДұЕҹДұr.
  Loss: maskelenen bГ¶lgelerdeki piksel deДҹerleri iГ§in MSE.

  Bu Еҹekilde aДҹ gГ¶rГјntГј yapДұsДұnДұ ve iГ§eriДҹini anlamak zorunda kalДұyor.

SAM'Дұn image encoder'Дұ MAE ile pretrain ediliyor.
Sonra segmentasyon iГ§in fine-tune.

SAM'Дұn loss fonksiyonu:
  Focal loss + Dice loss kombinasyonu.
  (Binary segmentation iГ§in standart iyi kombinasyon.)

sections.heading: S12. TrackFormer nesne takibine Transformer'Дұ nasДұl uyguluyor? Temporal baДҹlantДұ nasДұl?
sections.text:
TrackFormer: DETR'yi video takibine geniЕҹletiyor.

Fikir:
  Bir Г¶nceki frame'deki tespit edilen nesneler
  bir sonraki frame'e 'track query' olarak taЕҹДұnДұyor.

NasДұl Г§alДұЕҹДұyor?
  Frame t'de: normal DETR object query'leri + bir Г¶nceki frame'in query'leri.
  Track query'ler bir Г¶nceki nesnenin bilgisini taЕҹДұyor.
  Cross-attention ile yeni frame'deki konumlarДұnДұ arДұyorlar.
  ГҮДұktДұ: her track query iГ§in gГјncellenen konum + sДұnДұf.

Temporal baДҹlantДұ nasДұl kuruldu?
  Г–nceki frame'in decoder Г§ДұktДұsДұ (query vektГ¶rГј)
  doДҹrudan bir sonraki frame'in decoder girdisi olarak kullanДұlДұyor.
  Query vektГ¶rГј nesnenin 'hafДұzasДұ' gГ¶revi gГ¶rГјyor.

Yeni nesneler: yeni object query'ler ile tespit edilip track listesine ekleniyor.
Kaybolan nesneler: birkaГ§ frame tespit edilmezse listeden Г§ДұkarДұlДұyor.

ida08.title: UNETR: Transformers for Medical Image Segmentation
ida08.summary: UNETR (Hatamizadeh et al. 2022) uses a ViT encoder to extract global context from volumetric medical images, connected to a CNN decoder via skip connections in a U-shaped architecture. It bridges the global representation strength of Transformers with the spatial precision of U-Net.
ida08.context: Lecture 08 В· 07.12.2023 вҖ” Image Analysis with Transformers

sections.heading: Architecture: ViT Encoder + CNN Decoder
sections.text: UNETR splits a 3D medical volume (CT or MRI) into 3D patches (16Г—16Г—16) and processes them with a standard ViT encoder. The key insight: intermediate representations from different Transformer layers are extracted and connected as skip connections to the CNN decoder. Early ViT layers capture local texture; later layers capture global anatomy. The decoder uses 3D transposed convolutions to upsample and progressively refine the segmentation. This U-Net-like design means UNETR benefits from both the global context modelling of Transformers and the precise spatial localisation of CNNs. UNETR achieves state-of-the-art on the Medical Segmentation Decathlon benchmark.

sections.heading: U-Net vs UNETR for Medical Segmentation

right.title: UNETR
points: ViT encoder: global attention from layer 1
Better for tasks needing global reasoning

ida08.title: SAM: Segment Anything Model
ida08.summary: SAM (Kirillov et al. 2023) is a foundation model for image segmentation. Trained on 1 billion masks, it accepts flexible prompts (points, boxes, text, free-form) and produces high-quality masks for any object in any image вҖ” without task-specific fine-tuning.
ida08.context: Lecture 08 В· 07.12.2023 вҖ” Foundation models for segmentation

sections.heading: SAM Architecture: Image Encoder + Prompt Encoder + Mask Decoder
sections.text: SAM has three components. (1) Image encoder: a MAE-pretrained ViT-H processes the image once and produces dense image embeddings. This is the expensive part (done offline or cached). (2) Prompt encoder: handles different prompt types вҖ” sparse prompts (points, boxes) use positional encoding + learned tokens; dense prompts (masks) use convolutions. (3) Mask decoder: a lightweight Transformer decoder that attends from prompt tokens to image embeddings, predicting 3 mask candidates (and their quality scores) in real-time (<50ms). The three-mask output handles ambiguity: if a point clicks on a dog, the model offers masks for the dog's leg, body, and entire dog.

sections.heading: The SA-1B Dataset and Promptable Segmentation
sections.text: SAM was trained on SA-1B: 11 million images, 1 billion masks collected semi-automatically (human annotators prompted SAM iteratively). The scale of training data is unprecedented for segmentation. SAM enables zero-shot transfer: without any fine-tuning, it segments objects in medical images, aerial imagery, microscopy, and artistic paintings вҖ” domains completely absent from training. The promptable interface is key: rather than training separate models for each task, a single model with a flexible prompt interface can solve any segmentation task interactively. SAM2 extends this to video with a streaming memory mechanism.

sections.heading: SAM Prompt Types and Use Cases
items: Point prompt: click on foreground (positive) or background (negative) points
Box prompt: draw a bounding box around the region of interest
Mask prompt: provide a coarse mask to refine
Automatic mode: grid of points вҶ’ generates all masks вҶ’ filtered by quality score
Application: interactive annotation tools for training data collection
Limitation: struggles with very thin structures and high ambiguity scenarios

ida08.title: Self-Supervised Pretraining: MAE and DINO
ida08.summary: Masked Autoencoders (MAE) and DINO showed that ViTs can be pretrained self-supervisedly on unlabeled images, learning rich visual representations without any labels вҖ” enabling transfer to downstream tasks with minimal fine-tuning.
ida08.context: Lecture 08 В· 07.12.2023 вҖ” Self-supervised visual representation learning

sections.heading: MAE: Masked Autoencoder for Vision
sections.text: MAE (He et al. 2022) randomly masks 75% of image patches and trains a ViT encoder-decoder to reconstruct the masked patches in pixel space. The key: only the visible (unmasked) tokens are fed to the encoder вҖ” making it highly efficient. The decoder (lightweight, 8 layers vs encoder's 24) reconstructs missing patches from the encoder's output + learnable mask tokens. The high masking ratio forces the model to learn holistic image understanding rather than simple interpolation from adjacent patches. MAE pretraining on ImageNet-1k produces features that transfer excellently to classification (linear probe), detection, and segmentation вҖ” matching or beating supervised baselines.

sections.heading: DINO: Self-Distillation with No Labels
sections.text: DINO (Caron et al. 2021) uses a student-teacher self-distillation framework: two ViTs (student and teacher), the teacher being an exponential moving average (EMA) of the student. Both receive different augmented views of the same image; the student is trained to match the teacher's class token output (via cross-entropy with a sharpened softmax). DINO reveals remarkable emergent properties: the attention maps in the final layer spontaneously segment the main object from the background without any segmentation supervision. DINO features work well for nearest-neighbor classification without any fine-tuning, demonstrating genuinely semantic representations.

ida09.title: Lecture 9 вҖ” Q & A
ida09.summary: IDA Lecture 9'un tГјm sДұnav sorularДұ ve sade aГ§Дұklamalarla cevaplarДұ. Point cloud temsili, outlier removal, curvature, PointNet, PointNet++.
ida09.context: Lecture 09 В· 14.12.2023 вҖ” Detection and Segmentation in 3D Point Clouds

sections.heading: S1. Point cloud nedir? Hangi sensГ¶rler Гјretiyor? Д°Еҹlemenin zorluklarДұ neler?
sections.text:
Point cloud: 3D uzayda bir yГјzeyden Г¶rneklenmiЕҹ noktalar kГјmesi.
Her nokta: (x, y, z) koordinatДұ, bazen renk veya yoДҹunluk da var.
SДұrasДұz вҖ” noktalar arasДұnda baДҹlantДұ/grid yapДұsДұ yok.

Hangi sensГ¶rler Гјretiyor?
  LiDAR: lazer ДұЕҹДұnДұ gГ¶nder, geri dГ¶nГјЕҹ sГјresinden mesafe hesapla.
  Depth camera (RGB-D): yapДұlandДұrДұlmДұЕҹ ДұЕҹДұk veya ToF.
  Stereo kamera: iki gГ¶rГјntГјden derinlik tahmini.
  CT/MRI: medikal 3D gГ¶rГјntГјleme.

Д°Еҹlemenin zorluklarДұ:
  SДұrasДұz вҖ” CNN gibi grid tabanlДұ yГ¶ntemler doДҹrudan uygulanamaz.
  DeДҹiЕҹken yoДҹunluk вҖ” bazДұ bГ¶lgeler sДұk, bazДұlarДұ seyrek noktalar.
  BГјyГјk veri boyutu вҖ” milyonlarca nokta olabilir.
  GГјrГјltГј ve outlier'lar вҖ” sensГ¶r hatalarДұndan kaynaklДұ.

sections.heading: S2. Outlier removal nasДұl yapДұlДұr? Normal vektГ¶rler nasДұl hesaplanДұr?
sections.text:
Outlier removal:
  Statistical outlier removal:
    Her nokta iГ§in k en yakДұn komЕҹuya mesafeyi hesapla.
    Ortalama mesafesi genel ortalamanДұn Г§ok Гјzerinde olanlar вҶ’ outlier.
    Silinir.

  Radius outlier removal:
    Belirli yarДұГ§ap iГ§inde komЕҹu sayДұsДұ az olan noktalar вҶ’ outlier.

Normal vektГ¶r hesaplama:
  Her nokta iГ§in k en yakДұn komЕҹuyu bul.
  Bu komЕҹu noktalara PCA (Principal Component Analysis) uygula.
  En kГјГ§Гјk eigenvalue'ya karЕҹДұlДұk gelen eigenvector вҶ’ yГјzey normali.
  Fiziksel olarak: o bГ¶lgedeki yГјzeyin hangi yГ¶ne baktДұДҹДұnДұ gГ¶steriyor.

  Sorun: normal yГ¶nГј belirsiz (iГ§e mi dДұЕҹa mДұ bakДұyor?).
  ГҮГ¶zГјm: tutarlДұlДұk iГ§in yayДұlДұm algoritmasДұ veya gГ¶rГјЕҹ aГ§ДұsДұna gГ¶re dГјzeltme.

sections.heading: S3. Curvature nerede yГјksek? NasДұl hesaplanДұr? Region growing segmentation nedir?
sections.text:
Curvature (eДҹrilik) nerede yГјksek?
  Keskin kenarlar, kГ¶Еҹeler, sivri uГ§lar.
  DГјz yГјzeylerde curvature вүҲ 0.
  Kavisli yГјzeylerde orta, kГ¶Еҹelerde Г§ok yГјksek.

Curvature hesaplama:
  PCA'dan gelen eigenvalue'lardan:
  curvature = О»_min / (О»_1 + О»_2 + О»_3)
  KГјГ§Гјk eigenvalue ne kadar dominant вҶ’ o kadar yГјksek curvature.

Kenar tespiti:
  YГјksek curvature bГ¶lgeleri = kenarlara iЕҹaret eder.

Region growing segmentation:
  Bir tohum nokta seГ§ (dГјЕҹГјk curvature, dГјz alan).
  KomЕҹu noktalarДұ ekle вҖ” eДҹer normal vektГ¶rleri yeterince benzer ise.
  Benzer yГјzey parГ§alarДұ tek segment olarak bГјyГјyor.
  FarklДұ yГјzeylere geГ§iЕҹte (yГјksek curvature) bГјyГјme durur.
  SonuГ§: dГјz/dГјzgГјn yГјzey parГ§alarДұ ayrДұ segment olarak gruplanДұr.

sections.heading: S4. PointNet'in temel fikirleri neler? Permutation invariance nasДұl saДҹlanДұyor?
sections.text:
PointNet'in ГјГ§ temel sorunu Г§Г¶zmesi gerekiyor:
  1. SДұrasДұz girdi вҖ” hangi sДұrada verilirse verilsin aynДұ sonuГ§.
  2. Noktalar arasДұ etkileЕҹim вҖ” global yapДұyДұ anlama.
  3. DГ¶nГјЕҹГјmlere dayanДұklДұlДұk вҖ” rotation, scale.

Mimari:
  Her noktayДұ baДҹДұmsДұz olarak MLP ile iЕҹle вҶ’ nokta baЕҹДұ Г¶zellik.
  (TГјm noktalar aynДұ aДҹДұrlДұklarДұ paylaЕҹДұyor вҖ” weight sharing)
  TГјm nokta Г¶zelliklerini global max pooling ile birleЕҹtir вҶ’ global Г¶zellik.
  Global Г¶zellik + her nokta Г¶zelliДҹi вҶ’ sДұnДұflandДұrma/segmentasyon.

Permutation invariance:
  Max pooling simetrik bir fonksiyon.
  NoktalarДұ hangi sДұrayla verirsen ver, max aynДұ Г§Дұkar.
  Bu sayede sДұrasДұz kГјme giriЕҹi doДҹal olarak destekleniyor.

sections.heading: S5. PointNet lineer dГ¶nГјЕҹГјmlere nasДұl dayanДұklДұ hale geliyor? Classification ve segmentation nasДұl?
sections.text:
T-Net (Transformer Network):
  Girdiden kГјГ§Гјk bir aДҹ 3Г—3 dГ¶nГјЕҹГјm matrisi tahmin ediyor.
  Bu matris girdi noktalarДұna uygulanДұyor вҶ’ normalize edilmiЕҹ koordinatlar.
  Bir de feature uzayДұnda 64Г—64 dГ¶nГјЕҹГјm matrisi var.
  BГ¶ylece aДҹ rotasyon/yansДұma gibi dГ¶nГјЕҹГјmlere dayanДұklДұ hale geliyor.

Classification:
  Global Г¶zellik vektГ¶rГј вҶ’ MLP вҶ’ sДұnДұf olasДұlДұklarДұ.
  TГјm point cloud iГ§in tek Г§ДұktДұ.

Segmentation:
  Global Г¶zellik her nokta Г¶zelliДҹiyle birleЕҹtirilir (concatenate).
  Her nokta iГ§in: kendi Г¶zelliДҹi + global baДҹlam.
  MLP вҶ’ her nokta iГ§in sДұnДұf etiketi.

sections.heading: S6. PointNet++'Дұn hiyerarЕҹik yaklaЕҹДұmДұnДұn mantДұДҹДұ nedir? Tek seviyede neler yapДұlДұyor?
sections.text:
PointNet sorunu: tГјm noktalarДұ tek seferde global max pooling.
Lokal yapДұyДұ Г¶Дҹrenemiyor вҖ” komЕҹu noktalar arasДұndaki iliЕҹki kaybolabilir.

PointNet++ Г§Г¶zГјmГј: hiyerarЕҹik iЕҹleme.

Tek bir hiyerarЕҹi seviyesinde yapДұlanlar:

  1. Sampling:
     Farthest Point Sampling (FPS) ile merkez noktalar seГ§.
     Birbirinden uzak noktalarДұ seГ§ вҶ’ iyi kapsam.

  2. Grouping:
     Her merkez etrafДұnda belirli yarДұГ§apta komЕҹularДұ grupla.
     Her grup = kГјГ§Гјk bir local point cloud.

  3. PointNet uygula:
     Her gruba mini PointNet uygula вҶ’ o merkez iГ§in local Г¶zellik.

SonuГ§: daha az ama daha bilgi yoДҹun nokta kГјmesi.
Bu iЕҹlemi birkaГ§ kez tekrarla вҶ’ giderek daha global Г¶zellikler.

Classification: en derin seviyedeki global Г¶zellikten.
Segmentation: her seviyeden Г¶zelliДҹi birleЕҹtir (interpolation ile boyutu geri bГјyГјt).

ida09.title: Point Cloud Representation and Preprocessing
ida09.summary: A point cloud is an unordered set of 3D points sampled from a surface. Unlike voxels (regular 3D grid) or meshes (connectivity structure), point clouds are sparse and orderless. Preprocessing вҖ” normal estimation, outlier removal, downsampling вҖ” is critical before any learning.
ida09.context: Lecture 09 В· 14.12.2023 вҖ” 3D Point Cloud Analysis

sections.heading: Point Cloud Definition
sections.text:
Point cloud: $\mathcal{P} = \{\mathbf{p}_1, \mathbf{p}_2, \ldots, \mathbf{p}_N\}$ where $\mathbf{p}_i \in \mathbb{R}^D$.

Typically $D=3$ (XYZ coordinates); may include additional features: RGB colour, intensity, normals.

**Key properties**:
- Unordered: no canonical ordering of points
- Irregular: non-uniform density (near objects вҶ’ dense, far вҶ’ sparse)
- Variable size: $N$ varies per scene or object

Neighbourhood queries:
- $k$-NN: $k$ nearest neighbours by Euclidean distance
- Radius search: all points within radius $r$: $\mathcal{N}(\mathbf{p}) = \{\mathbf{q} : \|\mathbf{p}-\mathbf{q}\|_2 < r\}$

sections.heading: Surface Normal Estimation
sections.text: Surface normals are essential for shape descriptors, rendering, and many downstream tasks. Estimation via PCA on local neighbourhoods: for point $\mathbf{p}$ with $k$ nearest neighbours $\{\mathbf{q}_1,\ldots,\mathbf{q}_k\}$, compute the covariance matrix $C = \frac{1}{k}\sum_i (\mathbf{q}_i - \bar{\mathbf{q}})(\mathbf{q}_i - \bar{\mathbf{q}})^\top$ and take the eigenvector corresponding to the smallest eigenvalue as the normal direction. Normal orientation (inward/outward) is ambiguous and requires a reference (e.g. consistently orient toward the sensor). Normal quality depends on neighbourhood size: too small вҶ’ noisy; too large вҶ’ over-smooth, fails at sharp edges.

sections.heading: Standard Preprocessing Steps
items: Voxel downsampling: replace all points in each voxel with their centroid вҖ” reduces density to uniform
Statistical outlier removal: remove points whose mean distance to k-NN is more than NВ·std above the mean
Radius outlier removal: remove points with fewer than M neighbours within radius r
Normal estimation via PCA on k-NN neighbourhoods
Point cloud registration (ICP): align two overlapping point clouds into a common coordinate frame
Normalisation: translate centroid to origin, scale to unit sphere for network inputs

ida09.title: PointNet: Permutation-Invariant Learning
ida09.summary: PointNet (Qi et al. 2017) was the first deep learning architecture to directly consume unordered point clouds. It achieves permutation invariance via a symmetric function (global max pooling) and handles rigid transformations via a learnable T-Net alignment module.

sections.heading: PointNet: Symmetric Function Formulation
sections.text:
A function $f$ on a point set is permutation invariant if:
$$f(\mathbf{p}_1, \ldots, \mathbf{p}_N) \equiv f(\mathbf{p}_{\pi_1}, \ldots, \mathbf{p}_{\pi_N})$$
for any permutation $\pi$.

PointNet approximates this using a symmetric function (max pooling):
$$f(\{\mathbf{p}_1,\ldots,\mathbf{p}_N\}) = \gamma \circ g\bigl(h(\mathbf{p}_1), \ldots, h(\mathbf{p}_N)\bigr)$$

$h$: shared MLP applied independently to each point (maps $\mathbb{R}^3 \to \mathbb{R}^{1024}$)
$g$: element-wise max pooling over all points вҶ’ global feature $\in \mathbb{R}^{1024}$
$\gamma$: classification MLP

sections.heading: T-Net: Learnable Alignment Module
sections.text: PointNet applies a mini-network (T-Net) to predict a transformation matrix that aligns the input point cloud to a canonical orientation before processing. The T-Net produces a $3\times3$ transformation matrix applied to the raw coordinates. A second T-Net predicts a $64\times64$ feature-space transformation. The key constraint: a regularisation term $\|TT^\top - I\|^2$ is added to the loss to keep the feature transformation close to orthogonal, ensuring the transformation doesn't distort the feature space. This makes PointNet robust to rotations and translations вҖ” two common sources of variation in 3D data.

sections.heading: PointNet for Segmentation
sections.text: For per-point segmentation, PointNet concatenates the global feature (from max pooling) with the local point feature (from the first 64-dim shared MLP) for each point. This gives each point access to both global shape context and its own local features. The combined 1088-dim vector (1024 global + 64 local) is processed by another shared MLP to predict per-point class scores. The key insight: global max pooling creates a 'summary' of the entire shape that is rebroadcast to every point, enabling each point to reason about its role within the global structure.

ida09.title: PointNet++: Hierarchical Feature Learning
ida09.summary: PointNet++ (Qi et al. 2017) applies PointNet hierarchically using a Set Abstraction module that sub-samples, groups, and extracts features at multiple scales. This builds local context at fine scales and global context at coarse scales, dramatically improving performance on complex shapes.
ida09.context: Lecture 09 В· 14.12.2023 вҖ” Multi-scale point cloud learning

sections.heading: Set Abstraction Module
sections.text: Each Set Abstraction (SA) level has three components: (1) Sampling: use Farthest Point Sampling (FPS) to select $M$ representative points that cover the shape uniformly. (2) Grouping: for each representative point, find all points within radius $r$ (ball query, up to $K$ points). (3) PointNet: apply PointNet to each local group to extract a feature vector. The output is $M$ points, each with a local feature descriptor. Stacking SA levels progressively reduces the number of points ($N вҶ’ M_1 вҶ’ M_2$) while increasing feature dimensionality вҖ” exactly analogous to strided convolutions in CNNs building a spatial hierarchy.

sections.heading: Multi-Scale Grouping (MSG)
sections.text:
To handle non-uniform density, PointNet++ applies Multiple Scale Grouping:

For each centroid point $c$, apply ball query at radii $r_1 < r_2 < r_3$, extract PointNet features at each scale, then concatenate:
$$f(c) = [\mathrm{PN}(\mathcal{N}_{r_1}(c)),\; \mathrm{PN}(\mathcal{N}_{r_2}(c)),\; \mathrm{PN}(\mathcal{N}_{r_3}(c))]$$

Small radius: fine-grained local geometry. Large radius: coarser neighbourhood, robust to sparse regions. Concatenation provides multi-scale context at each point.

sections.heading: Feature Propagation for Segmentation
sections.text: For per-point segmentation (predicting a label for each of the $N$ original points), PointNet++ uses a Feature Propagation (FP) module to 'upsample' features back to the original resolution. Since sub-sampled points are a strict subset of the original, features are interpolated by inverse-distance weighted k-NN interpolation from the $M$ sub-sampled points to the $N$ original points. Skip connections (analogous to U-Net) pass features from the corresponding SA level directly to the FP module at the same resolution. This mirrors the encoder-decoder pattern of U-Net, achieving precise per-point predictions.

ida10.title: Lecture 10 вҖ” Q & A
ida10.summary: IDA Lecture 10'un tГјm sДұnav sorularДұ ve sade aГ§Дұklamalarla cevaplarДұ. Generative modeller, autoencoder, VAE, GAN, InfoGAN, conditional GAN, CycleGAN.
ida10.context: Lecture 10 В· 21.12.2023 вҖ” Generative Adversarial Networks

sections.heading: S1. Generative modellerin fikri nedir? Neden Г¶nemli olabilirler?
sections.text:
Discriminative modeller veriyi alДұp sana bir etiket ya da sayДұ verir вҖ” yani karar verirler. Generative modeller ise daha temel bir soruyu sorar: "Bu veri nasДұl ГјretilmiЕҹ olabilir?" AmacДұ verinin altДұndaki daДҹДұlДұmДұ Г¶Дҹrenmek, yani p(x)'i modellemek. Bunu baЕҹarДұrsa, o daДҹДұlДұmdan yeni Г¶rnekler Г¶rnekleyebilirsin вҖ” yani yoktan gerГ§ekГ§i gГ¶rГјntГјler, sesler ya da metinler Гјretebilirsin.

Neden bu Г¶nemli? BirkaГ§ farklДұ aГ§Дұdan dГјЕҹГјnebilirsin:

EДҹitim verisi artДұrma aГ§ДұsДұndan: etiketli veri toplamak pahalДұ ve zaman alДұcДұ, ama generative bir modelin ГјrettiДҹi sentetik veriyle bu aГ§ДұДҹДұ kapatabilirsin. Г–zellikle tДұbbi gГ¶rГјntГјlemede Г§ok az Г¶rnek olduДҹunda bu kritik hale geliyor.

Pratik gГ¶rГјntГј iЕҹleme aГ§ДұsДұndan: bir gГ¶rГјntГјdeki eksik alanДұ tamamlamak (inpainting), gece Г§ekimini gГјndГјz fotoДҹrafДұna dГ¶nГјЕҹtГјrmek ya da Г§izimden gerГ§ekГ§i fotoДҹraf Гјretmek gibi iЕҹler iГ§in generative modeller doДҹal bir araГ§.

Anomali tespiti aГ§ДұsДұndan: model normal verinin daДҹДұlДұmДұnДұ Г¶Дҹrenirse, o daДҹДұlДұm altДұnda Г§ok dГјЕҹГјk olasДұlДұklДұ gГ¶rГјnen yeni Г¶rnekleri anormal olarak iЕҹaretleyebilirsin.

Bilimsel aГ§Дұdan: hГјcre gГ¶rГјntГјsГј sentezi, protein yapДұsДұ Гјretimi, ilaГ§ molekГјlГј tasarДұmДұ gibi alanlarda generative modeller gerГ§ekten kullanДұlДұyor.

sections.heading: S2. Autoencoder nedir? Klasik vs. VAE farkДұ? EДҹitim amacДұ nedir?
sections.text:
Autoencoder iki parГ§adan oluЕҹur: encoder girdiyi alДұp kГјГ§Гјk bir vektГ¶re (latent code z) sДұkДұЕҹtДұrДұr, decoder bu z'den girdiyi yeniden inЕҹa etmeye Г§alДұЕҹДұr. EДҹitim amacДұ basit: x'e verip xМӮ al, aralarДұndaki farkДұ minimize et. Bu sayede aДҹ verinin Г¶nemli Г¶zelliklerini latent space'e sДұkДұЕҹtДұrmayДұ Г¶Дҹrenir. Boyut azaltma, gГјrГјltГј giderme, Г¶zellik Г¶Дҹrenme iГ§in iЕҹe yarДұyor.

Ama bir generative model olarak klasik autoencoder'Дұn ciddi bir sorunu var: z bir nokta, latent space'de boЕҹluklar var. Yani eДҹitimde hiГ§ gГ¶rmediДҹin bir z noktasДұndan decode etmeye Г§alДұЕҹДұrsan anlamsДұz bir Г§ДұktДұ alДұrsДұn. Rastgele z Г¶rnekleyemezsin вҖ” dolayДұsДұyla yeni veri Гјretemezsin.

VAE bu problemi ЕҹГ¶yle Г§Г¶zer: encoder artДұk tek bir nokta deДҹil, bir Gaussian daДҹДұlДұm parametresi (Ој, ПғВІ) Гјretir. z bu daДҹДұlДұmdan Г¶rneklenir. KL divergence terimi bu daДҹДұlДұmДұ N(0,I) standart Gaussian'a yakДұn kalmaya zorlar. BГ¶ylece latent space dolup taЕҹar, her noktadan anlamlДұ bir Г§ДұktДұ alabilirsin.

EДҹitim amacДұ (ELBO) iki terimin dengesidir:
  Reconstruction loss вҖ” decoder'Дұn girdiyi iyi yeniden inЕҹa etmesini ister.
  KL divergence вҖ” latent daДҹДұlДұmДұn Г§ok daДҹДұlmasДұnДұ engeller, dГјzenli tutar.
Bu dengeyi bulmak Г¶nemli: Г§ok sДұkДұЕҹtДұrДұrsan gГ¶rГјntГјler bulanДұklaЕҹДұr, Г§ok gevЕҹek olursa sampling Г§alДұЕҹmaz.

Reparameterization trick: z = Ој + Пғ вҠҷ Оө, Оө ~ N(0,I)
Bu formГјl Г¶rnekleme adДұmДұnДұ gradyan akДұЕҹДұna aГ§Дұk hale getirir, yoksa backprop Г§alДұЕҹmazdДұ.

sections.heading: S3. GAN'Дұn genel fikri nedir? EДҹitim nasДұl Г§alДұЕҹДұr? Small gradients problemi nasДұl Г§Г¶zГјlГјr?
sections.text:
GAN'Дұn ana fikri iki aДҹДұ birbirine karЕҹДұ koymak: Generator ve Discriminator. Generator rastgele bir gГјrГјltГј vektГ¶rГј z alДұp sahte bir gГ¶rГјntГј Гјretmeye Г§alДұЕҹДұrken, Discriminator gelen gГ¶rГјntГјnГјn gerГ§ek mi sahte mi olduДҹunu anlamaya Г§alДұЕҹДұr. Bu bir minimax oyunu: Discriminator ne kadar iyi ayДұrt ederse Generator da bir o kadar gerГ§ekГ§i Гјretmek zorunda kalДұr, ve bu gidiЕҹ geliЕҹte ikisi de ilerler.

EДҹitim dГ¶ngГјsГј iki adДұm Еҹeklinde iЕҹler:
Г–nce Discriminator gГјncellenir вҖ” hem gerГ§ek gГ¶rГјntГјler hem de Generator'Дұn ГјrettiДҹi sahteler gГ¶sterilir, ikisini doДҹru ayДұrt etmesi istenir.
Sonra Generator gГјncellenir вҖ” ГјrettiДҹi sahteleri Discriminator'a yutturmaya Г§alДұЕҹДұr, yani D(G(z)) вүҲ 1 olsun ister.
Bu iki adДұm dГ¶nГјЕҹГјmlГј tekrarlanДұr.

Small gradients (vanishing gradient) problemi Еҹu anda ortaya Г§Дұkar: eДҹitim baЕҹДұnda Generator Г§ok kГ¶tГј gГ¶rГјntГјler ГјrettiДҹi iГ§in Discriminator bu gГ¶rГјntГјleri kolayca sahte olarak %100 emin Еҹekilde sДұnДұflandДұrДұr. Matematiksel olarak log(1 - D(G(z))) вүҲ 0'a yaklaЕҹДұr, gradyan neredeyse sДұfДұrlanДұr ve Generator Г¶Дҹrenemez.

ГҮГ¶zГјm non-saturating loss adДұ verilen kГјГ§Гјk bir deДҹiЕҹikliktir: Generator'Дұ log(1 - D(G(z)))'yi minimize etmek yerine log(D(G(z)))'yi maximize edecek Еҹekilde yeniden formГјle edersin. Matematiksel olarak aynДұ yГ¶nГј gГ¶sterse de erken eДҹitimde Г§ok daha bГјyГјk gradyanlar saДҹlar, Discriminator ne kadar emin olursa olsun Generator anlamlДұ bir Г¶Дҹrenme sinyali almaya devam eder.

sections.heading: S4. CNN'lerin GAN'da yapДұ taЕҹДұ olarak kullanДұlmasДұnДұ saДҹlayan mimari kararlar neler?
sections.text:
Orijinal GAN fully connected katmanlar kullanДұyordu ve gГ¶rГјntГјler iГ§in verimli deДҹildi. DCGAN (Deep Convolutional GAN) CNN'i GAN mimarisine baЕҹarДұyla entegre etti ve birkaГ§ temel kararla bunu saДҹladДұ.

Generator iГ§in ana fikir Еҹu: gГјrГјltГј vektГ¶rГј z kГјГ§Гјk bir feature map'e dГ¶nГјЕҹtГјrГјlГјr, sonra transposed convolution (deconv) katmanlarДұyla adДұm adДұm bГјyГјtГјlerek tam Г§Г¶zГјnГјrlГјklГј gГ¶rГјntГјye ulaЕҹДұlДұr. Her katmanda Batch Normalization uygulanДұr Г§ГјnkГј bu eДҹitim sГјrecini dramatik biГ§imde kararlДұ hale getirir. Aktivasyon olarak ReLU kullanДұlДұr, sadece son Г§ДұktДұ katmanДұnda gГ¶rГјntГјyГј [-1,1] aralДұДҹДұna getirmek iГ§in Tanh tercih edilir.

Discriminator iГ§in ise tersi yapДұlДұr: gГ¶rГјntГј strided convolution'larla adДұm adДұm kГјГ§ГјltГјlГјr. Burada Г¶nemli bir karar pooling kullanmamak вҖ” bunun yerine stride=2 convolutional katmanlarla downsample edilir, bГ¶ylece aДҹДұn spatial bilgiyi nasДұl dГјЕҹГјreceДҹini Г¶Дҹrenmesine izin verilir. Aktivasyon LeakyReLU seГ§ilir Г§ГјnkГј standart ReLU'nun aksine negatif giriЕҹlere de kГјГ§Гјk bir gradyan geГ§irir ve discriminator'Дұn Г¶lГј nГ¶ronlara takДұlmasДұnДұ engeller.

Genel ilke olarak fully connected katmanlar mГјmkГјn olduДҹunca azaltДұlДұr, 3Г—3 veya 4Г—4 kernellar tercih edilir. BatchNorm'un eДҹitime katkДұsДұ o kadar bГјyГјk ki, onsuz GAN'larДұ stabil tutmak Г§ok zorlaЕҹДұyor.

sections.heading: S5. InfoGAN'da latent code disentangling ne anlama geliyor?
sections.text:
Vanilla GAN'da gГјrГјltГј vektГ¶rГј z rastgele bir vektГ¶rdГјr ve her boyutunun ne anlama geldiДҹi bilinmez. AДҹ istediДҹini kodlayabilir ve bu tamamen kara kutu gibidir вҖ” z'nin bir boyutunu deДҹiЕҹtirirsen Гјretilen gГ¶rГјntГјde ne deДҹiЕҹeceДҹini tahmin edemezsin.

Disentangling (ayrДұЕҹtДұrma) demek, latent space'in her boyutunu insan tarafДұndan anlamlДұ, birbirinden baДҹДұmsДұz bir faktГ¶rle eЕҹleЕҹtirmek demektir. Diyelim ki el yazДұsДұ rakam Гјretiyorsun: ideal bir disentangled latent space'de z[0] rakamДұn tipini (0-9 arasДұ), z[1] yazДұ kalДұnlДұДҹДұnДұ, z[2] eДҹim aГ§ДұsДұnДұ kontrol eder. Bir boyutu deДҹiЕҹtirince sadece o Г¶zellik deДҹiЕҹir, diДҹerleri sabit kalДұr.

InfoGAN bunu ЕҹГ¶yle baЕҹarДұr: z vektГ¶rГјnГј ikiye bГ¶ler вҖ” bir kДұsДұm gГјrГјltГј kalДұr, bir kДұsДұm latent code c olur. Loss'a ek bir mutual information terimi eklenir; bu terim, Гјretilen gГ¶rГјntГјye bakДұp c'yi geri tahmin edebilmeni ister. Yani Generator, c'deki bilgiyi gГ¶rГјntГјye yansДұtmak zorunda kalДұr, onu gГ¶rmezden gelemez. SonuГ§ olarak c boyutlarДұ anlamlДұ faktГ¶rlere karЕҹДұlДұk gelmeye baЕҹlar вҖ” ve bu Г¶Дҹrenim tamamen unsupervised, hangi boyutun hangi Г¶zelliДҹi temsil edeceДҹi Г¶nceden kimse tarafДұndan sГ¶ylenmemiЕҹ.

sections.heading: S6. Д°ki latent code arasДұnda interpolasyon: InfoGAN vs vanilla GAN farkДұ ne?
sections.text:
Deneyi ЕҹГ¶yle yaparsДұn: iki farklДұ latent code al (z_A ve z_B), bunlar arasДұnda lineer interpolasyon yaparak z_t = (1-t)*z_A + t*z_B vektГ¶rlerini Гјret, her t deДҹeri iГ§in Generator'dan gГ¶rГјntГј al ve bu geГ§iЕҹ gГ¶rГјntГјlerini gГ¶zlemle.

Vanilla GAN'da bu geГ§iЕҹ gГ¶rГјntГјleri Гјretilir ama Г§oДҹu zaman anlamsДұz ya da tutarsДұzdДұr. Bunun nedeni latent space'in organize olmamasДұdДұr вҖ” z_A ve z_B arasДұndaki yol tamamen keyfidir, hangi Г¶zelliДҹin deДҹiЕҹtiДҹi belli deДҹildir. Bir noktada gГ¶rГјntГј bozulabilir ya da beklediДҹinden Г§ok farklДұ bir Еҹeye dГ¶nГјЕҹebilir.

InfoGAN'da ise tablo Г§ok farklДұdДұr Г§ГјnkГј latent code c ayrДұЕҹtДұrДұlmДұЕҹtДұr. Mesela MNIST'te c'nin rakam tipini kontrol eden boyutunu interpolasyona sokarsan 3'ten 7'ye doДҹru dГјzgГјn ve anlamlДұ bir geГ§iЕҹ gГ¶rГјrsГјn. KalДұnlДұДҹДұ kontrol eden boyutu deДҹiЕҹtirirsen rakam tipi sabit kalДұrken sadece kalДұnlДұk deДҹiЕҹir. Her boyutun etkisi izole edilmiЕҹ olduДҹu iГ§in geГ§iЕҹ hem gГ¶rsel olarak dГјzgГјn hem de yorumlanabilirdir.

KДұsaca sГ¶ylersek: InfoGAN latent space'e bir koordinat sistemi kazandДұrДұr ve iГ§inde anlamlДұ yГ¶nler bulmana izin verir. Vanilla GAN'da bu koordinat sistemi yoktur.

sections.heading: S7. Conditional GAN'Дұn mantДұДҹДұ nedir? Ne koЕҹullandДұrДұlДұr? cGAN ile image-to-image translation nasДұl?
sections.text:
Vanilla GAN'da ne ГјretileceДҹini kontrol edemiyorsun вҖ” z'yi veriyorsun, ne Г§ДұkacaДҹДұ rastgele. Conditional GAN buna bir Г§Г¶zГјm getirir: Гјretimi istediДҹin bir koЕҹula gГ¶re yГ¶nlendirebilirsin. z'ye ek olarak bir koЕҹul c de verirsin ve aДҹ bu koЕҹula uygun iГ§erik Гјretir.

KoЕҹul olarak pek Г§ok Еҹey kullanДұlabilir. SДұnДұf etiketi ("kedi Гјret", "kГ¶pek Гјret") en basit Г¶rnektir. BaЕҹka bir gГ¶rГјntГј de koЕҹul olabilir вҖ” mesela gece fotoДҹrafДұnДұ gГјndГјz fotoДҹrafДұna Г§evirmek istiyorsan gece fotoДҹrafДұ koЕҹul olarak verilir. Metin aГ§ДұklamasДұ ya da segmentasyon maskesi de kullanДұlabilir.

Mimari aГ§Дұdan bakДұldДұДҹДұnda deДҹiЕҹiklik hem Generator'Дұ hem de Discriminator'Дұ etkiler. Generator artДұk [z, c] birleЕҹimini alДұr ve c'ye uygun iГ§erik Гјretmek zorundadДұr. Discriminator ise sadece "bu gГ¶rГјntГј gerГ§ek mi?" sorusunu deДҹil, "bu gГ¶rГјntГј koЕҹul c ile tutarlДұ mДұ?" sorusunu sorar. Yoksa Generator her koЕҹul iГ§in herhangi bir gerГ§ek gГ¶rГјntГј Гјretip Discriminator'Дұ kandДұrabilirdi.

Image-to-image translation iГ§in pix2pix bu yaklaЕҹДұmДұ somutlaЕҹtДұrДұr. Generator olarak U-Net kullanДұlДұr Г§ГјnkГј skip connection'lar sayesinde ince detaylar korunur. Discriminator olarak PatchGAN kullanДұlДұr вҖ” tГјm gГ¶rГјntГјyГј deДҹil, kГјГ§Гјk patch'leri ayrДұ ayrДұ deДҹerlendirir ve bu yerel doku kalitesine odaklanmayДұ saДҹlar. Loss ise adversarial kДұsmДұn yanДұna bir L1 terimi ekler, bu sayede piksel dГјzeyinde de hedef gГ¶rГјntГјye yakДұn kalДұnДұr.

sections.heading: S8. Conditional GAN ile image-to-image translation'Дұn potansiyel dezavantajДұ nedir?
sections.text:
pix2pix ve benzeri conditional GAN yaklaЕҹДұmlarДұnДұn kritik bir zayДұflДұДҹДұ var: eЕҹleЕҹtirilmiЕҹ (paired) eДҹitim verisi gerektiriyorlar. Her giriЕҹ gГ¶rГјntГјsГјnГјn tam olarak karЕҹДұlДұk gelen bir hedef gГ¶rГјntГјsГј olmasДұ lazДұm.

Bu gerГ§ek dГјnyada bГјyГјk bir sorun. ГҮizimden fotoДҹraf Гјretmek istiyorsan, her Г§izim iГ§in aynДұ sahnenin fotoДҹrafДұnДұ Г§ekmen gerekiyor. Yaz gГ¶rГјnГјmГјnГј kДұЕҹ gГ¶rГјnГјmГјne Г§evirmek istiyorsan, aynДұ sahnenin iki mevsim versiyonunu elde etmen gerekiyor вҖ” bu imkansДұza yakДұn. MRI'Дұ CT'ye Г§evirmek istiyorsan aynДұ hastanДұn her iki taramasДұna da ihtiyacДұn var.

BazДұ durumlar ise doДҹasДұ gereДҹi paired veri Гјretmeye izin vermez. Van Gogh tarzДұnda bir fotoДҹraf Г§evirmek istediДҹini dГјЕҹГјn вҖ” Van Gogh fotoДҹraf makinesi olan birinin Г§aДҹДұnda yaЕҹamamДұЕҹ, dolayДұsДұyla "bu fotoДҹrafДұn Van Gogh tarafДұndan boyanmДұЕҹ hali" diye bir veri seti var olamaz.

Д°Еҹte bu noktada paired veri gerektirmeyen CycleGAN devreye girer.

sections.heading: S9. CycleGAN'Дұn yararДұ ve Г§alДұЕҹma mantДұДҹДұ nedir? Mimarisi ve kayДұp fonksiyonlarДұ?
sections.text:
CycleGAN'Дұn sunduДҹu temel avantaj Еҹudur: eЕҹleЕҹtirilmiЕҹ veri olmadan iki domain arasДұnda Г§eviri yapabilir. At fotoДҹraflarДұ ve zebra fotoДҹraflarДұ gibi iki ayrДұ koleksiyon yeterli вҖ” hangi atДұn hangi zebranДұn karЕҹДұlДұДҹДұ olduДҹunu bilmen gerekmiyor.

Mimari iki Generator ve iki Discriminator iГ§erir:
  G: X domain'inden Y domain'ine Г§evirir (at вҶ’ zebra)
  F: Y domain'inden X domain'ine Г§evirir (zebra вҶ’ at)
  D_Y: gerГ§ek Y gГ¶rГјntГјlerini G'nin Гјrettiklerinden ayДұrt etmeye Г§alДұЕҹДұr
  D_X: gerГ§ek X gГ¶rГјntГјlerini F'nin Гјrettiklerinden ayДұrt etmeye Г§alДұЕҹДұr

EДҹitim sДұrasДұnda her iki discriminator da ayrДұ ayrДұ gГјncellenir, Generator'lar ise hem discriminator'Дұ kandДұrmak hem de cycle-consistency saДҹlamak iГ§in eДҹitilir.

KayДұp fonksiyonlarДұ ГјГ§ bileЕҹenden oluЕҹur:

Adversarial loss (her iki yГ¶n iГ§in): G'nin ГјrettiДҹi gГ¶rГјntГјler D_Y'yi, F'nin Гјrettikleri D_X'i kandДұrmalДұ.

Cycle-consistency loss: X вҶ’ Y вҶ’ X geri dГ¶nГјnce orijinal X Г§ДұkmalДұ; Y вҶ’ X вҶ’ Y geri dГ¶nГјnce orijinal Y Г§ДұkmalДұ. Yani F(G(x)) вүҲ x ve G(F(y)) вүҲ y. Bu en kritik kДұsДұt.

Identity loss (opsiyonel): G'ye zaten Y domain'inden bir gГ¶rГјntГј verirsen onu deДҹiЕҹtirmemeli вҖ” G(y) вүҲ y. Bu renk tutarlДұlДұДҹДұnДұ korumaya yardДұmcДұ olur.

sections.heading: S10. Cycle-consistency loss'un fikri nedir? Neden dahil etmek faydalДұ?
sections.text:
Problemi ЕҹГ¶yle dГјЕҹГјn: paired veri yok, yani sadece adversarial loss varsa Generator neyin doДҹru Г§eviri olduДҹunu bilemez. Teorik olarak Generator, X'teki girdi gГ¶rГјntГјsГјnГј tamamen gГ¶rmezden gelip Y domain'inden herhangi bir gerГ§ekГ§i gГ¶rГјntГј Гјretebilir ve Discriminator'Дұ hГўlГў kandДұrabilir. SonuГ§ olarak at fotoДҹrafДұndan zebra Гјretmek yerine tamamen alakasДұz ama gerГ§ekГ§i gГ¶rГјnen bir zebra gГ¶rГјntГјsГј Гјretir. Д°Г§erik kaybДұ tamamen gizli kalДұr.

Cycle-consistency bunu engeller, Г§ГјnkГј anlamlДұ bir Г§evirinin geri Г§evrilince orijinali vermesi gerektiДҹini sГ¶yler:
  x вҶ’ G(x) вҶ’ F(G(x)) вүҲ x
  y вҶ’ F(y) вҶ’ G(F(y)) вүҲ y

Bu kДұsДұt Generator'Дұ X'in iГ§eriДҹini korumaya zorlar. AtДұ zebraya Г§evirirken atДұn pozunu, yapДұsДұnДұ, arka planДұnДұ korumasДұ gerekiyor вҖ” sadece dДұЕҹ gГ¶rГјnГјm (Г§izgiler, renk) deДҹiЕҹmeli. Generator rastgele bir zebra fotoДҹrafДұ Гјretirse geri Г§evirince orijinal at gГ¶rГјntГјsГјnГј veremez, bu da loss'u artДұrДұr.

Bir dil Г§evirisi analojisi ile dГјЕҹГјnebilirsin: TГјrkГ§e cГјmleyi Д°ngilizceye Г§evirdikten sonra tekrar TГјrkГ§eye Г§evirdiДҹinde aynДұ anlamДұ elde edebiliyorsan, o Г§eviri muhtemelen doДҹrudur. Bu kДұsДұt olmadan sistem sadece Discriminator'Дұ kandДұran ama anlamsДұz Г§eviriler Г¶Дҹrenebilir.

ida10.title: Autoencoders and Variational Autoencoders
ida10.summary: Autoencoders learn compact latent representations by compressing and reconstructing data. VAEs (Kingma & Welling, 2013) add a probabilistic constraint вҖ” the latent space must follow a Gaussian prior вҖ” enabling generative sampling. They are trained with the ELBO (Evidence Lower BOund).

sections.heading: VAE Loss: Evidence Lower Bound (ELBO)
sections.text:
Encoder: $q_\phi(\mathbf{z}|\mathbf{x})$ outputs mean $\boldsymbol{\mu}$ and variance $\boldsymbol{\sigma}^2$.
Reparameterization: $\mathbf{z} = \boldsymbol{\mu} + \boldsymbol{\sigma} \odot \boldsymbol{\epsilon},\; \boldsymbol{\epsilon} \sim \mathcal{N}(0,I)$

Decoder: $p_\theta(\mathbf{x}|\mathbf{z})$ reconstructs the input.

$$\mathcal{L}_{\mathrm{ELBO}} = \underbrace{\mathbb{E}_{q_\phi}[\log p_\theta(\mathbf{x}|\mathbf{z})]}_{{\text{reconstruction term}}} - \underbrace{D_{\mathrm{KL}}(q_\phi(\mathbf{z}|\mathbf{x}) \| p(\mathbf{z}))}_{{\text{regularisation term}}}$$

For Gaussian prior $p(\mathbf{z}) = \mathcal{N}(0,I)$:
$$D_{\mathrm{KL}} = -\frac{1}{2}\sum_j (1 + \log\sigma_j^2 - \mu_j^2 - \sigma_j^2)$$

sections.heading: Why the KL Regularisation Term?
sections.text: Without regularisation, the encoder would map each input to a tiny Gaussian (small variance) around a unique point in latent space вҖ” effectively memorising, not learning a generalisable representation. The KL term forces the posterior $q_\phi(\mathbf{z}|\mathbf{x})$ to be close to the standard Gaussian prior, encouraging the latent space to be compact and smooth. In the latent space of a well-trained VAE, interpolating between two points $\mathbf{z}_1$ and $\mathbf{z}_2$ produces smooth, realistic-looking images. This smoothness is what enables generation: sample $\mathbf{z} \sim \mathcal{N}(0,I)$ and decode it. The reparameterization trick makes this differentiable вҖ” $\mathbf{z} = \boldsymbol{\mu} + \boldsymbol{\sigma} \odot \boldsymbol{\epsilon}$ lets gradients flow through the sampling step.

ida10.title: GAN Fundamentals: Minimax Training
ida10.summary: GANs (Goodfellow et al. 2014) pit two networks against each other: a generator G produces fake images; a discriminator D tries to distinguish real from fake. Training is a minimax game that, in theory, drives the generator distribution to match the real data distribution.
ida10.context: Lecture 10 В· 04.01.2024 вҖ” GAN formulation and training

sections.heading: GAN Minimax Objective
sections.text:
$$\min_G \max_D \; V(G,D) = \mathbb{E}_{\mathbf{x}\sim p_{\mathrm{data}}}[\log D(\mathbf{x})] + \mathbb{E}_{\mathbf{z}\sim p_\mathbf{z}}[\log(1-D(G(\mathbf{z})))]$$

$D$ maximises: correctly classify real as 1, fake as 0.
$G$ minimises: make D classify G(z) as real (close to 1).

In practice, $G$ maximises $\mathbb{E}[\log D(G(\mathbf{z}))]$ (non-saturating loss) instead, which provides stronger gradients early in training when D is confident that G's outputs are fake.

sections.heading: Mode Collapse and Training Instability
sections.text: GAN training is notoriously unstable. Mode collapse: G finds a small number of outputs that fool D and keeps generating only those, ignoring diversity (e.g. generating only one digit regardless of input). Training oscillations: D becomes too strong вҶ’ G gets no gradient signal (D(G(z)) вүҲ 0 вҶ’ log(1-0) = 0, flat); D becomes too weak вҶ’ G doesn't need to improve. Wasserstein GAN (WGAN) reformulates the objective as the Wasserstein-1 distance, providing meaningful gradients even when distributions don't overlap, solving many instability issues. Spectral normalisation (SN-GAN) constrains the discriminator's Lipschitz constant, stabilising training.

left.title: GAN
points: Mode collapse risk вҖ” may miss modes of p_data

right.title: VAE
points: Tractable ELBO (lower bound on log p(x))
Better latent space structure for editing

ida10.title: Conditional GANs and Image-to-Image Translation
ida10.summary: Conditional GANs (cGANs) condition both G and D on additional information (class label, input image), enabling controlled synthesis. Pix2pix enables paired image-to-image translation; CycleGAN enables unpaired translation using cycle-consistency.
ida10.context: Lecture 10 В· 04.01.2024 вҖ” Controlled image generation

sections.heading: Pix2pix: Paired Image Translation
sections.text: Pix2pix (Isola et al. 2017) trains a cGAN with paired training data: input image $x$ (e.g. semantic label map) and target image $y$ (e.g. photo). The generator is a U-Net that maps $x вҶ’ y$. The discriminator is a PatchGAN that classifies whether overlapping $70\times70$ image patches are real or fake вҖ” rather than classifying the entire image at once. PatchGAN is more efficient and focuses the discriminator on local texture quality. The loss is $\mathcal{L} = \mathcal{L}_{cGAN} + \lambda \mathcal{L}_{L1}$, where the L1 term directly encourages low-frequency similarity to the target (handles large-scale structure) while the adversarial term encourages high-frequency detail.

sections.heading: CycleGAN: Unpaired Translation
sections.text: CycleGAN (Zhu et al. 2017) enables translation between two image domains X and Y without paired examples (e.g. horsesвҶ”zebras, photosвҶ”Monet paintings). Two generators: $G: X вҶ’ Y$ and $F: Y вҶ’ X$, with two discriminators. Cycle-consistency loss enforces that translating back and forth should preserve the original: $\|F(G(x)) - x\|_1 + \|G(F(y)) - y\|_1$. Without pairing, the only supervision is this cycle-consistency вҖ” the network must learn a mapping that is both convincing to the discriminator and reversible. This prevents degenerate solutions (e.g. just ignoring the input and generating any image from domain Y).

ida11.title: Lecture 11 вҖ” Q & A
ida11.summary: IDA Lecture 11'in tГјm sДұnav sorularДұ ve sade aГ§Дұklamalarla cevaplarДұ. Diffusion modeller, forward/backward process, variance schedule, DDPM training, classifier guidance, CLIP, DALL-E 2, Imagen.

sections.heading: S1. Hangi generative model tГјrlerini biliyorsun? Benzerlik ve farklar neler?
sections.text:
DГ¶rt ana generative model ailesi var ve birbirinden Г¶nemli farklarДұ var.

VAE (Variational Autoencoder): GГ¶rГјntГјyГј latent bir Gaussian daДҹДұlДұma sДұkДұЕҹtДұrДұr, oradan tekrar gГ¶rГјntГјye dГ¶ner. EДҹitim Г§ok stabil Г§ГјnkГј net bir kayДұp fonksiyonu var (ELBO). Sorun Еҹu ki gГ¶rГјntГјler biraz bulanДұk Г§Дұkar вҖ” piksel dГјzeyinde reconstruction yapДұlДұnca aДҹ detay yerine ortalamalarДұ Г¶Дҹrenmeye yГ¶nelir. Latent space yapДұsДұ gГјzel ve dГјzenli.

GAN (Generative Adversarial Network): Generator ve Discriminator birbirine karЕҹДұ oynar. GГ¶rГјntГј kalitesi Г§ok yГјksek, keskin ve gerГ§ekГ§i Г§ДұktДұlar verir. Ama eДҹitim sГјreci dengesizdir, mode collapse riski var ve p(x) olasДұlДұДҹДұnДұ doДҹrudan hesaplamak mГјmkГјn deДҹil.

Diffusion Models (DDPM): GerГ§ek gГ¶rГјntГјye adДұm adДұm gГјrГјltГј ekleyip ardДұndan bu sГјreci tersine dГ¶ndГјrmeyi Г¶Дҹrenir. EДҹitim Г§ok stabil, gГ¶rГјntГј kalitesi Еҹu an en Гјst dГјzeyde. Tek dezavantajДұ sampling'in yavaЕҹ olmasДұ вҖ” yГјzlerce ya da binlerce adДұm gerekiyor.

Flow Models: Invertible (tersine Г§evrilebilir) transformasyonlar kullanДұr ve exact likelihood hesaplayabilir. ГҮok bellek yoДҹun olduДҹu iГ§in pratikte daha az kullanДұlДұyor.

Benzerlik aГ§ДұsДұndan hepsi bir veriden anlamlДұ temsil Г§Дұkarmaya ve daДҹДұlДұmДұ modellemeye Г§alДұЕҹДұr. Fark olarak eДҹitim stabilitesinde diffusion en iyisi, gГ¶rГјntГј kalitesinde diffusion ve GAN baЕҹa baЕҹ, hДұz aГ§ДұsДұndan GAN en hДұzlДұ.

sections.heading: S2. Denoising diffusion probabilistic models (DDPM) genel fikri nedir?
sections.text:
DDPM'in ana fikri iki yГ¶nlГј bir sГјreГ§ Гјzerine kurulu.

Forward process вҖ” gГјrГјltГј ekleme: GerГ§ek bir gГ¶rГјntГј x_0'dan baЕҹlarsДұn ve her adДұmda kГјГ§Гјk bir miktar Gaussian gГјrГјltГј eklersin. Yeterince Г§ok adДұm (tipik olarak T=1000) sonunda gГ¶rГјntГј tamamen saf Gaussian gГјrГјltГјye dГ¶nГјЕҹГјr. Bu sГјreГ§ sabit ve Г¶Дҹrenilmez вҖ” sadece gГјrГјltГј eklemek.

Backward process вҖ” gГјrГјltГј Г§Дұkarma: Еһimdi iЕҹi tersine yapmak istiyorsun. x_T'den (saf gГјrГјltГј) baЕҹlayДұp adДұm adДұm gГјrГјltГјyГј Г§Дұkararak x_0'a (temiz gГ¶rГјntГј) ulaЕҹmak. Bunu yapmak iГ§in bir sinir aДҹДұ eДҹitirsin. AДҹa sorduДҹun soru Еҹu: "Bu adДұmda hangi gГјrГјltГј eklenmiЕҹti?" AДҹ bunu tahmin etmeyi Г¶Дҹrenirse, tersini yaparak her adДұmda gГ¶rГјntГјyГј gГјrГјltГјden arДұndДұrabilirsin.

Neden bu Еҹekilde Г§alДұЕҹДұyor? ГҮГјnkГј gГ¶rГјntГјden gГјrГјltГјye geГ§iЕҹ matematiksel olarak kolayca tanДұmlanabiliyor, ama saf gГјrГјltГјden doДҹrudan gerГ§ekГ§i gГ¶rГјntГјye gitmek Г§ok zor. AdДұm adДұm yaparak zor problemi Г§ok sayДұda kГјГ§Гјk kolaylaЕҹtДұrДұlmДұЕҹ probleme bГ¶ldГјrГјyorsun.

EДҹitim stabili Г§ГјnkГј hiГ§bir adversarial oyun yok вҖ” sadece "ne kadar gГјrГјltГј vardДұ" sorusunu cevaplamaya Г§alДұЕҹДұyorsun.

sections.heading: S3. Forward diffusion process nasДұl tanДұmlanДұr? Ne zaman durulur? Pratikte nasДұl yapДұlДұr?
sections.text:
Forward process matematiksel olarak bir Markov zinciridir: her adДұm sadece bir Г¶nceki adДұma baДҹlДұdДұr. Her adДұm Еҹu Еҹekilde tanДұmlanДұr:

  q(x_t | x_{t-1}) = N(x_t; вҲҡ(1-ОІ_t) В· x_{t-1}, ОІ_t В· I)

Yani her adДұmda x_{t-1}'i biraz kГјГ§ГјltГјrsГјn (вҲҡ(1-ОІ_t) ile Г§arparak sinyali azaltДұrsДұn) ve Гјzerine ОІ_t kadarlДұk Gaussian gГјrГјltГј eklersin. ОІ_t deДҹerleri kГјГ§Гјk tutulur (tipik olarak 0.0001'den 0.02'ye kadar), bГ¶ylece her adДұm yavaЕҹ ve kademeli bir deДҹiЕҹim olur.

Ne zaman durulur? T adДұm sonra x_T artДұk neredeyse saf N(0, I) Gaussian gГјrГјltГјye dГ¶nГјЕҹmГјЕҹtГјr ve baЕҹlangДұГ§taki gГ¶rГјntГјden iz kalmamДұЕҹtДұr. Genellikle T = 1000 yeterli sayДұlДұr.

Pratikte Г¶nemli bir verimlilik var: tГјm bu adДұmlarДұ sДұrayla hesaplamak zorunda deДҹilsin. Оұ_t = 1-ОІ_t ve бҫұ_t = вҲҸОұ_s (kГјmГјlatif Г§arpДұm) tanДұmlarsan, herhangi bir t adДұmДұna tek seferde geГ§ebilirsin:

  x_t = вҲҡбҫұ_t В· x_0 + вҲҡ(1-бҫұ_t) В· Оө,   Оө ~ N(0,I)

Bu formГјl eДҹitimi Г§ok verimli kДұlar: eДҹitimde rastgele bir t seГ§, x_t'yi tek adДұmda hesapla, aДҹa ver. Bin adДұmДұ tek tek simГјle etmene gerek yok.

sections.heading: S4. Variance schedule nasДұl dahil edilir? Cosine scheduler DDPM performansДұnДұ nasДұl etkiler?
sections.text:
Variance schedule, ОІ_1'den ОІ_T'ye kadar giden dizidir ve her adДұmda ne kadar gГјrГјltГј ekleneceДҹini belirler. DoДҹru schedule seГ§imi eДҹitim kalitesini ciddi biГ§imde etkiler.

Linear schedule (orijinal DDPM): ОІ_t 0.0001'den 0.02'ye lineer olarak artar. KulaДҹa makul gelir ama bir problemi var: бҫұ_t Г§ok erken dГјЕҹer, yani gГ¶rГјntГј Г§ok hДұzlДұ bilgisini kaybeder. EДҹitimin son yarДұsДұnda aДҹ neredeyse tamamen saf gГјrГјltГјye bakДұyor, anlamlДұ gГ¶rГјntГј Г¶zelliДҹi yok, dolayДұsДұyla Г¶ДҹreneceДҹi bir Еҹey de kalmДұyor.

Cosine schedule (Improved DDPM): бҫұ_t deДҹerini cosinus fonksiyonuyla hesaplar, bГ¶ylece baЕҹlangДұГ§ta gГјrГјltГј ekleme Г§ok daha yavaЕҹ gerГ§ekleЕҹir, ortada dengeli bir geГ§iЕҹ yaЕҹanДұr, ve hatta sГјrecin sonuna kadar gГ¶rГјntГјde hГўlГў bir miktar sinyal kalmaya devam eder.

Bu neden Г¶nemli? AДҹ eДҹitim sДұrasДұnda her t deДҹerinde bir Еҹeyler Г¶Дҹrenmesi gerekiyor. EДҹer gГ¶rГјntГј t=500'de zaten tamamen gГјrГјltГјye dГ¶nmГјЕҹse, t=500 ila t=1000 arasДұndaki adДұmlarda aДҹa verecek anlamlДұ bilgi yok. Cosine schedule bu boЕҹa giden eДҹitim bГјtГ§esini ortadan kaldДұrДұr ve Г¶zellikle kГјГ§Гјk gГ¶rГјntГј Г§Г¶zГјnГјrlГјklerinde belirgin kalite iyileЕҹmesi saДҹlar.

sections.heading: S5. Backward process (reverse diffusion) mantДұДҹДұ nedir? q(x_{t-1}|x_t) nasДұl yaklaЕҹДұklanДұr?
sections.text:
Forward direction (gГјrГјltГј eklemek) kolaydДұr вҖ” sadece bir formГјl. Ama bizi asДұl ilgilendiren backward direction (gГјrГјltГјyГј Г§Дұkarmak): x_t'ye bakarak x_{t-1}'i elde etmek. Bu Г§ok daha zor bir problem.

Matematiksel olarak q(x_{t-1} | x_t) hesaplamak iГ§in tГјm veri daДҹДұlДұmДұnДұ p(x) bilmek gerekir вҖ” ama bu zaten Г¶Дҹrenmek istediДҹimiz Еҹey. DolayДұsДұyla bu daДҹДұlДұmДұ doДҹrudan hesaplayamayДұz.

ГҮГ¶zГјm, parametreli bir model p_Оё ile yaklaЕҹДұklamak:
  p_Оё(x_{t-1} | x_t) = N(x_{t-1}; Ој_Оё(x_t, t), ОЈ_Оё(x_t, t))

Bir sinir aДҹДұ, gГјrГјltГјlГј x_t ve zaman adДұmДұ t'ye bakarak bu Gaussian'Дұn ortalamasДұnДұ (ve kovaryansДұnДұ) tahmin etmeyi Г¶Дҹrenir. Pratikte aДҹ doДҹrudan Ој'yu tahmin etmek yerine eklenen gГјrГјltГј Оө'yДұ tahmin eder вҖ” bu matematiksel olarak eЕҹdeДҹer ama Г¶Дҹrenmesi daha kolay bir problem. Ој_Оё daha sonra bu gГјrГјltГј tahmininden kapalДұ formda hesaplanДұr. Kovaryans ОЈ ise genellikle basitlik aГ§ДұsДұndan sabit ОІ_t I olarak alДұnДұr.

EДҹitimde avantaj Еҹu: biz forward process'i kendimiz uyguladДұДҹДұmДұz iГ§in hangi Оө'yДұ eklediДҹimizi kesinlikle biliyoruz. Bu gerГ§ek gГјrГјltГј ile aДҹДұn tahmini arasДұndaki farkДұ minimize etmek aГ§Дұk ve stabil bir eДҹitim hedefi saДҹlДұyor.

sections.heading: S6. Diffusion model eДҹitimi nasДұl yapДұlДұr? Loss fonksiyonu nedir? DDPM pseudo-code nedir?
sections.text:
Loss fonksiyonu oldukГ§a basit ve temizdir:
  L = E [ ||Оө - Оө_Оё(x_t, t)||ВІ ]

Yani gerГ§ek eklenen gГјrГјltГј Оө ile aДҹДұn tahmini Оө_Оё arasДұndaki MSE'yi minimize etmek. GAN'daki gibi hiГ§bir rakip aДҹ yok, VAE'deki gibi KL terimi dengelemeye gerek yok вҖ” sadece dГјz bir regression problemi.

Sinir aДҹДұ mimarisi olarak genellikle U-Net kullanДұlДұr Г§ГјnkГј hem global hem lokal Г¶zellikleri iyi yakalar. Zaman adДұmДұ t, sinusoidal pozisyon embedding ile aДҹa verilir вҖ” her adДұmdaki gГјrГјltГј miktarДұ farklДұ olduДҹu iГ§in aДҹДұn t'yi bilmesi kritik.

EДҹitim pseudo-code:
  1. GerГ§ek veri x_0'Дұ al, rastgele t seГ§
  2. Rastgele Оө ~ N(0, I) Г¶rnekle
  3. x_t = вҲҡбҫұ_t В· x_0 + вҲҡ(1-бҫұ_t) В· Оө (tek adДұmda)
  4. Loss = ||Оө - Оө_Оё(x_t, t)||ВІ
  5. Gradient descent adДұmДұ at, baЕҹa dГ¶n

Sampling (yeni gГ¶rГјntГј Гјretme) pseudo-code:
  1. x_T ~ N(0, I) вҖ” saf gГјrГјltГјden baЕҹla
  2. t = T, T-1, ..., 1 adДұmlarДұnda geri git:
       GГјrГјltГј tahmin et: Оө_Оё(x_t, t)
       Ој_Оё'Дұ hesapla (formГјlden)
       x_{t-1} = Ој_Оё + вҲҡОІ_t В· z  (z ~ N(0,I), t=1'de z=0)
  3. x_0'Дұ dГ¶ndГјr вҖ” temiz gГ¶rГјntГј

sections.heading: S7. DDPM nasДұl koЕҹullandДұrДұlДұr? Classifier guidance vs classifier-free guidance nedir?
sections.text:
Vanilla DDPM sДұnДұf veya iГ§erik bilgisi olmadan Гјretir вҖ” ne Г§ДұkacaДҹДұ tamamen rastgele. KontrollГј Гјretim iГ§in modeli bir koЕҹula (class etiketi, metin, gГ¶rГјntГј) gГ¶re yГ¶nlendirmek gerekir.

Zaman koЕҹullandДұrmasДұ aДҹДұn zaten iГ§inde var: aДҹ sinusoidal embedding ile t'yi alДұyor, farklДұ adДұmlarda ne kadar gГјrГјltГј bekleneceДҹini biliyor. Bu olmadan model Г§alДұЕҹamaz.

SДұnДұf koЕҹullandДұrmasДұ ise t'ye ek olarak class label c'yi de aДҹa vermekle yapДұlДұr. Оө_Оё(x_t, t, c) artДұk hem adДұma hem de sДұnДұfa gГ¶re gГјrГјltГјyГј tahmin eder.

Classifier Guidance, ayrДұ bir sДұnДұflandДұrДұcДұ aДҹДұ eДҹitmeyi gerektirir. Bu sДұnДұflandДұrДұcДұ gГјrГјltГјlГј gГ¶rГјntГјler Гјzerinde Г§alДұЕҹДұr ve Гјretim sДұrasДұnda log p(c|x_t)'nin gradyanДұ diffusion skoruna eklenerek Гјretim istenen sДұnДұfa doДҹru Г§ekilir. Guidance scale s ne kadar bГјyГјk olursa Гјretim o kadar sДұkДұ koЕҹula baДҹlanДұr ama Г§eЕҹitlilik azalДұr. Ekstra bir aДҹ eДҹitmek gerektiДҹi iГ§in maliyetli.

Classifier-Free Guidance ise Г§ok daha elegant: tek bir model hem koЕҹullu hem koЕҹulsuz Гјretim yapabilecek Еҹekilde eДҹitilir. EДҹitim sДұrasДұnda c rastgele boЕҹ token ile deДҹiЕҹtirilir (dropout). Гңretimde her adДұmda iki tahmin yapДұlДұr вҖ” koЕҹullu ve koЕҹulsuz вҖ” ve farklarДұ alДұnДұr: sonuГ§ = Оө(вҲ…) + s В· (Оө(c) - Оө(вҲ…)). Bu DALL-E 2 ve Imagen'in kullandДұДҹДұ yГ¶ntemdir, ekstra aДҹ gerektirmediДҹi iГ§in hem daha basit hem de pratikte daha iyi Г§alДұЕҹДұyor.

sections.heading: S8. CLIP / DALL-E 2 / Imagen genel Г§alДұЕҹma prensibi nedir? Imagen Video nasДұl Г§alДұЕҹДұr?
sections.text:
CLIP (Contrastive Language-Image Pretraining), metni ve gГ¶rГјntГјyГј aynДұ embedding uzayДұna getirmeyi Г¶Дҹrenir. Milyarlarca (gГ¶rГјntГј, metin aГ§ДұklamasДұ) Г§iftini contrastive loss ile eДҹitir: eЕҹleЕҹen Г§iftler birbirine yakДұn, eЕҹleЕҹmeyenler uzak olmalДұ. SonuГ§ olarak metinden gГ¶rГјntГјnГјn semantiДҹini anlayan evrensel bir encoder elde edersin. "Bir Г§ayДұrda koЕҹan at" yazДұsДұnДұn embedding'i ile o sahneyi gГ¶steren gГ¶rГјntГјnГјn embedding'i aynДұ uzayda yakДұn noktalar olur.

DALL-E 2 CLIP Гјzerine inЕҹa edilmiЕҹtir. Г–nce metin CLIP text encoder'dan geГ§er ve text embedding elde edilir. ArdДұndan bir "prior" aДҹДұ bu text embedding'i image embedding'e dГ¶nГјЕҹtГјrГјr. Son adДұmda bir diffusion model bu image embedding'den gerГ§ek gГ¶rГјntГј Гјretir. Yani sistem metin вҶ’ CLIP semantik uzayДұ вҶ’ gГ¶rГјntГј pipeline'ДұnДұ takip eder.

Imagen ise farklДұ bir yol izler ve CLIP kullanmaz; bunun yerine Г§ok bГјyГјk frozen bir dil modeli (T5-XXL) ile metin embedding Гјretir. Bu dil modeli metni derin bir Еҹekilde anlДұyor ve bu temsilden yararlanarak Гјretim yapДұlДұyor. GГ¶rГјntГј Гјretimi bir cascade ile yapДұlДұr: Г¶nce 64Г—64 Г§Г¶zГјnГјrlГјklГј gГ¶rГјntГј Гјretilir, ardДұndan iki ayrДұ super-resolution diffusion modeli 256Г—256'ya ve 1024Г—1024'e Г§ДұkarДұr. Classifier-free guidance her aЕҹamada uygulanДұr.

Imagen Video aynДұ cascade mimarisini zaman boyutuna da geniЕҹletir. Spatial attention'Дұn yanДұna temporal attention bloklarДұ eklenir; bu sayede ardДұЕҹДұk frame'ler birbiriyle tutarlДұ Гјretilir. Г–nce kДұsa, dГјЕҹГјk Г§Г¶zГјnГјrlГјklГј videolar Гјretilir, ardДұndan hem zamansal hem uzamsal super-resolution modellerle HD videoya ulaЕҹДұlДұr.

ida11.title: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models: Overview
ida11.summary: DDPMs (Ho et al. 2020) define a Markov chain that gradually adds Gaussian noise to data (forward process) and learn to reverse it (backward process). Unlike GANs, training is stable; unlike VAEs, the latent space has the same dimensionality as the data. They currently produce the best image quality.
ida11.context: Lecture 11 В· 11.01.2024 вҖ” Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models

left.title: GANs / VAEs
points: GAN: unstable adversarial training, mode collapse
VAE: blurry outputs, surrogate ELBO loss

right.title: Diffusion Models (DDPM)
points: Stable training: simple noise prediction loss
Latent space same dim as data
Slow: T=1000 denoising steps for generation

sections.heading: Key Idea: Destroy then Reconstruct
sections.text: Diffusion models are inspired by non-equilibrium thermodynamics. The forward process gradually destroys the data structure by adding noise, until the image becomes pure Gaussian noise. The model learns the reverse: given a slightly noisier image, predict the slightly less noisy version. At generation time, start from pure Gaussian noise and apply the learned reverse process T times to produce a sample from the data distribution. The training signal comes from comparing the predicted noise at each step to the actual noise that was added вҖ” a simple regression loss. This stability (no discriminator, no mode collapse) is the key advantage over GANs.

ida11.title: Forward Diffusion: Markov Chain and Reparameterisation
ida11.summary: The forward process is a fixed Markov chain that adds Gaussian noise with variance ОІ_t at each step. The reparameterisation trick collapses this to a closed-form expression, allowing direct sampling of x_t at any timestep from x_0 without running T sequential steps.
ida11.context: Lecture 11 В· 11.01.2024 вҖ” Forward diffusion process

sections.heading: Forward Process: Step-by-Step and Closed Form
sections.text:
Step-wise forward process:
$$q(\mathbf{x}_t | \mathbf{x}_{t-1}) = \mathcal{N}(\mathbf{x}_t;\; \sqrt{1-\beta_t}\,\mathbf{x}_{t-1},\; \beta_t \mathbf{I})$$

Full trajectory (Markov):
$$q(\mathbf{x}_{1:T}|\mathbf{x}_0) = \prod_{t=1}^T q(\mathbf{x}_t|\mathbf{x}_{t-1})$$

Let $\alpha_t = 1-\beta_t$, $\bar{\alpha}_t = \prod_{s=0}^t \alpha_s$. **Reparameterisation** (closed form, sample any $t$ directly):
$$\mathbf{x}_t \sim q(\mathbf{x}_t|\mathbf{x}_0) = \mathcal{N}(\mathbf{x}_t;\; \sqrt{\bar{\alpha}_t}\,\mathbf{x}_0,\; (1-\bar{\alpha}_t)\mathbf{I})$$
$$\mathbf{x}_t = \sqrt{\bar{\alpha}_t}\,\mathbf{x}_0 + \sqrt{1-\bar{\alpha}_t}\,\boldsymbol{\epsilon},\quad \boldsymbol{\epsilon}\sim\mathcal{N}(0,I)$$

sections.heading: Variance Schedule: Linear vs Cosine
sections.text: The variance schedule $\{\beta_t\}_{t=1}^T$ controls how fast information is destroyed. Ho et al. (2020) used a linear schedule: $\beta_1 = 10^{-4}$ increasing to $\beta_T = 0.02$ over $T=1000$ steps. The linear schedule destroys information quickly вҖ” $\bar{\alpha}_t$ falls rapidly in the early steps, wasting many denoising steps on already-noisy data. Nichol et al. (2021) proposed a cosine schedule: $\bar{\alpha}_t = \cos^2(\frac{\pi}{2}\frac{t/T+s}{1+s})$, which decreases more slowly and smoothly, keeping structure longer at moderate noise levels. The cosine schedule significantly improves sample quality.

ida11.title: Reverse Diffusion and Training
ida11.summary: The reverse process is learned by a neural network (U-Net with time conditioning) that predicts the noise added at each step. Training minimises a simple MSE loss between predicted and actual noise. The U-Net architecture with temporal embedding enables high-quality denoising.
ida11.context: Lecture 11 В· 11.01.2024 вҖ” Learning the reverse diffusion process

sections.heading: Reverse Process and Training Objective
sections.text:
Reverse process (parameterised by neural net $\boldsymbol{\epsilon}_\theta$):
$$p_\theta(\mathbf{x}_{t-1}|\mathbf{x}_t) = \mathcal{N}(\mathbf{x}_{t-1};\; \boldsymbol{\mu}_\theta(\mathbf{x}_t, t),\; \sigma_t^2 \mathbf{I})$$

Simplified training objective (Ho et al.):
$$\mathcal{L}_{\mathrm{simple}} = \mathbb{E}_{t, \mathbf{x}_0, \boldsymbol{\epsilon}}\bigl[\| \boldsymbol{\epsilon} - \boldsymbol{\epsilon}_\theta(\mathbf{x}_t, t) \|^2\bigr]$$

The network $\boldsymbol{\epsilon}_\theta$ predicts the noise $\boldsymbol{\epsilon}$ that was added to $\mathbf{x}_0$ to produce $\mathbf{x}_t$.

Sampling (DDPM): $\mathbf{x}_{t-1} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{\alpha_t}}\bigl(\mathbf{x}_t - \frac{1-\alpha_t}{\sqrt{1-\bar{\alpha}_t}}\boldsymbol{\epsilon}_\theta(\mathbf{x}_t,t)\bigr) + \sigma_t\mathbf{z}$

sections.heading: U-Net Architecture for Noise Prediction
sections.text: The noise prediction network $\boldsymbol{\epsilon}_\theta$ is a U-Net with residual connections and attention at multiple resolutions. The timestep $t$ is encoded via sinusoidal positional encoding (analogous to Transformer positional encoding) and added to the feature maps at each resolution via scale-shift normalisation (AdaGN). This allows the same network to handle all timesteps вҖ” at small $t$ (slightly noisy), the network learns fine detail denoising; at large $t$ (heavily noisy), it learns coarse structure recovery. DDPM inference requires $T=1000$ sequential steps, which is slow. DDIM (Denoising Diffusion Implicit Models) achieves comparable quality in 50вҖ“200 steps by using a deterministic sampling process.

ida11.title: Conditional Diffusion and Text-to-Image Generation
ida11.summary: Classifier-free guidance conditions the diffusion model on a signal (text, class, image) without needing a separate classifier. Combined with latent diffusion (compressing to a lower-dimensional space first), this enables practical text-to-image models like Stable Diffusion and DALL-E 2.
ida11.context: Lecture 11 В· 11.01.2024 вҖ” Conditional generation and modern applications

sections.heading: Classifier-Free Guidance
sections.text:
Train a single conditional model $\boldsymbol{\epsilon}_\theta(\mathbf{x}_t, t, c)$ and occasionally drop the condition $c$ (replace with null token $\emptyset$) during training at probability $p_{\mathrm{uncond}}$ (typically 0.1вҖ“0.2).

At sampling time, interpolate between unconditional and conditional predictions:
$$\hat{\boldsymbol{\epsilon}}_\theta(\mathbf{x}_t, c) = (1+w)\boldsymbol{\epsilon}_\theta(\mathbf{x}_t, c) - w\boldsymbol{\epsilon}_\theta(\mathbf{x}_t, \emptyset)$$

$w > 0$: guidance strength. Large $w$ вҶ’ images more strongly conditioned on $c$ (sharper, more faithful to prompt) but less diverse. $w=0$: unconditional. Typical: $w=7.5$.

sections.heading: Stable Diffusion: Latent Diffusion Models
sections.text: Running diffusion in pixel space on a 512Г—512 image is expensive вҖ” $T=1000$ U-Net passes on a large image. Stable Diffusion (Rombach et al. 2022) uses Latent Diffusion Models (LDM): a VQ-VAE first encodes the image to a compact latent representation (8Г— spatially smaller: 512Г—512 вҶ’ 64Г—64), and the diffusion process runs in this latent space. The U-Net runs on 64Г—64 latents, dramatically reducing compute. Text conditioning uses CLIP's text encoder вҖ” the text embedding is injected into the U-Net via cross-attention at each resolution. At generation time: diffuse in latent space, then decode with the VAE decoder.

ida12.title: Lecture 12 вҖ” Q & A
ida12.summary: IDA Lecture 12'nin tГјm sДұnav sorularДұ ve sade aГ§Дұklamalarla cevaplarДұ. PointNet++ kДұsДұtlarДұ, FCPN, EdgeConv/DGCNN, FoldingNet, DDPM ile implant Гјretimi.
ida12.context: Lecture 12 В· 18.01.2024 вҖ” Advanced Methods for Point Cloud Processing

sections.heading: S1. PointNet++'Дұn potansiyel kДұsДұtlamalarДұ neler? FCPN uzamsal fazlalДұДҹДұ nasДұl aЕҹДұyor?
sections.text:
PointNet++ bГјyГјk point cloud'larДұ iЕҹlerken birbirine Г¶rtГјЕҹen (overlapping) bloklara bГ¶ler ve her bloДҹu ayrДұ ayrДұ iЕҹler. Sonra bu bloklarДұn sonuГ§larДұnДұ birbirine yapДұЕҹtДұrДұr. Burada iki problem var: birincisi, Г¶rtГјЕҹen bГ¶lgeler birden fazla kez hesaplanДұr, yani gereksiz yere aynДұ hesabДұ tekrarlarsДұn вҖ” bu uzamsal fazlalДұktДұr. Д°kincisi, blok sДұnДұrlarДұnda bilgi tutarsДұzlДұklarДұ ve artifactlar oluЕҹabilir.

FCPN (Fully-Convolutional Point Network) bu problemi kГ¶kten farklДұ bir yaklaЕҹДұmla Г§Г¶zer. Fikir Еҹu: Г¶nce dГјzensiz point cloud'u dГјzenli bir 3B voksel ДұzgarasДұna dГ¶nГјЕҹtГјr, sonra bu dГјzenli yapДұ Гјzerinde standart 3B konvolГјsyon uygula. BГ¶ylece tГјm bГјyГјk point cloud'u tek seferlik ve Г¶rtГјЕҹme olmadan iЕҹleyebilirsin. Her bГ¶lge tam olarak bir kez hesaplanДұr, sДұnДұr problemi yoktur ve GPU'nun toplu konvolГјsyon optimizasyonlarДұndan tam faydalanabilirsin.
